BRAYDEN (30s) paces the razor-thin concrete edge. He wears neon board shorts, a heavy gold chain, and the aggressive sunburn of a man who thinks sunscreen is a government psy-op. 

He barks into his AirPods. 

BRAYDEN
Liquidate the apes! I don't care about the gas fees, dump it! If he cries about his kid's tuition again, tell him to mint a new kid!

Under the shade of a minimalist cabana sits VALERIE VANCE. Immaculate white pencil skirt. Oversized Celine sunglasses. She sips a San Pellegrino. 

Her posture is rigid. Transactional. She tracks Brayden like a hawk calculating a risk premium.

Brayden kicks a stray towel into the water. 

BRAYDEN
(into phone)
Hold on, I gotta deal with the help. 

He points a wet finger at Valerie. 

BRAYDEN
Hey. Vance. The water's freezing. You said this place was turnkey. I'm not dropping forty mil on a house with a broken heater. 

Valerie doesn't blink behind the dark lenses. 

VALERIE
The climate control is fully autonomous, Brayden. The property intuitively adjusts to the... specific biological needs of its occupants. A seamless value add.

Brayden scoffs. He steps closer to the edge, peering down into the crystal-clear depths. 

BRAYDEN
Whatever. Just fix it before escrow closes. 
(taps AirPod)
Yeah, I'm back. No, the agent's clueless. I'm gonna lowball the shit out of her.

He turns his back to the pool. 

Behind him, the gentle ripples of the water STOP. 

The surface goes completely flat. Unnaturally still. The color shifts from a vibrant aquamarine to a pale, cloudy white. 

It thickens. 

Brayden steps backward, his heel dipping into the pool. 

Except, it doesn't splash. His heel sinks into a viscous, gelatinous membrane. Like stepping into a vat of clear glue. 

BRAYDEN
(into phone)
Tell the board they can suck my--

The water SURGES. 

A thick tendril of gelatinous liquid snaps out of the pool, wrapping around Brayden's ankle like a python. 

BRAYDEN
What the--

VIOLENT YANK. 

Brayden goes down hard. His chin smacks the wet concrete with a sickening CRACK. His phone skitters across the deck. 

He claws desperately at the grout. 

BRAYDEN
Help! Hey! 

Valerie slowly lowers her Pellegrino. She checks her Cartier watch. 

The water drags Brayden backward. He slides across the concrete, fingernails bleeding, leaving a trail of red that the water instantly laps up. 

With a wet SCHLUCK, he is pulled beneath the surface. 

UNDERWATER

Brayden thrashes. The water is no longer a liquid. It's a sentient, shrinking vacuum seal. 

He punches at the gelatinous mass. His fists barely move. He opens his mouth to scream, releasing a massive air bubble. 

The bubble doesn't rise. It freezes in the gel. 

Through the glass bottom of the pool, Brayden sees the deadly drop to the canyon below. He twists in a panic, looking up at the surface. 

ABOVE WATER

Valerie stands at the edge of the pool. She peers down at him. Hands clasped neatly behind her back. 

UNDERWATER

Brayden locks eyes with her. His face turns purple. He presses his hands against the tightening gel, pleading. 

ABOVE WATER

Valerie tilts her head. She watches the violent churning with the mild annoyance of a homeowner inspecting a leaky faucet. 

VALERIE
(murmuring)
Market correction. 

The thrashing beneath the surface slows. 

Brayden's body jerks once. Twice. 

Then, the water collapses inward with a muffled, concussive THUMP. 

Instantly, the pool returns to normal. Crystal clear. Gentle, inviting ripples. Brayden is entirely gone. 

Silence, save for the hum of the pool pump. 

A tiny bubble rises from the deep end. 

Bloop. 

A massive, flawless, fist-sized DIAMOND breaches the surface. It bobs gently in the aquamarine water, catching the harsh Los Angeles sun. 

Valerie exhales. She smooths a microscopic wrinkle from her skirt. 

She walks over to a sleek maintenance box and retrieves a long, aluminum pool skimmer. 

Returning to the edge, she extends the pole over the water. With practiced, mechanical precision, she scoops the diamond into the blue mesh net. 

She drags it back to the edge. 

Plucking the wet diamond from the net, she holds it up to the light. It refracts brilliantly against her sunglasses. Pristine. Blood-free. 

Valerie pulls out her phone. She swipes to her contacts. 

VALERIE
Toby? Yes. Cancel the afternoon showings. We've successfully leveraged the property's unique assets. The buyer is... fully invested. 

She drops the diamond into her designer purse. It clinks heavily against several others. 

VALERIE
(into phone)
And pivot our maintenance budget. Send a cleaning crew to the pool deck immediately. We have a minor scuff mark on the concrete that's negatively impacting the curb appeal. 

She turns her back on the pool. Her heels click sharply against the stone as she walks away, leaving the pristine water rippling behind her.

scenes.narrative_action: A predatory Hedge Fund Manager aggressively taps his cane against the hardwood floor, demanding structural blueprints. Valerie takes two deliberate steps backward into the hallway. Above him, the massive vaulted ceiling detaches with a sickening crack. It rapidly descends, crushing the manager flat against the floorboards with immense force. Not a single drop of blood spills. The ceiling resets. Valerie steps forward to collect her freshly minted diamond.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Concludes the kill montage, establishing the bloodless, efficient routine Valerie has developed with the entity.
scenes.draft_text:
26

INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - DAY

Sunlight pours through twenty-foot, floor-to-ceiling windows, illuminating an architectural masterpiece of glass, steel, and immaculate mahogany. 

It is dead, pristine quiet. 

THWACK. 

A heavy, silver-tipped cane strikes the hardwood. 

ARTHUR PENDLETON (60s, bespoke suit, the posture of a man who liquidates pensions for sport) paces the center of the room. He scowls at the massive vaulted ceiling above him.

THWACK. He strikes the floor again. Testing the density. 

ARTHUR
It’s a mausoleum. The acoustics are entirely too aggressive. If I’m hosting the board, I don’t want my conversations echoing into the kitchen. 

VALERIE VANCE stands perfectly still at the edge of the room. She remains exactly one inch outside the living room threshold, her Prada heels planted on the hallway tile. 

She holds an iPad to her chest like a shield. Her smile is a weaponized, high-gloss veneer. 

VALERIE
The current footprint offers tremendous value add, Arthur. It’s a turnkey asset. The acoustics actually maximize the open-concept flow. 

Arthur sneers. He drags the rubber tip of his cane across the flawless mahogany grain. 

ARTHUR
Flow is a buzzword for people who can’t afford walls. I want to gut it. Strip this entire section down to the studs and drop the ceiling ten feet. 

Valerie’s eyes track the cane. The house is listening. 

VALERIE
We can certainly pivot to a remodel discussion, but the structural integrity--

ARTHUR
(interrupting)
I don’t care about the integrity. I care about the equity. 

THWACK. 

Arthur drives the cane into the floor with immense force. 

ARTHUR
I told your assistant I needed the structural blueprints yesterday. You expect me to wire eight figures blind? 

A low, subsonic hum vibrates through the room. The mahogany floorboards seem to tighten. The ambient temperature drops ten degrees in a second. 

Valerie feels the shift. The house is hungry. 

VALERIE
I understand the urgency regarding the schematics. Let me just... step out and call the zoning office. 

Arthur doesn't hear her. He is glaring up at the vaulted ceiling, pointing his cane at the recessed lighting. 

ARTHUR
All of this overhead space. It’s wasted capital. It’s inefficient. 

THWACK. THWACK. 

ARTHUR
Get me the damn blueprints, Valerie. 

Valerie’s smile vanishes. Her face goes completely blank. 

She takes one deliberate step backward into the hallway. 

Then a second. 

Her heels click against the tile. Safe. 

VALERIE
Right away. 

Above Arthur, the architecture groans. 

A SICKENING CRACK echoes through the room. 

Arthur looks up. His eyes widen. 

The entire vaulted ceiling detaches from the walls. 

It does not fall. It DRIVES downward. A hydraulic slam of drywall, steel beams, and pure, impossible gravity. 

It hits Arthur with the force of a freight train. 

BOOM. 

The impact shakes the foundation. Dust violently blasts out from the edges of the room, washing over Valerie’s shoes. 

She doesn't flinch. She doesn't blink. 

The ceiling rests perfectly flush against the floorboards. Arthur is completely gone. Crushed utterly flat beneath tons of impossible weight. 

Silence returns to the room. 

Not a single drop of blood seeps out from the edges. No viscera. No fluid. 

A beat. 

The ceiling seamlessly ascends. It glides upward, silent and frictionless, sliding back into its original position twenty feet above. 

The recessed lights flicker back on. 

The mahogany floor is spotless. No scuffs. No Arthur. No cane. 

Resting in the exact center of the room, catching the afternoon sunlight, is a flawless, egg-sized diamond. 

Valerie exhales a slow, measured breath. The tension leaves her shoulders. 

She steps over the threshold, walking gracefully across the hardwood. She stops, crouches, and picks up the diamond. 

It is warm to the touch. Gleaming. Pristine. 

Valerie drops it into her designer blazer pocket. She taps her iPad, swiping Arthur Pendleton’s name off her schedule.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling pours vintage champagne into two crystal flutes. He hands one to Valerie, gesturing grandly to her new, massive corner desk visible through the glass walls, overflowing with congratulatory floral arrangements. Valerie accepts the glass, soaking in the opulent praise. She sips the champagne, completely ignoring Toby, who watches her nervously from the bullpen outside.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The false victory. Valerie reaches the top of the corporate ladder, completely blind to the moral and physical danger she is in.
scenes.draft_text:
Scene 27

INT. RICHARD STERLING'S OFFICE - DAY

The sharp, concussive POP of a cork. A wisp of white vapor curls into the climate-controlled air. 

RICHARD STERLING (60s) holds a bottle of vintage Dom Perignon. His bespoke suit costs more than a sedan. He pours the pale gold liquid into two heavy Baccarat crystal flutes. The bubbles hiss.

VALERIE VANCE stands at the floor-to-ceiling glass wall. Her posture is rigidly straight, her tailored blazer acting as armor. She watches the floor below.

RICHARD
Look at them out there. The herd. 

Richard steps up beside her. He offers a flute. Valerie takes it without breaking her gaze from the glass.

Through the soundproof barrier: The bustling BULLPEN. 

At the far end, a massive new mahogany corner desk dominates the floor. It is entirely swallowed by extravagant, sprawling floral arrangements. White orchids. Blood-red lilies. 

RICHARD
They look like funeral wreaths, don't they? The flowers. 

VALERIE
I prefer to view them as a yield on investment. The market recognizes value add.

RICHARD
Indeed it does. 

Richard takes a slow sip. His eyes remain fixed on her-- genial, warm, and entirely dead. 

RICHARD
I must admit, Valerie. I had my doubts. Last quarter, your portfolio was... anaemic. I thought perhaps you lacked the stomach for this echelon.

VALERIE
A temporary liquidity friction. I just needed to leverage the right asset. 

RICHARD
Aggressive repositioning. I like it. 

Richard turns to face the bullpen, standing shoulder-to-shoulder with her. 

RICHARD
You secured Councilwoman Rostova. You moved the Hawthorne estate. Clients who step into that house... they simply vanish from the open market. They buy, and they are never heard from again.

Valerie's fingers tighten around the stem of her crystal flute. The glass squeaks, barely audible. 

VALERIE
We offer an all-consuming buyer experience. Total immersion.

RICHARD
It is the natural order. Capitalism isn't a spreadsheet, Valerie. It's an ecosystem. 

Richard points a perfectly manicured finger at the junior agents scrambling outside the glass.

RICHARD
Ninety-nine percent of the people in this city are plankton. They graze on scraps. They exist to be devoured by interest rates and inflation. The tragedy is that so few agents realize they are meant to be the whale.

Valerie takes this in. The opulent praise washes over her. She stands a little taller. 

VALERIE
I'm just optimizing my position. The property has a unique... appetite. I'm facilitating the transaction.

RICHARD
And reaping the rewards. 

Richard gestures grandly toward her new desk. 

RICHARD
The corner office is next. Provided this synergy continues.

Outside the glass, TOBY ARIS (20s) steps into view. His suit is rumpled. He holds a stack of red-flagged folders tightly against his chest. 

Toby stops near the coffee machine. He stares directly up at Richard's office. At Valerie. 

Toby's face is pale. He nervously pushes his glasses up the bridge of his nose. He clears his throat, shifting his weight from foot to foot, practically vibrating with moral panic. 

RICHARD
Your assistant looks like he's going to be sick.

Valerie doesn't blink. She watches Toby squirm. 

VALERIE
Toby struggles with high-yield environments. He lacks the bandwidth for our current growth metrics. 

Valerie deliberately turns her back on the glass. She shuts Toby out completely, facing Richard. 

VALERIE
I'll recalibrate his workflow. 

Richard smiles. A predator recognizing its own. He raises his glass.

RICHARD
To the top of the food chain.

VALERIE
To the top.

They clink glasses. The crystal rings out like a bell. 

Valerie takes a long, slow sip of the champagne. Her eyes are bright, triumphant, and utterly blind to the shadow she is standing in.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, dressed in casual but expensive loungewear, hums to herself as she wipes down a floating glass banister with a microfiber cloth. She pauses to sip champagne from a crystal flute resting on the stairs. She treats the deadly space like a tamed pet. As she wipes away a single speck of dust, a deep, unnatural groan vibrates through the soles of her shoes, rattling the champagne flute until it shatters on the steps.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The turn of the sequence. Shatters Valerie's illusion of control and signals the monster's escalating hunger.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - DAY

Sunlight slices through floor-to-ceiling windows, illuminating fifty-thousand square feet of sterile, modernist perfection. Concrete. Steel. Floating glass.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) glides up the floating staircase. She wears a silk La Perla kimono--casual, but strictly high-net-worth. 

She hums a tuneless, upbeat melody. A plush microfiber cloth dances in her hand. She wipes a microscopic smudge from the glass banister.

She strokes the glass. Affectionate. Like petting a purebred Doberman that just won Best in Show. 

A crystal flute of Dom Pérignon rests on the edge of a concrete tread. Valerie pauses, picks it up, and takes a slow, luxurious sip.

Her phone BUZZES on the step below. The screen reads: TOBY.

She taps the speaker icon with a perfectly manicured nail.

VALERIE
Tell me you closed the escrow on the Rostova viewing.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
I sent the NDA. But Valerie, her team is asking for a structural inspection. They heard rumors about the... foundation.

VALERIE
There are no rumors, Toby. There is only a lack of market education. Pivot the narrative.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
Right. Pivot. It's just... I can't find the original blueprints. The city registry has a blank file for this parcel.

VALERIE
(sighs)
Because it's an exclusive off-market build. That's the value add. Rarity. If Councilwoman Rostova wants to play in the big leagues, she doesn't get to kick the tires.

Valerie sets the champagne flute back down on the concrete step. She resumes wiping the glass, her movements rhythmic, soothing.

VALERIE
Tell her it's an as-is acquisition. If she balks, we call Julian and let him offer it to his tech bros. That'll light a fire under her pension fund.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
(clears throat)
Are you sure we should invite more people inside? After what happened to--

VALERIE
(sharp)
Toby. We are maximizing the asset's yield. The house provides, we facilitate. It's a symbiotic ecosystem.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
It doesn't feel symbiotic. It feels hungry.

VALERIE
Hunger is just ambition without capital. I'll see you at the office.

She taps the phone dead. The silence of the massive room returns.

Valerie smiles. She owns this silence. She owns this space.

She leans in close to the glass banister. A single, stubborn speck of dust clings to the underside of the railing.

She exhales a short breath onto the glass. Fogs it up. 

Wipes it clean. Squeaky, pristine perfection.

A low, subsonic hum begins.

Valerie freezes. The microfiber cloth hovers in the air.

The hum deepens into a guttural GROAN. It doesn't echo from the walls. It emanates from the marrow of the house itself.

The vibration travels up through the polished concrete floor. It climbs through the soles of Valerie's cashmere slippers.

It feels like a stomach growling. A massive, starving stomach.

Valerie's smile vanishes. Her eyes dart to the floorboards.

On the concrete step, the crystal flute of champagne begins to vibrate. 

The golden liquid trembles. Tiny ripples form concentric circles.

The groan intensifies. A grinding of unseen gears. Or teeth.

The flute rattles against the hard concrete. 

Tink-tink-tink-tink.

Valerie reaches out, her hand trembling slightly, trying to save the glass. Trying to hush the beast.

VALERIE
(whispering)
Shh. We're closed.

The house ROARS--a sudden, violent shudder that violently bucks the staircase.

The champagne flute tips.

It hits the step below. SHATTERS.

Glass shards and vintage champagne explode across Valerie's silk kimono.

She flinches back, pressing herself flat against the wall. Her breath comes fast and shallow. The feral panic of a cornered animal breaks through her polished mask.

The vibration stops. The groan fades. 

Total silence returns.

Valerie stares down at the broken glass, chest heaving. 

The puddle of spilled champagne slowly seeps into a microscopic crack in the concrete. 

Drinking it in.

scenes.narrative_action: The pristine white walls suddenly splinter and crack like dry skin. Thick, black blood seeps from the fissures, dripping down and ruining the imported Persian rugs. Valerie drops her cleaning cloth, her arrogant composure shattering into primal panic. She scrambles backward as every heavy oak door and reinforced glass window in the mansion violently slams shut and locks simultaneously. The cavernous room is plunged into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the stakes by trapping the protagonist, physically demonstrating that the house is the master, not the pet.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie scrubs a microscopic smudge off the pristine quartz coffee table. She wields a microfiber cloth like a scalpel. 

Her Bluetooth earpiece blinks with an incoming call. She taps it.

VALERIE
(into earpiece)
I told you to pivot the messaging, Toby. It's not a 'missing persons' issue, it's an 'exclusive off-market' transition. 

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered)
I know, Val, I'm trying, but Julian is asking questions about the comps and he saw Councilwoman Rostova's town car in the driveway yesterday, and now her office is saying she's completely unreachable, which means--

VALERIE
Elena is taking a wellness retreat. We discussed this. The narrative is exclusivity. Control the narrative, Toby.

Valerie spots a glimmer under the edge of the sofa. She kneels, reaching into the plush carpet. 

She pulls out a flawless, two-carat diamond. 

She holds it up to the light. No blood on it. Perfect. 

VALERIE
(CONT'D)
Besides, the ROI on this property is compounding hourly. Tell Richard the escrow is bulletproof. I have to go. 

She taps her earpiece off. Drops the diamond into her tailored blazer pocket.

A sharp, brittle CRACK echoes through the cavernous room. 

Valerie freezes. 

She turns slowly toward the grand fireplace. 

The flawless, white Venetian plaster wall is splintering. 

A jagged fissure creeps up the drywall. It moves like a living thing. Peeling back. Flaking like dry, dead skin.

Valerie’s jaw tightens. Not fear. Financial calculation.

VALERIE
(muttering)
Structural settling. Unbelievable. Do you know what contractors charge in this zip code?

She marches over to the wall. Reaches out to trace the crack. 

Before her acrylic nail touches the plaster, the wall HEAVES. 

A wet, tearing sound. 

Thick, viscous BLACK BLOOD pulses from the fissure. 

It spills over the baseboards. It drips down in heavy, syrupy globs, puddling directly onto the antique Persian rug.

VALERIE
(gasping)
No, no, no--the silk warp!

She drops to her knees, scrubbing frantically at the rug with her microfiber cloth. 

The blood is hot. It smells like copper and ozone. 

It stains her hands. It ruins the cuffs of her blazer. 

Above her, the ceiling GROANS. 

Valerie looks up. 

The ceiling is spider-webbing. Dozens of cracks. Hundreds. 

Black blood begins to rain from the plaster. 

The soiled microfiber cloth slips from her trembling fingers. 

The polished, transactional predator vanishes. Her veneer shatters. Her eyes go wide, feral, darting around the massive room. 

The realization hits her. She is not the owner here. 

She is the prey.

VALERIE
(voice cracking)
Okay. Okay, we're done here. 

She scrambles backward on her hands and knees, slipping in the slick black ooze. 

She kicks off her designer heels to gain traction. Barefoot, she sprints for the grand entryway.

SLAM.

The massive, twelve-foot solid oak front doors violently swing shut. 

Valerie crashes hard into the wood. She claws at the heavy brass handles, pulling with all her body weight.

CLACK. 

The deadbolt slides into place on its own. 

VALERIE
Hey! Open! 

She spins around, chest heaving. 

Across the room, the reinforced glass French doors slam shut. 

CLACK.

The floor-to-ceiling bay windows. 

SLAM. CLACK.

The kitchen entry doors. 

SLAM. CLACK.

A deafening, percussive symphony of entrapment. Every exit in the house seals itself with mechanical precision.

Valerie backs into the center of the room. Trembling. 

The black blood continues to pool around her bare feet. 

VALERIE
(pleading, breathless)
I brought you Elena. I brought you the best... I'm an asset! I am a value add!

The house answers with a deep, guttural rumble that vibrates through the floorboards, traveling straight up Valerie's spine.

Above her, the massive crystal chandelier flickers. 

Once. Twice. 

The bulbs shatter simultaneously.

The room is plunged into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pounds her bleeding fists against the unbreakable glass windows. The floorboards beneath her shift and tilt, physically sliding her toward the center of the room. The wood peels back, revealing the gaping, fleshy maw. Valerie digs her fingernails into the floorboards, desperately fighting the pull. Hovering inches above the jagged teeth, she screams out a frantic bargain, promising a massive, invite-only gala. The floorboards instantly snap shut, dropping her onto the solid wood, leaving her gasping for air.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Midpoint escalation. Valerie barely survives by making a desperate, massive promise, setting the ticking clock for the gala.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie slams her fists against the floor-to-ceiling windows. 

THUD. THUD. THUD.

Blood sprays from her split knuckles, speckling the flawless, double-paned glass. Outside, the manicured lawn sits in perfect, mocking stillness. The street is empty. 

She steps back, chest heaving. Her designer blazer is torn. Her immaculate presentation destroyed.

A deep, subsonic VIBRATION rattles her teeth. 

The polished oak floorboards beneath her feet groan. Not the creak of settling wood, but the wet, muscular contraction of a throat preparing to swallow.

The far end of the living room pitches upward. A violent fifteen-degree tilt.

Valerie loses her footing. Her Louboutins skid across the slick polyurethane finish. She crashes hard onto her hip.

Gravity drags her backward, sliding her toward the center of the vast room.

VALERIE
No, no, no--

She claws at the smooth wood, finding no purchase. 

In the center of the room, the floorboards split perfectly along the grain. They peel back. Not breaking, but curling outward like a blooming flower. 

Or peeling skin.

Beneath the joists: a gaping, fleshy MAW. 

It is a cavern of wet, pulsating muscle lined with concentric rings of jagged, translucent teeth. A hot, coppery stench wafts up from the abyss.

Valerie slides faster now. The tilt hits thirty degrees. 

She flips onto her stomach. Digs her manicured acrylic nails into the microscopic seams between the boards. 

Wood splinters. Two nails tear clean off. 

She screams, but the sound is swallowed by a wet, guttural HISS from the maw.

Her lower half dangles over the edge. The heat of the abyss singes the fabric of her skirt. The teeth click together, inches from her ankles.

She looks down into the dark. Her transactional instinct, her feral survival mechanism, violently kicks in.

VALERIE
Wait! You're looking at this wrong!

The floor tilts steeper. Forty-five degrees. Her grip slips another inch.

VALERIE
I'm a depreciating asset down there! You consume me, the pipeline dries up! I am the broker!

The house groans. A wet, acidic drool splatters against the joists below her. It doesn't care.

VALERIE
(screaming over the rumble)
I can scale this! You want volume? I can bring you volume! High net-worth!

The shifting floor pauses. Just for a microsecond. The house is listening.

Valerie’s arms tremble. Her muscles burn. Blood from her torn cuticles smears across the pristine oak.

VALERIE
You've been eating scraps. One client at a time. It's inefficient! Let me leverage my network!

The floor shifts again, dropping her half an inch lower. The teeth graze her heel.

VALERIE
A gala!

The sliding stops. 

Valerie hangs suspended, hovering over the throat of the beast. The house breathes beneath her. In and out. Hot and rancid.

VALERIE
An exclusive, invite-only gala. Right here. In the great room. The apex predators of the city. Politicians. Developers. The absolute top of the food chain.

She strains to pull herself up, but the angle is too steep. She locks eyes with the dark void.

VALERIE
I will pack this room with the most corrupt, bloodsucking capital in the state. A massive value add. A feast.

The house rumbles. A low, vibrating hum that feels almost like a purr.

VALERIE
I cater the whole damn thing. You just have to let me work.

Silence. The hot wind from the maw ceases.

SNAP.

The floorboards slam shut with the velocity of a bear trap. 

The architecture violently rights itself. The forty-five-degree tilt drops to zero in a fraction of a second.

Valerie slams flat against the perfectly leveled, solid wood floor.

The room is dead silent. Just the gentle, rhythmic hum of the central air conditioning.

Valerie lies spread-eagle on her back. Her chest heaves. Her hands are slick with her own blood.

She stares up at the vaulted ceiling. The pristine, recessed lighting shines down on her. 

She swallows hard. The deal is struck.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sits in the driver's seat, her fingers flying aggressively across her laptop keyboard. She hits 'ENTER' on a massive offshore wire transfer. A green 'ZERO BALANCE' confirmation screen illuminates her face. She exhales a long, shaky breath as the crushing weight of her secret debt vanishes. She flips down the sun visor, checks her reflection, and applies a coat of blood-red lipstick with absolute, unyielding precision.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's immediate reward for her sin, providing the financial relief that fuels her dangerous new arrogance.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. VALERIE'S LUXURY SEDAN - DAY

The interior of a Mercedes S-Class. Black leather. Burl walnut. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) sits behind the wheel. The engine is off. The windows are tinted pitch black.

She is a coiled spring in a designer blazer.

A sleek MacBook Pro balances precariously against the steering wheel. Her manicured fingers hammer the keys. Aggressive. Feral.

Click-clack-click. The rhythm of a woman digging her way out of a grave.

ON THE SCREEN: A labyrinth of offshore banking portals. Cayman routing numbers. Shell company accounts.

The cursor hovers over a red button: INITIATE WIRE.

AMOUNT: $4,250,000.00.

Valerie’s breathing is shallow, rapid. A bead of sweat threatens the perfect foundation on her forehead.

Her phone BUZZES in the cup holder. 

CALLER ID: TOBY ARIS.

She doesn't even blink. She blindly swats the phone, sending the call to voicemail. Her eyes never leave the screen.

She presses ENTER.

A loading wheel spins. 
One second. Two seconds. 
The silence in the airtight cabin is deafening.

Then--

The screen flashes BRIGHT GREEN.

The emerald glow washes over Valerie’s face, illuminating her wide, unblinking eyes.

TEXT: TRANSFER COMPLETE. OUTSTANDING DEBT: $0.00.

Valerie stares at the zeroes. They reflect in her dark pupils.

Her hands slip off the keyboard. They tremble violently in her lap.

She leans back against the leather headrest and exhales. It’s a long, jagged, ugly sound. The sound of a drowning victim finally breaking the surface.

For ten seconds, she just breathes. The feral desperation drains out of her posture, replaced by something heavier.

Something cold.

Her phone BUZZES again. Toby.

She snatches it. Swipes to answer.

VALERIE
(smooth, zero hesitation)
Toby. Pivot the afternoon schedule. Cancel the staging at the Silver Lake teardown.

TOBY (V.O.)
(through speaker, anxious)
But the sellers are already--

VALERIE
They're low-yield. Cut them loose. We're only focusing on high-net-worth acquisitions from now on. The market just shifted.

TOBY (V.O.)
Valerie, you can't just--

VALERIE
Value add, Toby. Keep up.

She ends the call. Tosses the phone onto the passenger seat.

She reaches up and snaps down the sun visor. The vanity mirror slides open. Harsh LED lights flick on.

Valerie stares at her reflection. The sweat is gone. The panic is erased. The mask is back in place.

She opens the center console and pulls out a heavy gold tube of lipstick.

She twists it. The color is deep, arterial blood-red.

With absolute, unyielding precision, she traces the outline of her lips. She doesn't smudge a millimeter.

She presses her lips together. A perfect, predatory smile spreads across her face.

She snaps the laptop shut.

She turns the ignition. The V8 engine ROARS to life.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie struts through the sterile, glass-walled brokerage with the posture of an apex predator. She stops at Toby's cluttered desk and tosses a heavy velvet box directly onto his keyboard. Toby opens it, his eyes widening at the designer watch inside. He nervously pushes up his glasses and immediately pulls out a thick, worn binder labeled 'Affordable Housing Trust,' sliding it toward her. Valerie offers a practiced, plastic smile, her eyes completely dead as she pretends to look at his zoning maps.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the B-Story of Toby's noble ambition, contrasting sharply with Valerie's transactional, guilt-driven generosity.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE - DAY

Sterile. Transparent. A labyrinth of floor-to-ceiling glass.

VALERIE VANCE cuts through the bullpen like a shark navigating a bait ball. Impeccably tailored. Shoulders squared. Junior agents instinctively shrink behind their monitors as she passes.

She zeroes in on a single desk in the corner. It is a chaotic island of Post-it notes and half-empty coffee cups. 

TOBY ARIS sits hunched behind dual screens, furiously typing. 

Valerie doesn't break stride. She tosses a heavy, black VELVET BOX. 

It lands dead center on Toby's keyboard. Clack.

TOBY
Oh, god, sorry, I was just-- the escrow on the Miller place is stalling, I'm trying to--

He blinks at the box. 

VALERIE
Open it.

Toby hesitates. He pops the lid. 

Nestled in the silk is a Patek Philippe watch. It catches the harsh fluorescent light. It costs more than his annual salary. 

TOBY
Valerie. This is... I mean, I can't. I didn't actually do anything to warrant an expenditure like this, the open house was basically a disaster before you told me to leave--

VALERIE
You are a high-yield asset, Toby. We're aggressively scaling our market presence, and I need my ecosystem optimized. Consider it a retention bonus.

Toby pushes his glasses up the bridge of his nose. A nervous, jagged motion. He doesn't look at the watch. He looks at her.

TOBY
Right, but, the clients... Mr. Sterling was asking about the Belmont buyers, and I didn't know what to tell him, because they just vanished, and--

VALERIE
They pivoted to an off-market overseas opportunity. The comps in that bracket are highly volatile. We don't chase dead leads.

Toby swallows hard. He reaches over and closes the velvet box. He immediately starts straightening a stack of manila folders, aligning their edges with surgical precision.

TOBY
Okay. Sure. Sorry. It's just... if we have this kind of liquidity right now? If we're operating with this kind of surplus?

He reaches into his bottom drawer. He heaves a massive, worn binder onto the desk. The spine is held together by duct tape. 

A hand-written label reads: AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST.

TOBY (CONT'D)
I was looking at the zoning maps for the Eastside corridor. The city is auctioning three distressed lots. If we leverage the brokerage's philanthropic tax incentives, we could underwrite a multi-family co-op. I mean, I know it's not our usual demographic, but the displacement rate down there is--

Valerie places a manicured hand flat on the binder. 

VALERIE
Toby.

TOBY
Sorry, I know, it's a lot of upfront capital, but the long-term community stabilization--

VALERIE
Where is the value add?

Toby freezes. 

TOBY
The... the value is that eighty families wouldn't be sleeping in their cars.

Valerie offers a smile. It is perfectly symmetrical. It does not reach her eyes. Her gaze is completely, terrifyingly hollow. 

VALERIE
Affordable housing is a depreciating asset class. It doesn't generate synergy. It generates maintenance requests.

Toby shrinks slightly. His fingers twitch toward the velvet box, then pull away.

TOBY
But you just spent... this watch could fund the architectural surveys.

VALERIE
(smoothly)
I invest in top-tier infrastructure. You are infrastructure. Those lots are a liability.

She pulls the binder toward her. She flips it open. 

Pages of meticulous zoning maps. Redlined districts. Photos of run-down neighborhoods. 

Valerie stares down at the pages. She isn't seeing them. She's seeing the pristine, blood-free diamonds rattling in her glove compartment. 

VALERIE (CONT'D)
Let's pivot back to the Bel Air listing. I need the square footage verified by noon.

Toby stares at her. The silence stretches, thick and heavy. 

He slowly reaches out and pulls the velvet box closer to his chest.

TOBY
I'll... I'll get the blueprints.

Valerie pats the open binder, a patronizing double-tap. 

VALERIE
That's the spirit. We're building an empire, Toby. Ground up.

She turns and glides away, perfectly composed, disappearing behind a wall of frosted glass.

Toby sits alone in the chaos of his desk. He looks down at the Affordable Housing binder. Then at the velvet box. 

He pushes his glasses up his nose.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leans intimately over Toby's shoulder. She reaches out, her manicured hand covering his, and physically guides his mouse to close the housing trust binder. She clicks open the city's public records database. Toby hesitates, his throat clearing in a nervous tic, but Valerie pats his shoulder encouragingly. Toby pulls up thick digital dossiers on corrupt slumlords and embezzlers. Valerie's eyes lock onto the screen, her smile sharpening into a predatory grin as she taps the glass monitor, selecting her targets.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Valerie weaponizes Toby's empathy, actively curating her hit list and committing to her role as the monster's provider.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. BROKERAGE - MOMENTS LATER

Toby's desk is a tragic island of empathy in a sea of chrome and glass. A thick, three-ring binder sits open next to his keyboard: GREENWAY AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST. 

Toby leans close to his monitor, highlighting a density zoning statute. He rubs his tired eyes beneath his glasses.

Valerie glides up behind him. Silent. She invades his personal space, leaning intimately over his shoulder. The scent of expensive perfume and ozone washes over him. 

She is close enough that her breath stirs his hair.

VALERIE
(soft)
Beautiful margins on that proposal, Toby. But we need to pivot.

Toby jumps. He immediately grabs a microfiber cloth and starts aggressively wiping down his monitor stand.

TOBY
Oh, God, Valerie, I was just-- I'm sorry, I was just reviewing the East Side variances while I waited for the Miller escrow docs. Which are coming, they just had a routing issue with the title company, I can call them right now--

Valerie’s manicured hand snakes out. Her cool fingers drape over his hand, trapping it against the computer mouse. 

Toby freezes. 

Slowly, deliberately, she guides his hand. The cursor glides across the screen. Click. The housing trust document minimizes. 

She reaches out with her free hand and flips his physical binder shut. A loud, final snap.

VALERIE
We're reallocating our bandwidth. Open the city's public records database.

Toby’s throat clicks. A harsh, nervous tic. He uses his free hand to perfectly align a stack of Post-it notes with the edge of his desk.

TOBY
The public records? I mean, I can, but those are mostly tax liens, code violations... it's not exactly our target demographic. I should probably follow up with the Miller escrow. Richard was asking--

VALERIE
The market is shifting. We need to identify high-net-worth individuals who are... highly motivated. Unencumbered by traditional oversight.

Valerie pats his shoulder. A slow, rhythmic tapping. Encouraging, yet heavy as a lead weight.

VALERIE
Think of it as a value-add for the community. We're stepping up. Disrupting the stagnation.

Toby hesitates. He adjusts his glasses, staring at the blank desktop background. 

TOBY
I'm sorry, I just-- I don't understand how targeting code violators helps our Q3 projections. The brokerage doesn't usually interface with distressed assets. 

VALERIE
Who owns the Vista complex? 

Toby blinks. 

VALERIE
The one with the black mold you're always petitioning against? The guy who evicted those three families last month.

TOBY
Marcus Grier. But he’s a slumlord, Valerie. He hides his assets in shell companies. He’s not looking for a modernist mansion in the hills.

VALERIE
Everyone is looking for an upgrade, Toby. Pull him up.

Toby’s fingers hover over the keyboard. He clears his throat again. He types. 

A thick digital dossier populates the screen. MARCUS GRIER. Associated LLCs. A laundry list of EPA fines, noise complaints, and brutal eviction notices.

VALERIE
Look at all that trapped capital. Just squatting on the city's potential. Who else?

Toby swallows hard. He picks up a stray paperclip, bending it out of shape.

TOBY
Well, there's... there's Arthur Vance-- no relation to you, obviously-- he embezzled from the teachers' pension fund last year. Never indicted because of a jurisdictional error. 

VALERIE
Bring him up.

Toby types. Another dossier appears. Side-by-side with Grier. 

VALERIE
(murmuring)
Untapped potential. 

Valerie’s eyes lock onto the glowing monitors. The polite, corporate veneer melts away entirely. Her posture shifts, dropping into something feral. 

A predatory grin stretches across her face. Her teeth slightly bared.

She raises a hand and taps a lacquered fingernail against the glass monitor. Right between the digital eyes of Marcus Grier.

VALERIE
Send them the exclusive invites. Tell them it's an off-market opportunity. Highly discreet. 

TOBY
I... I don't know if Richard will approve of this guest list. It's a massive liability to associate the brand with--

VALERIE
Richard doesn't have our vision, Toby. We're doing a public service. Expediting a market correction. 

She taps the glass again. Harder. A sharp, violent clack.

VALERIE
Book them for Friday night. The house is going to love them.

scenes.narrative_action: Councilwoman Elena Rostova drags her heavily ringed hand across the pristine marble countertops, sneering at the fixtures. Valerie stands safely by the doorway, gripping a clipboard tightly against her chest. Suddenly, the massive kitchen island splits down the middle with a wet, tearing sound. Jagged drywall and rusted rebar form a churning vortex. Rostova is violently yanked off her feet and dragged screaming into the fleshy garbage disposal. The island snaps shut. A fist-sized diamond clatters onto the floor. Valerie calmly walks over, pulling a silk handkerchief from her pocket to scoop it up.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Kicks off the Fun and Games montage with a spectacular, thematic kill that eliminates a major corrupt figure.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION KITCHEN - DAY

Sunlight bleaches the ultra-modern kitchen. It is a sterile cathedral of white marble and brushed steel. Too clean. Too quiet.

COUNCILWOMAN ELENA ROSTOVA (50s) paces the perimeter. She wears a sharply tailored suit that costs more than a sedan. 

Heavy, emerald-cut rings weigh down her fingers. She drags her hand across the pristine marble countertops. 

*Clink. Clink. Clink.* 

The rings tap rhythmically against the stone. Rostova sneers at a brushed-brass faucet, tapping it with a lacquered nail.

ROSTOVA
(measured, slow)
When your brokerage petitioned my office to re-zone this... historic parcel, the understanding was that the resulting development would reflect a certain civic standard.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands ten feet away, safely framed by the doorway. She grips a leather-bound clipboard tightly against her chest like a Kevlar vest. 

Her smile is a flawless, high-gloss veneer.

VALERIE
The developer spared no expense, Councilwoman. The Calacatta marble alone is a massive value-add. It completely redefines the market comps for the entire district.

ROSTOVA
Marble is just rock, Valerie. It doesn't vote. And it certainly doesn't fund civic re-election initiatives. 

Rostova moves toward the center of the room. A massive, monolithic kitchen island dominates the space. 

VALERIE
We are fully prepared to pivot our community outreach strategy. 

ROSTOVA
(softly)
Are you? Because looking at this excess... I feel a deep, moral concern for my constituents. I’m not sure I can sign off on the final permits. Not unless the property transitions to an owner who truly understands... public service.

Rostova runs her hand along the edge of the central island. 

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
An owner who might acquire it at, say, a heavily subsidized civic discount. For the good of the community.

Valerie doesn’t step into the room. Her knuckles are white against the clipboard. 

VALERIE
A civic discount.

ROSTOVA
To offset the moral cost of such aggressive gentrification. 

Deep within the walls, a subsonic hum begins to vibrate. The brushed steel of the refrigerator subtly rattles. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the floorboards, then back to Rostova. 

VALERIE
I think we can structure a highly motivated transaction. The seller is... exceptionally eager to close.

Rostova smiles. A cold, unblinking victory. 

ROSTOVA
I knew you were a pragmatist. 

Rostova leans over the kitchen island, peering down into the deep, stainless-steel basin of the sink. 

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
Though I will be calling my own contractors. The finish on this basin is entirely--

*GURGLE.*

A wet, organic sound echoes up from the sink’s drain. 

Rostova frowns. She leans closer. 

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
Is that the plumbing? You have a localized pressure failure.

VALERIE
It’s a bespoke disposal system. Very aggressive. 

ROSTOVA
It sounds like a slaughterhouse. 

*CRACK.*

The massive marble island splits violently down the middle. 

Not a clean break. The stone tears like wet flesh. A deafening, wet *RIIIIP* echoes through the kitchen as the architectural facade peels back.

Rostova stumbles back, her eyes wide. 

Beneath the marble, there is no plumbing. 

A churning vortex of jagged drywall and rusted rebar spins wildly. It is a fleshy, mechanical maw. Tendrils of copper wire writhe like exposed nerves.

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
What in god’s name--

A thick, pulsing cable of rebar whips out from the vortex. It wraps around Rostova’s ankle.

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
No! Help! 

She is violently yanked off her feet. Her chin slams against the edge of the collapsing island. Blood sprays across the white cabinets.

Valerie does not move. She watches, perfectly still.

Rostova claws desperately at the slick marble, her emerald rings screeching against the stone. 

ROSTOVA (CONT'D)
(screaming)
I am a city official! You can't--!

The rusted rebar pulls taut. 

Rostova is dragged backward, screaming into the fleshy garbage disposal. 

The vortex churns. A sickening crunch of bone and metal. The screaming stops abruptly. 

*SNAP.*

The two halves of the kitchen island slam back together. 

The wet tearing sounds vanish. The marble is instantly seamless. Pristine. Not a drop of blood remains on the cabinets. 

The kitchen is dead silent. 

Valerie slowly exhales. She lowers the clipboard from her chest.

*CLINK.*

A sharp, bright sound. 

Valerie walks into the kitchen. She approaches the island. 

Resting perfectly in the center of the spotless sink basin is a fist-sized, flawless diamond. It catches the sunlight, refracting brilliant prisms across the walls.

Valerie reaches into the pocket of her blazer. She pulls out a folded silk handkerchief. 

She calmly wraps the silk around the diamond, scooping it up without letting the stone touch her bare skin. 

She drops the heavy silk bundle into her pocket. 

VALERIE
(to the empty room)
Pleasure doing business.

scenes.narrative_action: A toxic Crypto-Billionaire paces the edge of the glass-bottomed infinity pool, aggressively shouting into his phone about liquidating assets. The water in the pool suddenly thickens, turning into a viscous, gelatinous membrane. It surges upward, wrapping around his ankles and violently yanking him beneath the surface. He thrashes against the shrinking, sentient water until his movements cease entirely. A pristine diamond bobs to the surface. Valerie, wearing oversized designer sunglasses, casually fishes it out with a pool skimmer.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates the montage, showcasing the house's creative, architectural methods of consumption and Valerie's growing desensitization.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MANSION INFINITY POOL - DAY

The infinity pool juts out over a dizzying, five-hundred-foot canyon drop. The water is a flawless, geometric slab of cerulean blue.

BRENT (30s) paces the wet teakwood coping. He is a walking red flag in five-hundred-dollar designer swim trunks, barefoot, and vibrating with toxic, stimulant-fueled energy. 

He barks into a waterproof satellite phone.

BRENT
Liquidate the tier-two wallets! I don't care about the gas fees, just dump it!

A few yards away, in the shade of a minimalist cabana, sits VALERIE VANCE. 

She wears a crisp white linen power suit and oversized, black designer sunglasses. She is meticulously reviewing a stack of escrow documents. 

She doesn't look up. 

BRENT
Because they're paper-handed cowards, that's why! You tell them I'm pulling the liquidity pool. The whole ecosystem is mine.

Brent kicks aggressively at the water's edge. 

The water doesn't splash. 

A ripple moves backward, against the wind. The cerulean surface loses its transparency. It turns cloudy. Viscous.

Brent is too busy screaming to notice the pool thickening into a gelatinous slime.

BRENT
I want them bled out! Every last token. If they cry about it, screenshot the thread and mint it as an NFT. 

The water at Brent's feet defies gravity. It creeps up the wet concrete. It breaches the coping.

A thick, translucent tendril of water slides over Brent's left ankle. 

Brent stops pacing. He frowns. He shakes his leg, expecting a splash. 

The water doesn't let go. It grips his skin like liquid silicone.

BRENT
(into phone)
Hold on. The filtration system is acting up or some shit. 

He tries to step back. A second tendril whips out, latching onto his right calf with the speed of a striking snake.

Brent loses his balance. He crashes hard onto the edge of the pool. The phone clatters across the teakwood deck.

BRENT
What the hell--

The pool surges. A tidal wave of clear gelatin rises up, entirely silent, and crashes down over Brent's lower half. 

It yanks him backward. Violently.

Brent's fingernails scrape across the wet wood, peeling back his cuticles. He screams, but the sound is instantly swallowed as he is dragged beneath the surface.

UNDERWATER

Brent thrashes in the deep end. But this isn't water anymore. It is a living, shrinking membrane. A synthetic womb of heavy fluid.

He kicks. He punches. The fluid absorbs his kinetic energy, hardening around his fists like cornstarch. 

Brent's eyes bulge in terror. He looks down. 

The bottom of the pool is solid glass. Below him is the terrifying, sheer drop to the canyon floor. 

The membrane compresses. Shrinking. Squeezing the remaining oxygen from his lungs like a python.

A stream of silver bubbles escapes Brent's lips. They don't rise. The gelatinous water digests them.

Brent spasms. Once. Twice. 

Then, absolute stillness. His body seems to dissolve into the blue, losing its edges, melting seamlessly into the architecture.

BACK ON THE SURFACE

The pool instantly relaxes. The viscosity vanishes. It is once again a flawless, gently rippling infinity edge.

Silence returns to the deck, save for the faint, tinny voice of the frantic crypto-trader still yelling from the dropped phone.

Plop.

Something small and heavy breaks the surface. It bobs gently on the water.

A rough-cut, fist-sized diamond. Pristine. Catching the harsh afternoon sun.

Valerie turns a page on her escrow documents. She signs the bottom line with a gold Montblanc pen. 

She finally looks up. Her expression behind the oversized sunglasses is entirely unreadable. 

She sets the clipboard down and smooths her linen jacket.

Valerie walks to the edge of the pool. She steps right over Brent's abandoned, chattering phone. 

She picks up a long aluminum pool skimmer leaning against the cabana wall. 

With practiced, mechanical efficiency, Valerie extends the telescopic pole over the water. 

She loops the net under the massive diamond. 

She drags it through the water, pulling the stone to the edge. It clinks heavily against the aluminum rim. 

Valerie reaches down, plucks the diamond from the wet mesh, and drops it into her designer blazer pocket. 

She doesn't even check the bottom of the pool. She just turns and walks back to her paperwork.

scenes.narrative_action: A predatory Hedge Fund Manager aggressively taps his cane against the hardwood floor, loudly demanding structural blueprints. Valerie takes two deliberate, measured steps backward into the hallway. Above him, the massive vaulted ceiling detaches with a sickening crack. It rapidly descends, crushing the manager flat against the floorboards with immense force. Not a single drop of blood spills. The ceiling silently resets to its original height. Valerie steps forward to collect her freshly minted diamond.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Concludes the kill montage, establishing the bloodless, efficient routine Valerie has developed with the entity.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - DAY

Sunlight streams through floor-to-ceiling glass, illuminating a breathtaking expanse of Brazilian walnut hardwood. 

GARRICK (60s) paces the empty room. He wears a bespoke three-piece suit and leans heavily on a silver-tipped mahogany cane. 

Thwack. 

He drives the tip of the cane into the floorboards. Testing the density. Looking for weakness. 

GARRICK
It’s a lot of dead space. 

VALERIE VANCE stands near the arched entryway. Impeccably tailored. A polite, razor-sharp smile plastered across her face. 

VALERIE
We prefer to call it an open-concept canvas. The previous owner engineered the acoustics to maximize the flow of natural light. It’s a tremendous value add.

Thwack. 

Garrick strikes the floor again. Harder this time. 

GARRICK
Acoustics don't generate ROI, Valerie. Square footage does. I look at this room and I see wasted capital. 

He points his cane toward the massive, thirty-foot vaulted ceiling above them. Exposed steel beams crosshatch the pristine white plaster. 

GARRICK
I want to drop a mezzanine right through the middle. Subdivide the airspace. Turn this cavern into two distinct, high-yield entertaining zones. 

VALERIE
A bold pivot. We’d certainly be maximizing the property's vertical footprint. 

Garrick sneers. He taps the cane rhythmically against the wood. 

Thwack. Thwack. Thwack. 

GARRICK
Don't manage me with your corporate jargon. I gut companies for a living. I know how to gut a house. 

Valerie’s smile doesn’t waver. Her eyes, however, drop to the floorboards beneath Garrick’s feet. A faint, almost imperceptible vibration ripples through the wood. 

VALERIE
I assure you, Mr. Garrick, this property is incredibly... accommodating to aggressive restructuring. 

GARRICK
Prove it. I want the structural blueprints. Now. I need to know which of these walls are load-bearing before I make an offer. 

Valerie takes a slow, deliberate step backward. Her Louboutin heel clicks against the marble of the hallway threshold. 

VALERIE
Of course. Due diligence is paramount. 

She takes a second measured step backward. She is now entirely out of the living room. Safe in the corridor. 

VALERIE
Though I suspect you'll find the structural integrity here is absolute. 

GARRICK
We’ll see about--

A sickening, deafening CRACK echoes through the room. 

Garrick freezes. He looks up. 

The entire thirty-foot vaulted ceiling detaches from the walls. 

It does not fall. It DRIVES downward. A piston of architectural fury. 

Garrick doesn't even have time to scream. 

The ceiling SLAMS into the hardwood floor with the force of a freight train. A massive displacement of air blasts into the hallway, whipping Valerie’s hair back. 

She doesn't blink. 

Underneath the tons of plaster and steel, there is a wet, muffled crunch. 

Then, absolute silence. 

No dust clouds the air. Not a single drop of blood seeps from the edges of the crushed drywall. The violent act is sterile. Surgical. 

A deep, hydraulic hum vibrates through the walls. 

Slowly, smoothly, the vaulted ceiling begins to rise. It glides upward, resetting itself into the high rafters as if on an invisible track. 

The ceiling locks back into place. Seamless. 

The living room is empty. Garrick is gone. His bespoke suit, his silver-tipped cane, his body. Erased. 

In the exact center of the pristine Brazilian walnut floor sits a solitary object. 

A massive, flawless, uncut diamond. It catches the sunlight, refracting a brilliant spectrum of color across the empty room. 

Valerie steps forward, crossing the threshold back into the living room. 

She walks with calm, practiced efficiency. She kneels beside the diamond. 

She picks it up, examining the clarity for a brief second. She slips it into her blazer pocket. 

VALERIE
(to the empty room)
Pleasure doing business. 

She turns on her heel and walks out. The floorboards gleam behind her.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling pours vintage champagne into two crystal flutes with steady, sociopathic precision. He hands one to Valerie, gesturing grandly through the glass walls to her new, massive corner desk, which is overflowing with congratulatory floral arrangements. Valerie accepts the glass, soaking in the opulent praise. She takes a long sip of the champagne, completely ignoring Toby, who watches her nervously from the bullpen outside, anxiously adjusting his watch.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The false victory. Valerie reaches the top of the corporate ladder, completely blind to the moral and physical danger she is in.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. RICHARD STERLING'S OFFICE - DAY

A bottle of 2004 Krug tilts. Golden liquid flows into a Baccarat crystal flute. 

Not a single drop splashes. The pour is executed with steady, sociopathic precision.

RICHARD STERLING (50s) watches the bubbles rise. Impeccably tailored. Serene.

RICHARD
Listen to that, Valerie. The carbonation. It sounds like inevitability.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands opposite the sprawling mahogany desk. She is radiant. Armor-plated in a designer pantsuit that costs more than her first car.

VALERIE
It sounds like a closed escrow.

Richard smiles. A tight, fatherly expression that doesn't quite reach his dead, unblinking eyes. 

RICHARD
Eighty-five percent of this city is prey. They wake up, they graze, they pray they don't get slaughtered by fluctuating interest rates. 

He stops pouring. The champagne rests exactly one millimeter below the rim.

RICHARD
And the other fifteen percent? 

VALERIE
They own the slaughterhouse.

RICHARD
Exactly. 

Richard picks up the flutes. He crosses the Persian rug, moving with the silent grace of a shark gliding through open water. He hands a glass to Valerie.

RICHARD
When you first came to my brokerage, you were hungry. But it was a frantic, desperate hunger. The kind that gets you eaten. 

VALERIE
I had a cash-flow problem. I pivoted. 

RICHARD
You evolved. 

He turns, gesturing grandly toward the floor-to-ceiling glass wall that separates his office from the rest of the world. 

Through the glass: The bullpen. 

Dozens of agents sit at cramped cubicles. But at the far end sits a massive, newly installed corner desk. 

It looks like a botanical garden. The mahogany surface is completely drowning in congratulatory floral arrangements--towering white orchids, lilies, and imported roses.

RICHARD
Look at it. You’ve completely disrupted the food chain. 

Valerie steps up to the glass. She stares at the desk. The physical manifestation of her survival. 

VALERIE
The floral budget alone could house a family in the valley. 

RICHARD
Empathy is a market inefficiency, Valerie. You finally shed it. You stopped swimming with the plankton.

VALERIE
I just optimized my strategy. The luxury sector requires a different... intake. The property demands a certain caliber of client, and I simply facilitate the transaction.

RICHARD
(chuckling)
Intake. I like that. Clinical. Unsentimental. 

Richard takes a slow sip of his champagne. His eyes lock onto the side of her face.

RICHARD
And Councilwoman Rostova? She just vanished into the ether after signing the disclosures?

Valerie doesn’t flinch. Her composure is absolute. 

VALERIE
She relocated. Discretion was her primary value add. It was a highly motivated transition.

RICHARD
Beautiful. No forwarding address. No messy paper trails. Just a clean, absolute monopoly on the city's most coveted zip code. 

Richard raises his glass toward the bullpen. 

RICHARD
To the apex.

VALERIE
To the apex.

Valerie raises her flute. 

Through the glass, just beyond her new desk, stands TOBY ARIS. 

His cheap suit is wrinkled. He holds a stack of escrow files tight against his chest like a shield. 

Toby isn't looking at the orchids. He is staring directly through the glass at Valerie. 

His breathing is shallow. He anxiously adjusts his cheap wristwatch. Then he pushes his glasses up his nose. A physical manifestation of mounting terror. 

Valerie’s eyes catch Toby’s through the glass. 

Toby swallows hard, silently pleading with her. 

Valerie's expression remains perfectly blank. She doesn't blink. She doesn't nod. 

She simply breaks eye contact, entirely dismissing his existence. 

Valerie turns her back to the glass, facing Richard again. She takes a long, deep sip of the vintage champagne. 

VALERIE
We should discuss my new commission splits. If I'm feeding the firm at this volume, I need a larger slice of the gross.

RICHARD
Naturally. The lioness always deserves her share of the kill.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, dressed in casual but expensive silk loungewear, hums to herself as she wipes down a floating glass banister with a microfiber cloth. She pauses to sip champagne from a crystal flute resting on the stairs, treating the deadly space like a tamed pet. As she wipes away a single speck of dust, a deep, unnatural groan vibrates through the soles of her shoes. The vibration intensifies, rattling the champagne flute until it vibrates off the step and shatters on the floorboards.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The turn of the sequence. Shatters Valerie's illusion of control and signals the monster's escalating hunger.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - DUSK

The dying sun bleeds through floor-to-ceiling windows, casting long, razor-sharp shadows across the minimalist expanse.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) glides down the floating staircase. She wears emerald silk loungewear. The fabric whispers against her skin, a soft counterpoint to the sterile, hard angles of the room. 

In one hand, a plush microfiber cloth. In the other, absolute ownership. 

She hums a slow, rhythmic jazz tune. "Lullaby of Birdland."

She pauses. Rests a crystal flute of Dom Pérignon on the edge of a teak tread. 

Valerie runs the cloth along the edge of the floating glass banister. Smooth. Methodical. She strokes the glass not like a janitor, but like a handler soothing a prized, lethal Doberman.

VALERIE
(murmuring)
Good boy. Settle down now. We had a very high-yield week.

She picks up the flute. Takes a sip. The champagne sparkles in the amber light. 

She looks out over the cavernous living room. The site of her recent, bloody triumphs. Now, just pristine, staged perfection. Not a drop of evidence left behind. Just pure equity.

VALERIE
Market comps are up twelve percent in the zip code. You and I... we're setting the new floor. I bring the assets, you process the transaction. Highly synergistic.

She smiles. A cold, corporate victory lap. 

She sets the flute back down on the teak step. 

Her eyes catch a flaw. A single, microscopic speck of dust on the glass. 

Valerie leans in. Her reflection stares back—polished, untouchable. She exhales softly onto the smudge. 

She brings the cloth down. Rubs it. 

A low frequency thrums. 

It doesn't start in the air. It starts in the floorboards. 

Valerie freezes. The cloth hovers an inch from the glass. 

The vibration climbs up the soles of her cashmere slippers. It travels through her calves, settling deep in her chest. 

A guttural, unnatural GROAN. Like the grinding of tectonic plates. Or an empty stomach.

VALERIE
(tight)
No. We talked about this. The zoning board doesn't meet until Tuesday.

The house doesn't care. The groan deepens. The pitch shifts from a satisfied purr to a structural demand. 

Dust shakes loose from the recessed lighting above. It drifts down like dirty snow.

Valerie grips the glass banister. Her knuckles go white. The veneer cracks. Her breathing turns shallow, rapid. The feral kid from the eviction line flashes in her wide eyes.

VALERIE
I said wait. You're fully capitalized. I just fed you a tech CEO. You're full.

The teak staircase shudders. A violent, localized tremor. 

Beside her foot, the crystal flute begins to dance. 

Tink. Tink. Tink.

The base of the glass taps against the wood, inching toward the precipice. 

Valerie stares at it. Paralyzed. The vibration is deafening now, rattling the fillings in her teeth. The house is starving.

Tink. Tink. Tink.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
Stop. That's a directive. Stop.

The flute reaches the edge. 

It tips. 

Time slows as the crystal drops through the empty space beneath the floating stairs. 

SHATTER.

The glass explodes against the polished concrete floor below. A violent, chaotic spray of crystal and champagne.

Silence slams back into the room. 

The vibration ceases. The house is still. Waiting.

Valerie stares down at the jagged shards. A drop of champagne drips from the teak step. It hits the wreckage below with a sickening, rhythmic plop.

She slowly pulls her foot back from the edge. She isn't the handler anymore.

scenes.narrative_action: The pristine white walls suddenly splinter and crack like dry skin. Thick, black blood seeps from the fissures, dripping down and ruining the imported Persian rugs. Valerie drops her cleaning cloth, her arrogant composure shattering into primal panic. She scrambles backward as every heavy oak door and reinforced glass window in the mansion violently slams shut and locks simultaneously with a deafening boom. The cavernous room is plunged into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the stakes by trapping the protagonist, physically demonstrating that the house is the master, not the pet.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie Vance (30s) is on her hands and knees. Her immaculate power suit is speckled with plaster dust. 

She scrubs a spot on the pristine hardwood floor using a branded microfiber cloth. Aggressive, tight circles.

A Bluetooth earpiece blinks on her ear.

VALERIE
(into earpiece)
I don’t care about the zoning board’s timeline, Toby. We pivot. Tell Councilwoman Rostova the seller is fielding aggressive cash offers. Create the scarcity.

She stops scrubbing. Her fingers brush against something lodged in the floorboards. 

She pries it loose. 

A DIAMOND. The size of a quail egg. Flawless. Pristine.

Valerie holds it up to the recessed lighting. The greed in her eyes is absolute, washing away any exhaustion.

VALERIE
(into earpiece)
Actually, Toby? Cancel my afternoon. The ROI on this property just went exponential.

She taps the earpiece. Call ends.

Valerie slips the diamond into her blazer pocket. She stands, brushing off her knees, regaining her towering corporate posture.

A sharp CRACK echoes through the cavernous room.

Like a glacier splitting in two.

Valerie freezes. Her eyes dart to the far wall.

The museum-quality Venetian plaster is expanding. A hairline fracture races from the baseboard to the crown molding.

VALERIE
You have got to be kidding me.

She marches toward the wall, instantly calculating the liability.

VALERIE
Structural settling. Fine. We escrow a repair credit. Minimal footprint. I can hide it behind the Rothko.

She reaches out to touch the crack.

The wall SPLINTERS.

Dry, jagged flakes of plaster peel back violently, like dead skin flaying off a corpse. 

Valerie recoils, clutching her hand to her chest.

A thick, viscous substance bulges from the fissure. It is not water. It is BLACK BLOOD.

It weeps from the wound in the wall, sluggish and heavy. The smell of copper and rotting earth fills the room.

The blood drops. SPLAT.

Right onto the center of a $40,000 imported Persian rug. The intricate silk fibers hiss, instantly saturated with the dark sludge.

Valerie’s breath hitches. Her polished corporate facade cracks. 

VALERIE
No. No, no, no. The staging.

She drops to her knees, frantically dabbing at the black blood with her little microfiber cloth. 

It is entirely futile. The blood smears, staining her manicured hands, ruining the silk.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
We had an arrangement! I bring the market value, you provide the equity! You do not damage the asset!

The house answers with a GROAN.

A deep, guttural vibration that rattles the fillings in Valerie’s teeth. 

Above her, the ceiling spiderwebs. Dozens of fresh cracks erupt across the flawless white expanse.

Black blood rains down in heavy, erratic droplets.

It splatters across Valerie’s designer suit. 

Primal panic takes over. The feral, desperate survivor bleeds through the executive exterior. 

She drops the blood-soaked cloth. She scrambles backward, crab-walking away from the center of the room.

Her Louboutins slip on the slick floor. She kicks them off, abandoning them.

VALERIE
This is a breach of contract! Do you hear me?! I am the exclusive listing agent!

She scrambles to her feet, sprinting toward the grand entryway.

BOOM.

The massive, solid oak front doors violently slam shut.

Valerie lunges, grabbing the heavy brass handles. She yanks. She throws her entire body weight backward.

Locked solid.

VALERIE
Open! Open the door!

BOOM.

The reinforced glass patio doors slam shut at the far end of the room.

Valerie spins around, her chest heaving.

BOOM. BOOM. BOOM.

Every single window in the living room slams down in rapid succession. The heavy steel latches snap into place on their own with a deafening metallic clatter.

The house is sealing itself.

Valerie backs away from the doors, her breathing shallow and ragged. She fumbles for her phone. 

She stares at the screen. No signal. Just a dead piece of glass.

The guttural vibration grows louder. The sound of a massive, starving stomach rumbling beneath the floorboards.

The recessed lights flicker. Once. Twice.

They die.

The cavernous living room is plunged into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.

Only the sound of dripping blood remains.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pounds her fists against the unbreakable glass windows until her knuckles bleed. The floorboards beneath her shift and tilt, physically sliding her toward the center of the room. The wood peels back, revealing a gaping, fleshy maw lined with jagged rebar teeth. Valerie digs her fingernails into the floorboards, desperately fighting the pull. Hovering inches above the maw, she screams out a frantic bargain, promising a massive, invite-only gala filled with the city's worst elites. The floorboards instantly snap shut, dropping her onto the solid wood, leaving her gasping for air in the dark.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Midpoint escalation. Valerie barely survives by making a desperate, massive promise, setting the ticking clock for the climax.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Thud. Thud. THUD.

Fists against floor-to-ceiling glass.

VALERIE VANCE hammers the impenetrable pane. Her tailored blazer is rumpled, her perfect blowout plastered to her forehead with sweat.

Smears of crimson streak the glass. Her knuckles are split open, bleeding freely.

She doesn't feel it. She steps back, chest heaving.

The pristine woods outside mock her. No neighbors. No cell towers. Just acreage.

A deep, sub-audible VIBRATION rattles her teeth. 

The house is waking up.

Valerie spins around. The sprawling, minimalist living room feels too long. The geometry is wrong.

Groaaan.

The floorboards beneath her feet shift. 

Not a subtle settling of foundation. A violent, mechanical TILT.

The entire eastern half of the room drops into a steep fifteen-degree incline.

Valerie loses her footing. Her designer heels slip on the polished oak. 

She falls hard onto her hip.

Gravity takes over. She slides toward the center of the room.

She kicks off her heels. Bare feet scrabble for purchase.

Nothing to grab. The architecture is flawlessly, ruthlessly smooth.

In the dead center of the room, the herringbone wood floor begins to WARP.

The planks bulge upward. They splinter, groaning like a dying animal.

Then, they PEEL BACK.

Not breaking. Peeling. Like thick strips of rotting skin.

A GAPING MAW opens in the foundation.

It is a throat of wet, pulsing darkness. Jagged, rusted REBAR juts out from the edges, angled inward like the teeth of a great white shark.

A hot, putrid wind exhales from the pit. It smells of copper and old dust.

Valerie slides faster. The incline steepens to thirty degrees.

A heavy marble coffee table slides past her. It tumbles into the maw.

CRUNCH.

The rebar teeth flex and crush the solid marble into white dust. Swallowed in a second.

Valerie’s eyes go wide. Feral panic breaks through the polished veneer.

She digs her acrylic fingernails into the seams of the hardwood.

Snap. Three nails tear completely off. Blood pools on the oak.

She catches a brass floor vent with her left hand. Her arm jerks taut.

Her lower half dangles over the edge of the pit.

The heat of the maw licks at her bare legs. The rebar teeth twitch, inches from her toes.

The floor steepens again. Forty-five degrees.

The metal vent begins to buckle under her weight.

VALERIE
(screaming over the rumble)
Wait! Stop! This is terrible ROI!

The house roars. A deafening rush of air. It wants meat.

VALERIE
I am the asset! You kill me, the pipeline dies!

One of the screws in the heating vent pops out. It pings down into the dark.

Valerie slips another inch. A rebar tooth grazes her shin. It burns like a hot iron.

She looks down into the abyss. There is no bottom. Just hunger.

She has one card left to play. She pitches.

VALERIE
You want volume?! I can scale this!

The rumble falters. Just a fraction. The house is listening.

VALERIE
(voice cracking, desperate)
I can bring you volume! A gala. An exclusive, invite-only open house!

The second screw in the vent groans.

VALERIE
The absolute top of the food chain! Politicians! Developers! The worst, richest parasites in this city!

The hot wind from the pit pauses. 

VALERIE
High net-worth! High caloric yield! A dozen of them. A buffet!

Valerie’s grip is failing. Her fingers are slick with her own blood.

VALERIE
I bring them to you! I serve them up! Just... let me close the deal!

Silence. 

The heavy, oppressive silence of a signed contract.

SNAP!

The floorboards violently slam shut.

The incline vanishes instantly. The room snaps back to perfectly level.

Valerie drops.

She hits the solid, flat oak floor with a bone-rattling THUD.

The ambient recessed lighting flickers, then dims into a cool, dormant twilight.

Valerie lies on her back. 

Gasping for air. Chest heaving.

She stares up at the pristine, vaulted ceiling.

Slowly, she brings her bloody, ruined hands to her face. 

She traces the unbroken, perfectly smooth floorboards beside her cheek. 

No maw. No teeth. Just a multi-million dollar listing.

She closes her eyes. The deal is struck.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, wearing a stunning, blood-red crimson gown, stands at the massive mahogany double doors, greeting a steady stream of the city's worst elites. Richard Sterling arrives, handing his cashmere coat to a terrified server. Valerie flashes a tight, practiced smile and physically places a hand on his back, subtly steering him and a group of predatory hedge-fund managers away from the safe zones and directly toward the 'hungriest' east wing of the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the geography of the trap and Valerie's active complicity in orchestrating the mass feeding.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION GRAND FOYER - NIGHT

The vaulted ceiling arches like a ribcage. A massive crystal chandelier hangs in the center, vibrating. A low, subsonic HUM resonates through the imported Italian marble, felt more than heard. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands dead center of the mahogany double doors. She wears a blood-red crimson gown. It fits like liquid armor. 

She flashes a tight, practiced smile. It doesn’t reach her eyes. 

A steady stream of the city’s elite floods the foyer. Tech bros in tailored hoodies. Pharmaceutical lobbyists. 

Valerie shakes hands, her movements mechanical, precise. 

VALERIE
Leverage the open floor plan, gentlemen. The networking ROI tonight is unprecedented. Please, pivot toward the champagne. 

TOBY ARIS (20s) hovers near the coat check. He aggressively polishes a silver tray. His knuckles are white. He refuses to look at the floorboards. 

Valerie catches his eye. She taps her wrist. 

VALERIE
(low)
Keep the foot traffic moving, Toby. Idle assets depreciate. And lock down the West Atrium. It’s at capacity. 

TOBY
(staring at the tray)
Right. Capacity. I’ll just... I’ll reorganize the velvet ropes. The hinges over there are making a sound. Like wet paper. 

VALERIE
It’s the settling foundation, Toby. Value-add charm. Go. 

Toby scurries off. 

The heavy mahogany doors swing wide. The winter wind howls, then abruptly dies as RICHARD STERLING (50s) steps over the threshold. 

He wears a bespoke tuxedo that costs more than a starter home. He surveys the foyer with the dead, unblinking eyes of a great white shark. 

Without breaking his stride, Richard shruggs off his heavy cashmere overcoat. He simply lets it fall backward. 

A TERRIFIED SERVER (20s) dives, catching the coat inches from the floor. The server scrambles away, hyperventilating. Richard doesn't even register the boy exists. 

Richard glides toward Valerie. 

RICHARD
You’ve created a remarkable ecosystem here, Valerie. I must admit, I didn't think you had the killer instinct to monopolize this tier of the market. 

VALERIE
(tight smile)
High barrier to entry, Richard. But once you optimize the structural advantages, the property practically manages itself. 

RICHARD
Nature abhors a vacuum. You found a weakness in the city's zoning, and you exploited it. Like a true apex predator. 

Richard casually taps the mahogany wall. 

RICHARD
Good bones. You can feel the hunger of the architect. It demands to be filled. 

VALERIE
It’s a seller's market. Demand is... exponential. 

Loud, braying laughter echoes from the entrance. A trio of HEDGE-FUND MANAGERS swagger in. They hold snifters of scotch, already flushed, loudly debating the liquidation of a pension fund. 

Richard watches them with mild amusement. 

RICHARD
Ah. The krill arrives. Shall we mingle with the capital, Valerie? 

Richard takes a step toward the grand staircase. The staircase leads to the second-floor safe zones. 

Valerie steps into his path. A seamless, elegant block. 

She places a firm, manicured hand flat against the small of Richard's back. 

VALERIE
Actually, Richard, let's pivot away from the main thoroughfare. The acoustics here are terrible for closing. 

Richard pauses, glancing down at her hand on his back. A micro-aggression. He hates being steered. 

RICHARD
I prefer the high ground. 

VALERIE
(smooth, unyielding)
The real untapped equity is in the East Wing. Original crown molding. Completely isolated from the noise. It’s... ravenous for a visionary. 

Valerie applies a fraction of an ounce of pressure to his spine. 

VALERIE
I want you to be the first to evaluate its consumption potential. 

Richard’s ego flares, successfully stroked. He nods slowly, his dead eyes shifting toward the darkened hallway of the East Wing. 

RICHARD
Isolated. I like the sound of that. Lead the way. 

VALERIE
Oh, I wouldn't dare walk ahead of you. 

Valerie gestures gracefully toward the Hedge-Fund Managers. 

VALERIE
Gentlemen? Exclusive showing. East Wing. Follow Richard, he knows exactly how to navigate a hostile takeover. 

The Managers cheer, raising their glasses to Richard. 

Richard smirks, straightening his cuffs. He leads the pack of men away from the brightly lit staircase. 

Valerie watches them walk. Her hand drops to her side. 

They cross the threshold into the East Wing. The hallway is impossibly dark. The shadows seem to stretch out, leaning hungrily toward the men's tailored suits. 

The subsonic HUM of the floorboards pitches up into a low, guttural purr. 

Valerie remains in the light. She watches the darkness swallow Richard Sterling whole.
