She doesn't blink. She just checks her watch.

scenes.narrative_action: Toby nervously pushes up his glasses, awkwardly holding open his 'Affordable Housing Trust' binder in front of a dismissive, champagne-swilling tech bro. Across the room, Valerie watches from the wet bar. She grips her crystal flute tightly, her jaw clenching as she witnesses Toby's earnest humiliation. She forces herself to turn her back on him, aggressively checking her diamond-encrusted watch to track the feeding schedule.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Highlights Toby's innocence and Valerie's suppressed guilt, setting up the moral stakes of the impending massacre.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

A cavernous, modernist cathedral of glass and black marble. The air is thick with the smell of expensive perfume and impending doom.

Dozens of elite, morally bankrupt guests mingle. City officials. Venture capitalists. Apex predators in tailored suits.

At the edge of a sunken conversation pit, TOBY (20s) stands out like a bruised thumb in his off-the-rack suit. 

He clutches a thick, worn binder. The cover reads: AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST.

Toby pushes his glasses up his sweaty nose. He holds the binder open toward BRENT (30s), a tech bro wearing a Patagonia vest over a bespoke Zegna shirt. Brent casually swills a glass of vintage Bollinger.

TOBY
(clears throat)
So, if you look at the, um, the third tab-- sorry, the pages are sticking-- the zoning pivot actually incentivizes a hybrid model. If we aggregate the underutilized parcels in the lower ward, the tax abatement alone covers the--

BRENT
Wait. Stop.

Brent holds up a hand, his smartwatch flashing.

BRENT
You're talking about putting low-income housing in the lower ward?

TOBY
Yes. Well, working-class families primarily. The trust structure ensures that we--

BRENT
Why would I want to subsidize my own traffic jam?

Toby blinks. He nervously brushes a piece of imaginary lint off his lapel.

TOBY
I'm sorry, I don't... the ROI isn't just financial, Mr. Miller. It's community stabilization. It keeps the workforce housed.

BRENT
Bro. I just dropped eight mil on a crypto-compound in the hills specifically to get away from the workforce. The vibes here are completely off.

Across the room, at the sprawling marble wet bar.

VALERIE stands motionless. Draped in severe, flawless silk. She looks less like a hostess and more like a sniper in a guard tower.

She watches Toby.

She grips her crystal champagne flute. Her knuckles are stark white against the glass.

BRENT (O.S.)
You're Valerie's assistant, right?

Valerie's eyes dart back to the conversation pit.

Brent laughs loudly. A drop of champagne sloshes out of his glass and lands directly on Toby's open binder.

BRENT
Does she know you're pitching this communist bake sale at her A-list mixer?

Toby frantically wipes the champagne off the plastic sheet protector. He shrinks into himself, his shoulders caving.

TOBY
She, um. She encourages a diversified portfolio. We're always looking for... value adds.

BRENT
Right. Tell you what. Put a golf simulator in the blueprints and I'll buy the air rights.

Brent smirks, his eyes already scanning the room for someone more important. He takes another long, arrogant sip.

Valerie's jaw clenches. A muscle feathers violently in her cheek.

She takes a half-step forward. The instinct to intervene. To absolutely eviscerate the tech bro with a few choice words about his over-leveraged startup.

A deep, sub-audible THUM vibrates through the floorboards.

The ice in Valerie's glass rattles. The surface of the champagne ripples.

The House is waking up. It's hungry.

Valerie freezes. The feral, panicked desperation claws at the back of her throat.

If she saves Toby's pride, she draws attention to herself. If she stops the feeding, she loses the diamonds. She loses the armor. She goes back to nothing.

Valerie squeezes her eyes shut. Just for a microsecond.

She forces herself to pivot on her stilettos. She turns her back completely on Toby's earnest, humiliating struggle.

She aggressively shoves back her silk sleeve, revealing a heavy, diamond-encrusted Patek Philippe watch.

11:43 PM.

She taps the glass face of the watch with a manicured fingernail. Tracking the schedule. Tracking the slaughter.

VALERIE
(whispering to herself)
Market correction. It's just a market correction.

A WAITER glides past the bar, carrying a silver tray of caviar blinis.

VALERIE
Excuse me.

The Waiter stops.

VALERIE
The gentleman in the fleece vest by the fireplace. Tell him the master suite's panic room is open for a private viewing.

The Waiter nods and heads toward Brent.

Valerie doesn't look back. She stares at her own reflection in the dark marble of the bar.

Cold. Untouchable.

She raises the crystal flute to her lips and drains it.

scenes.narrative_action: Julian Vance violently yanks open the drawers of Valerie's staging desk. He discovers a false bottom and rips it out, unearthing a heavy velvet bag. He dumps the contents onto the silk bedsheets: a pile of pristine, golf-ball-sized diamonds mixed with the bloody driver's licenses of the missing clients. Julian's eyes go wide. He pulls out his phone and snaps a rapid series of flash photos of the evidence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The ticking clock of exposure. Julian uncovers the physical proof of Valerie's horrific crimes.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MASTER SUITE - NIGHT

Moonlight bleeds through floor-to-ceiling windows, casting long, sharp shadows across the minimalist furniture.

JULIAN VANCE (30s, wearing a tailored suit jacket over a graphic tee) slips through the doorway. He holds his phone up, a live video call illuminating his smug, overly-tanned face.

JULIAN
(into phone)
No, I'm telling you, bro, the vibes are completely off. You don't go from dodging calls from creditors to moving a fifty-million-dollar pocket listing overnight.

Julian glides across the plush carpet toward a sleek, obsidian staging desk. 

JULIAN
She's laundering. Has to be. I'm gonna find the shell company, leak it to the board, and scoop the listing by Tuesday. I'm literally crushing it right now.

He props the phone against a high-end geometric lamp. The camera captures him going to work on the desk.

He yanks the top drawer open. Nothing but glossy brochures and high-end pens. He tosses them aside.

JULIAN
(muttering)
Come on, Val. Where's the leverage.

Second drawer. Empty. He slams it shut. The sound echoes too loudly in the cavernous room.

Julian’s smile tightens. His reflection in the dark window looks small, panicked. He aggressively checks his phone screen, making sure his buddy is still watching.

JULIAN
Probably uses a burner. Classic boomer move.

He grabs the handle of the bottom drawer. Pulls. It sticks.

Julian grunts, abandoning his polished posture. He braces his knee against the desk and YANKS.

The entire drawer tears off its tracks. Julian stumbles back. The drawer hits the floor with a heavy, unnatural THUD.

Julian dusts off his jacket, annoyed. He looks down.

The inside of the drawer is shallow. Barely three inches deep. But the exterior profile is easily six inches.

He kneels. Knocks his knuckles against the interior wood.

Hollow.

JULIAN
Oh, hello.

He grabs a solid steel letter opener from the desk. He jams the blade into the seam of the drawer's floor.

He pries it upward. Wood splinters. The false bottom snaps and gives way. Julian rips the thin wooden panel out and tosses it aside.

Sitting in the hidden cavity is a heavy, black velvet bag. It looks ancient, completely out of place in the sterile, modern room.

Julian lifts it. It weighs a solid ten pounds. The contents clink together with a dense, glassy resonance.

JULIAN
(grinning)
Game, set, match, Valerie. Let's see what you're hiding.

He carries the heavy bag to the center of the room. The massive king bed is covered in pristine, white silk sheets.

Julian grabs the bottom corners of the velvet bag. He upends it over the mattress.

A torrential cascade of wealth and horror.

Dozens of massive, golf-ball-sized DIAMONDS hit the silk. They catch the moonlight, refracting blinding, prismatic shards of light across the dark walls.

But they aren't alone.

Mixed in with the priceless stones are rectangles of laminated plastic. 

Julian stares. The bravado drains from his face, replaced by a cold, primal slackness.

He reaches out with a trembling hand. He ignores the millions of dollars in gems and picks up one of the plastic cards.

It's a California Driver's License.

The edges are caked in thick, dried blood. A dark, rust-colored smear obscures the face, but the name is legible: MARCUS TRENT.

JULIAN
(breathless)
The crypto guy... from the open house...

Julian drops the ID. It lands on a diamond, smearing rust-colored flakes onto the pristine facet.

He looks at the pile. There are at least five other licenses. All belonging to people who recently vanished. All painted in the same visceral red.

The silence of the house suddenly feels heavy. Alive.

Julian backs away from the bed. He trips over the discarded drawer, scrambling backward like a frightened child.

He bumps into the desk. His phone is still propped against the lamp.

Survival instinct--and a desperate need for proof--overrides his paralysis. He snatches the phone. Exits the video call. Opens the camera.

He steps back toward the bed. Hands shaking violently.

FLASH.

The blinding white strobe illuminates the diamonds and the bloody plastic.

FLASH. FLASH.

He snaps rapid-fire photos, the harsh light rhythmically cutting through the darkness, cementing the nightmare in digital reality.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches down the dimly lit corridor, only to be violently shoved against the custom wainscoting by Julian. He traps her against the wall, his breath reeking of gin, and shoves the velvet bag of diamonds directly into her chest. He aggressively taps his phone screen, showing her the photos, and holds up five fingers, physically demanding a fifty-percent cut of her operation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The external threat peaks. Julian corners the protagonist, forcing a confrontation that threatens to destroy her hard-won empire.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - NIGHT

Shadows stretch across pristine Brazilian cherrywood floors. 

VALERIE marches down the corridor. Her heels click like a metronome against the hardwood. She is a shark in a tailored blazer. Focused. Calculating.

A blur of seersucker fabric steps out from a recessed doorway.

JULIAN violently shoves Valerie against the custom wainscoting. 

The impact knocks the wind out of her. Her shoulder blades slam into the artisanal woodwork with a sickening thud. 

Julian crowds her. He plants his forearms on the wall on either side of her head, physically trapping her. The heavy, sweet stench of gin washes over her face.

VALERIE
(wheezing, adjusting her collar)
Julian. You're scuffing the millwork. I don't expect you to understand the overhead on custom finishes, but--

JULIAN
(laughs loudly)
God, the vibes in this place are atrocious. Seriously, Val? It's giving 'haunted mausoleum.'

He digs into his blazer pocket. Yanks out a small, black velvet bag. 

He shoves it hard into her sternum. 

JULIAN
Found your little side hustle.

The bag clinks against her collarbone. Heavy. Unmistakable. 

VALERIE
(eyes darting)
I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about. If you're looking for a referral fee on the Sterling account, we can pivot to a standard dual-agency contract. The market comps--

JULIAN
Stop pitching. You're embarrassing yourself. Look at this.

Julian whips out his phone. He aggressively taps the screen, swiping past his own lock screen. He shoves the glowing retina display inches from her nose.

On screen: grainy, low-light photos. Valerie dragging a heavy tarp. The unmistakable, pristine flash of raw diamonds scattered in the basement dirt.

JULIAN
Crushing it. Honestly. I didn't think you had the bandwidth to pull off a jewel smuggling ring. 

VALERIE
(voice tightening)
Those images are entirely out of context. You're trespassing on an exclusive listing. The liability you're exposing yourself to right now--

JULIAN
Is that blazer off the rack? Cute. Very 'assistant regional manager.' 

Julian pockets the phone. He leans in closer, pressing his weight against her.

JULIAN
Here's the new paradigm, Val. I'm your new partner.

Julian raises his right hand, placing it right in her face. He slowly, deliberately, extends all five fingers. 

JULIAN
Fifty percent. Of everything. Or I forward this little album to the zoning board. And the cops. And Ricky Sterling.

Valerie's polished veneer shatters. Her breathing turns shallow, feral. Her eyes go wide with absolute panic. 

VALERIE
The margins don't support that kind of split. You don't know what you're asking for. The maintenance on this property... the overhead is astronomical. It's not a liquid asset, Julian. It requires... specific capital.

JULIAN
Figure it out. You're the one always talking about 'value add.' Add some value to my portfolio.

VALERIE
Julian, listen to me. You can't handle the pipeline here. The clientele. It's a highly volatile market ecosystem. You don't even know how to run a basic title search without your father's paralegal.

Julian's smile tightens into a thin line. He pulls out his phone again, aggressively tapping the screen, refusing to look her in the eye.

JULIAN
I've got three million followers on TikTok, Val. I think I understand ecosystems. 

He looks up, his eyes dead and greedy. 

JULIAN
Fifty percent. By tomorrow.

He pats her patronizingly on the cheek. 

JULIAN
Don't look so stressed. It's bad for your pores.

Julian steps back, smoothing his lapels. He turns and saunters down the dark hallway, laughing to himself. 

Valerie remains pinned to the wall. Her chest heaves. 

Her eyes lock onto the velvet bag in her trembling hands. The metronome of her heels is replaced by the deafening silence of the house. 

Waiting to be fed.

scenes.narrative_action: Before Valerie can answer, the ambient recessed lighting flickers and shifts to a sickly, pulsating crimson. The pristine white drywall directly behind Julian ripples like a bruised stomach. A wet, fleshy fissure tears open in the wall, violently yanking Julian backward by his blazer. He screams, dropping his phone, as the drywall seamlessly seals over his face. A synchronized, ear-splitting slam echoes through the house as thick, bone-like deadbolts shoot across every door and window.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The sequence turn. The house drops its disguise, eliminates the blackmailer, and traps everyone inside.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Julian stands blocking the exit. He taps the screen of his iPhone, a smug, unearned grin plastered across his face. 

JULIAN
The vibes are entirely off, Val. I mean, you’re supposed to be crushing it, right? But I’ve got three different buyers telling me your open houses are roach motels. People check in. They don’t check out. 

Valerie stands paralyzed by the imported marble console. Her perfectly manicured fingers grip the stone edge so hard her knuckles turn white. 

VALERIE
Let’s pivot, Julian. You’re looking at micro-anomalies instead of the macro value-add. This property is an exclusive, off-market ecosystem. The clients who tour this home demand absolute privacy. 

Julian laughs loudly. It echoes down the cavernous, empty hall. He aggressively checks his phone, thumb hovering over the screen.

JULIAN
Privacy? Bro, I have Ring cam footage from the neighbor. Rostova’s people walked in Tuesday. Never walked out. I’m literally drafting the expose right now. It’s going to break the algorithm. 

Valerie steps forward. The polished veneer vanishes. Her eyes are wide, feral. A trapped animal calculating the cost of a severed limb. 

VALERIE
We can synergize. A fifty-fifty split on the commission. I’ll even let you put your name first on the signage. Think of the brand elevation. 

Julian scoffs, adjusting the lapels of his velvet blazer. 

JULIAN
I don’t want a co-list. I want the whole portfolio. You’re going to transfer the exclusive rights to me right now, or I press send and nuke your entire career. So. What’s the play?

Before Valerie can answer--

BZZZT. 

The recessed LED lighting flickers. Dies. 

A beat of heavy darkness. 

Then, the hallway is bathed in a sickly, pulsating CRIMSON glow. It throbs. A steady, biological rhythm. 

Julian sighs, rolling his eyes. 

JULIAN
(annoyed)
Really? Cheap smart-home theatrics? That is so off-brand for a luxury--

Behind Julian, the pristine white drywall RIPPLES. 

It undulates like a bruised stomach digesting a heavy meal. The texture shifts from flat matte paint to wet, glistening membrane. 

Valerie backs away. Her breath catches in her throat. 

VALERIE
Julian. Step away from the wall. 

JULIAN
Don't try to gaslight me, Val. I asked you a direct--

SCHLUCK. 

A wet, fleshy FISSURE tears open in the center of the drywall. 

Thick, muscle-like tendrils whip out. They latch onto Julian’s velvet blazer. 

Julian doesn't even have time to turn his head. 

The wall violently YANKS him backward. 

JULIAN
(a high-pitched shriek)
WAIT--!

He drops his iPhone. It clatters loudly against the hardwood. 

Julian is pulled backward into the fissure. His arms flail, expensive loafers kicking wildly against the baseboards. 

The drywall SEALS over his face. 

A wet crunch. A muffled whimper. 

Then, seamless perfection. The wall is pristine, flat, and white once more. Not a single drop of blood. 

Valerie stares at the empty space. Her chest heaves. She doesn't scream. She calculates. 

On the floor, Julian’s iPhone glows. The draft is still open. 

Suddenly, the house groans. A deep, structural vibration that rattles the floorboards. 

THWACK. 

A massive, bone-like deadbolt shoots across the front door. 

THWACK. THWACK. THWACK. 

A synchronized, ear-splitting SLAM echoes down the corridor as identical, skeletal locks violently secure every single door and window in sight. 

The crimson light pulses faster. 

Valerie is trapped.

scenes.narrative_action: Absolute chaos. A group of embezzling tech billionaires scramble over each other as the massive white sectional sofa liquefies into a fleshy, tooth-filled pit, swallowing three of them whole. Valerie casually sidesteps a snapping floorboard that tries to bite a fleeing guest's ankle. She strides through the blood-soaked carnage with bulletproof arrogance, adjusting her crimson gown and expecting absolute immunity from the slaughter.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates the house's terrifying new form while showcasing Valerie's sociopathic delusion that she is safe from the monster she feeds.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Pure auditory assault. A symphony of tearing fabric, snapping cartilage, and the frantic scuff of Italian leather on hardwood. 

Three EMBEZZLING TECH BILLIONAIRES--uniformed in matching fleece vests and sheer panic--scramble over the living room’s imported Persian rug. 

Behind them, the room’s centerpiece is actively liquefying. 

The massive, custom white velvet sectional sofa undulates. The pristine fabric splits open to reveal a cavernous, fleshy pit. Rows of jagged, translucent teeth rotate inward like a garbage disposal.

TRENT (40s) and CHAD (30s) slide backward toward the chasm. Their fingernails carve deep, desperate grooves into the rug.

The sofa inhales. 

Trent and Chad are violently yanked into the maw. A wet, deafening CRUNCH. A geyser of crimson mist sprays across the floor-to-ceiling windows.

Enter VALERIE. 

Her crimson evening gown is immaculate. She glides into the room holding a leather-bound portfolio, completely unbothered. She surveys the slaughter with the mild irritation of a host dealing with a spilled cocktail.

BRADEN (30s), the last surviving billionaire, clings desperately to the edge of the solid marble coffee table. His legs dangle over the churning sofa-pit.

BRADEN
(screaming)
Valerie! Jesus Christ, help me! Call the police!

Valerie stops. She checks her Cartier watch.

VALERIE
I really can't authorize third-party vendors on site right now, Braden. We're in the middle of a showing. 

Braden's sweaty hands slip. He drops an inch. 

The sofa growls. A low, rumbling vibration rattles the crystal chandelier above them.

BRADEN
It ate Chad! It fucking ate him!

VALERIE
Chad was over-leveraged. We discussed this at the mixer. In a volatile market, dead weight gets liquidated. It’s just a standard correction.

A FLEEING GUEST sprints past Valerie, blindly heading for the hallway archway. 

Beneath the guest’s feet, a wide plank of French oak flooring suddenly snaps upward like a bear trap. Splintered wood acts as fangs. It lunges at the guest's ankle.

The jaws miss bone but clamp onto a Gucci loafer, ripping it right off the guest’s foot. The guest scrambles away down the hall, sobbing.

Valerie casually steps over the thrashing, hungry floorboard. 

She pauses to adjust the delicate silk strap of her gown, taking a moment to admire her reflection in the blood-spattered window glass.

BRADEN
I'll give you anything! Seed capital! My offshore accounts! Just pull me up!

Valerie sighs. She steps closer to the edge of the pit, looking down at him. 

VALERIE
Braden, let's focus on the value add here. Your startup is bleeding cash, the SEC is drafting your indictment as we speak, and frankly, your personal brand is a liability to this property's exclusivity. 

BRADEN
You're insane! You're a monster!

VALERIE
I'm a facilitator. And right now, the seller is highly motivated.

The coffee table groans under Braden's weight. The marble begins to tilt toward the pit.

The sofa surges upward. A thick, fleshy appendage whips out and wraps around Braden's waist.

He doesn't even have time to scream. He is violently yanked down into the dark chasm. 

The jaws snap shut with the heavy, metallic finality of a bank vault.

Silence crashes back into the room. 

The fleshy pit begins to bubble and reform. The teeth retract into the gums. The dark meat smooths out, bleaching itself back into soft, pristine white velvet. 

Within seconds, it is just a high-end sectional again. Not a single drop of blood remains on the upholstery. The room is immaculate.

Valerie stands perfectly still. Waiting.

The house settles. Deep inside the walls, the copper pipes groan softly, mimicking the sound of a satisfied stomach.

From beneath the sofa's center throw pillow, a soft CLINK.

A flawless, golf-ball-sized diamond rolls across the Persian rug. It catches the light of the chandelier, refracting a brilliant spectrum across the room.

The diamond comes to a gentle stop perfectly against the pointed toe of Valerie's stiletto.

She doesn't flinch. She just smiles, bending down to pick it up.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's confident stride halts abruptly. Above a cowering Richard Sterling, fleshy, muscle-like tendrils whip down from the HVAC vents. Instead of grabbing the corrupt boss, the tendrils snatch up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. Silver trays crash to the marble floor as innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling. Valerie's arrogant smile vanishes, replaced by sheer, paralyzed horror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Shatters Valerie's illusion of control. The monster mutates from a tool of vigilante justice into an indiscriminate, gluttonous beast.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The click of designer heels against imported marble. A rhythmic, victorious sound.

Valerie strides into the cavernous dining room. Her posture is immaculate. The apex predator surveying her newly acquired territory.

At the far end of the long mahogany table, RICHARD STERLING is on his knees. 

His bespoke Tom Ford suit is stained with spilled burgundy. He scrambles backward, his manicured hands slipping on the polished floor, until his back hits the wainscoting. He is cornered.

Valerie stops ten feet away. She looks down at him. A cold, arrogant smile plays on her lips. 

VALERIE
You’re over-leveraged, Richard. For five years I watched you treat our agents like disposable capital. You told me empathy was a market inefficiency.

Richard stares up at her, chest heaving. The genial, fatherly facade is completely gone, replaced by the cornered panic of a trapped animal.

RICHARD
(breathless)
You don't control this... this property, Valerie. You're a minority shareholder. You always were.

VALERIE
Consider this a structural optimization. I'm just balancing the books.

Valerie takes a deliberate step back. She gestures toward the ceiling with an open palm, offering him up. 

A deep, structural GROAN reverberates through the floorboards. The temperature in the room plummets. The crystal chandelier begins to sway, clinking softly in the sudden, icy draft.

From deep within the walls, a wet, rhythmic thudding begins. A heartbeat in the drywall.

Richard presses himself flat against the floorboards. He covers his head with his arms, squeezing his eyes shut.

Above him, the massive brass grilles of the central HVAC vents buckle inward. The metal groans, then SNAPS.

Thick, fleshy tendrils whip down from the dark ceiling shafts. They are raw, pulsing muscle, glistening with a clear, acidic mucus. They uncoil with terrifying speed, hovering inches above Richard’s trembling back.

Valerie’s smile widens. She waits for the liquidation.

The tendrils taste the air. They twitch. 

Then, they shoot right past him.

Across the room, standing frozen near the swinging kitchen doors, are THREE CATERING SERVERS. Young, underpaid, clad in crisp white shirts and black aprons. 

The lead tendril snaps forward like a bullwhip. 

It wraps violently around the ankle of a YOUNG WAITER. 

He is yanked off his feet. 

His massive silver tray of champagne flutes crashes to the marble floor. A deafening explosion of shattering crystal and foaming wine.

VALERIE
Wait. No. 

The arrogant smile vanishes from Valerie's face. 

More tendrils erupt from the perimeter vents. They bypass the cowering billionaire entirely, surging toward the terrified staff. 

A second tendril wraps around the waist of an OLDER BARTENDER. He screams, clawing desperately at the doorframe. His fingernails splinter against the oak as the house drags him backward across the slick marble.

VALERIE
(voice cracking)
Stop! The target is him! The transaction is Richard!

The house does not listen. It is not a vigilante. It is just hungry.

The Young Waiter is hoisted into the air, dangling upside down. He thrashes, kicking at the pulsing muscle binding his leg.

YOUNG WAITER
Help me! Please, somebody help!

Valerie is paralyzed. Her pristine veneer shatters completely. She takes a half-step forward, her hands trembling, but she is entirely powerless. 

The ceiling plaster around the vents cracks and crumbles, widening the shafts into jagged, fleshy maws. 

The Older Bartender is violently yanked upward. His head slams into the brass grating. He is pulled through the narrow opening with a sickening, wet crunch of dislocating bones. 

His screams are muffled by the ductwork, then silenced entirely.

The Young Waiter is dragged up next, clawing at the air. 

YOUNG WAITER
(sobbing)
I don't want to die, I don't--

With a violent jerk, the house swallows him into the ceiling. 

The brass grilles snap back into place. 

Silence slams back into the dining room. The only sound is the soft dripping of spilled champagne pooling among the shattered glass.

Valerie stands frozen. Her breathing is shallow, rapid. The feral desperation of her childhood poverty, the absolute terror of powerlessness, bleeds into her wide eyes. 

Slowly, Richard lowers his arms. 

He looks at the empty space by the kitchen doors. He looks at the shattered crystal. Then, he looks up at the ceiling. 

He brushes a piece of fallen plaster off his ruined suit lapel. He climbs to his feet, straightening his tie. The cold, sociopathic calm returns to his face. 

RICHARD
Supply and demand, Valerie. 

Valerie slowly turns her horrified gaze toward him.

RICHARD
The market doesn't care about your little moral crusades. The apex predators adapt. The bottom feeders... 
(he steps over a piece of broken glass)
They just provide the volume.

Valerie stares at the bloodless ceiling, the illusion of her control completely, irreparably shattered.

scenes.narrative_action: Toby drops his housing binder, backing away in terror as the hallway walls rapidly calcify into jagged, bone-like spikes. The walls begin to slide together on a motorized track of flesh, crushing inward. Toby covers his head, screaming for help as the spiked drywall tears at his suit jacket. From across the foyer, Valerie spots him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The ultimate consequence of Valerie's greed targets the one person she actually cares about, forcing her to act.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS

Pristine, minimalist architecture. Cold and perfect.

Toby navigates the narrow hallway. He clutches a massive, heavily tabbed binder to his chest. The cover reads: "URBAN HOUSING INITIATIVE."

He pauses. Adjusts his wire-rimmed glasses. He zeroes in on a microscopic scuff mark on the Venetian plaster.

He sighs, pulling a crumpled tissue from his pocket, and bends down to buff it out.

TOBY
(muttering)
Just log the square footage, Toby. Focus on the deliverables. Don't look at the... the implications.

A deep, guttural GRUMBLE vibrates through the floorboards. Like a massive stomach turning over.

Toby freezes. His hand hovers over the scuff mark. 

He recoils, yanking his hand back. The pristine plaster is sweating.

TOBY
Hello? Is someone-- I’m just doing the final punch list. I didn't mean to intrude on the, um, the private spaces. I can come back.

The wall BUBBLES.

Toby scrambles backward. The smooth white surface ripples like boiling milk.

With a sickening CRACK, the plaster calcifies. It violently hardens into a jagged, ivory protrusion. A massive bone spike.

Then another. And another. 

They erupt down the length of the corridor in a synchronized, violent wave.

Toby’s hands shake uncontrollably. The heavy binder slips from his sweaty grip.

SMACK. 

Hundreds of affordable housing blueprints scatter across the hardwood floor. 

Toby doesn't even look down. He backs away, hyperventilating.

TOBY
Okay. Okay, no, this is just a structural anomaly. Just a... a shifting foundation issue. 

The baseboards SPLIT open. 

A wet, tearing sound fills the hall. Beneath the cracked wood lies a motorized track of raw, pulsating muscle. 

The corridor walls begin to slide inward. 

TOBY
Oh god. Oh my god, I’m sorry! I’m sorry!

The walls accelerate, crushing together. The floor space vanishes.

Toby turns to run, but a jagged bone-hook lunges from the drywall. It snags the shoulder of his suit jacket. 

RIP. The fabric tears. Toby is violently pinned against the shifting, fleshy wall.

He covers his head with his arms as the spiked drywall inches from his face, threatening to impale him.

TOBY
(screaming)
VALERIE! PLEASE! VALERIE!

ACROSS THE FOYER

Valerie stands beside the monolithic marble kitchen island. Impeccably tailored. Cold as the stone she leans against.

She holds her cell phone to her ear.

VALERIE
No, Richard, listen to me. We pivot. It’s a massive value add. The seller is highly motivated and the comps in this zip code are bulletproof.

She inspects her flawless manicure.

VALERIE
I don't care about the optics. The human element is a non-factor. We close the deal today.

Toby’s blood-curdling scream echoes out from the corridor archway.

Valerie stops mid-breath. 

She turns. Through the sweeping archway, she sees the shadows stretching across the hall. She sees the walls closing in on a track of wet sinew.

She sees Toby. Her assistant. 

TOBY (O.S.)
I don't want to die here! Valerie!

For the first time, Valerie's polished, predatory mask slips. The corporate veneer shatters, revealing pure, feral panic. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
Toby.

The cell phone slips from her hand. 

It shatters against the marble floor. 

Valerie abandons her posture. She abandons her deal. She sprints toward the crushing walls.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie violently kicks off her designer stilettos. She sprints barefoot across the blood-slicked marble, diving headfirst into the closing, spiked corridor. She grabs Toby by the collar of his shirt, using her momentum to violently yank him backward out into the foyer just as the jagged walls slam together with a deafening, powdery crunch.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Valerie takes her first physical action to save someone other than herself, shedding her corporate armor (the shoes).
scenes.draft_text:
49 INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The corridor ahead of Toby GROANS. It is not settling wood. It is a deep, digestive rumble.

The mahogany paneled walls suddenly WARP inward. Crown molding fractures into jagged, stalactite-like spikes.

Toby stands frozen in the center of the hallway. He clutches his leather-bound folio to his chest. He pushes his glasses up his nose.

TOBY
Valerie, I think the structural integrity here is-- I’m sorry, I should have checked the inspection report again, I--

The walls LURCH. Closing in. A foot of space vanishes.

In the foyer, Valerie stands perfectly still. Her eyes dart from Toby to the closing walls. 

Calculations race behind her eyes. The house is hungry. Let it eat. 

Toby drops his pen. He bends down to pick it up.

TOBY
(muttering)
Just need to file the addendums...

The walls SNAP closer. Six inches left. 

Valerie looks down at her feet. Five-inch Christian Louboutin stilettos. Her armor. Her status.

She violently kicks them off. They clatter against the marble.

Valerie SPRINTS.

Her bare feet hit the blood-slicked floor. She skids, arms windmilling, fighting for traction. 

She finds it. Pushes off a dry patch of stone. 

The corridor is a vice, grinding shut. The sound is deafening-- a cacophony of shrieking nails and snapping timber.

Valerie dives headfirst into the crushing maw.

She slides on her stomach across the slick floor. The jagged spikes tear at the shoulders of her blazer. 

Toby looks up, eyes wide. 

Valerie’s hand shoots out. She grabs a fistful of Toby’s wrinkled dress shirt collar.

VALERIE
Pivot!

She uses her forward momentum to violently YANK him backward. 

They fly out of the corridor, tumbling backward into the sprawling expanse of the foyer.

CRUNCH.

The walls SLAM together. A deafening, powdery explosion of pulverized drywall and splintered mahogany. 

A thick cloud of white plaster dust billows out, coating them both.

Silence. Just the heavy, ragged breathing of two survivors.

Valerie lies flat on her back. Her immaculate blazer is shredded. Her bare feet are smeared with dark, tacky blood.

Toby is sprawled beside her. He blinks through dust-caked glasses. He frantically pats his chest, searching for his folio. It is gone. Eaten.

TOBY
I lost the Rostova contracts. I’m... I’m so sorry, Valerie. I can draft them again from memory, I just need a minute to--

Valerie sits up. She stares at the solid wall of crushed wood where the hallway used to be.

VALERIE
The market shifted. We’re restructuring the portfolio.

Toby stops patting his chest. He looks at her bare, bloody feet. He looks at the discarded Louboutins near the front door.

TOBY
You took off your shoes.

Valerie pulls her torn lapels together. A futile attempt to restore her corporate veneer.

VALERIE
They were impeding my lateral mobility.

TOBY
They were three thousand dollars. You said they were a write-off.

VALERIE
Depreciating asset.

Toby stares at her. The nervous tic in his jaw stills. For the first time, he does not look away.

TOBY
You pulled me out.

Valerie refuses to meet his gaze. She aggressively brushes a thick layer of plaster dust off her sleeve.

VALERIE
Replacing an executive assistant in Q4 is a logistical nightmare, Toby. The onboarding alone would kill my margins.

TOBY
It was a direct action. You intervened.

VALERIE
I protected an essential company resource. Do not read into it.

She tries to stand, her bare feet slipping slightly on the bloody marble. She catches her balance. 

Valerie looks down at her hands. They are trembling. She quickly balls them into tight fists.

VALERIE
We need to re-evaluate the floor plan. Clearly, the square footage is... fluid.

Toby slowly pushes himself up. He does not dust himself off. He just watches her.

TOBY
I don't think we can appraise this, Valerie.

VALERIE
Everything has a price per square foot. We just have to find the right buyer.

She turns her back to the crushed hallway. To the house. To him.

VALERIE
Let's go. We have a showing at five.

She walks away, leaving her designer shoes behind in the dust.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags Toby up the glass floating stairs, desperately trying to escape the shifting ground floor. Midway up, the architectural glass violently buckles and shatters under their weight. They plummet, slamming hard onto a narrow, trembling landing. Beneath them, the hardwood floor splits wide open, revealing a gaping, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth. Sulfurous smoke billows up, choking them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Physically corners the protagonist and her B-story anchor, removing all avenues of escape for the climax of the sequence.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FLOATING STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

The foyer floor below them is a churning sea of architectural violence. Hardwood ripples like water. Plaster snaps.

Valerie hauls Toby by the collar of his cheap suit. They scramble onto the floating glass staircase. 

Toby’s loafers slip on the pristine glass treads. He slams his shin against the edge.

TOBY
(panting)
I dropped the clipboard. Valerie, the NDA forms--

VALERIE
Leave them! Upward mobility, Toby! We pivot to the second floor!

She yanks him up another step. Her breathing is ragged, but her grip is iron. 

Below them, the Persian rug folds inward, swallowing a marble pedestal whole with a wet, fleshy SLURP.

Toby looks down. His face drains of color. He pushes his glasses up his sweaty nose.

TOBY
It ate her. The house... it actually digested a sitting city councilwoman. The liability...

VALERIE
It’s a structural anomaly. We re-appraise. We disclose the... aggressive subsidence. Keep moving!

TOBY
Valerie, this isn't a market fluctuation! It’s a digestive tract!

A sickening CRACK echoes through the stairwell.

Valerie freezes. Her Louboutin stiletto hovers over the next tread. 

Spiderweb fractures race across the reinforced architectural glass beneath her feet. The sound is like ice giving way over a frozen lake.

Toby stops breathing. He presses his back against the glass handrail. 

TOBY
The load-bearing capacity. It wasn't rated for--

VALERIE
Shut up. Don't look down. Look at the mezzanine. Look at the equity. 

She slowly extends a manicured hand toward him. Her polished veneer is cracking. The feral, desperate survivor from the gutter peeks through her wide eyes.

VALERIE
Just take my hand. We restructure the exit strategy. 

Toby reaches out. His trembling fingers brush hers.

The staircase BUCKLES.

The glass shatters into a million glittering shards. 

Valerie and Toby plummet. 

They slam brutally onto the narrow mid-level landing. 

Breath leaves their lungs in a violent rush. A rain of tempered glass cascades over them, pinging against the Brazilian walnut floorboards.

Toby curls into a fetal position, gasping for air. He blindly searches the floor, finds his glasses--now diagonally cracked--and shoves them onto his face.

Valerie pushes herself onto her hands and knees. Her tailored blazer is torn at the shoulder. Blood trickles down her forearm. She ignores it.

VALERIE
(wheezing)
Okay. Okay. We utilize the master suite balcony. The fire escape is a viable asset.

Toby looks back at the empty air where the stairs used to be. Nothing but a twenty-foot drop into a churning, hungry dark.

TOBY
Valerie. The infrastructure is gone. We're completely isolated.

A deep, guttural RUMBLE vibrates through the floorboards beneath their palms.

The landing trembles violently. 

Valerie scrambles backward, hitting the solid concrete wall. Toby crawls beside her. 

The walnut wood groans, warping upward as if something massive is pressing against the subfloor. Nails pop out with the velocity of bullet casings.

VALERIE
No. No, I hold the exclusive listing on this property. 

A jagged fissure splits the floor right between them. 

Splinters explode outward. The floorboards peel back like dead, dry skin. 

Beneath the elegant flooring: a gaping, fleshy trench. 

Rows of translucent, jagged teeth shift and grind in the darkness. They are the size of butcher knives. Saliva thick as industrial resin drips from the enamel, sizzling as it hits the remaining wood.

A hiss of immense pressure. 

Yellow, sulfurous smoke billows up from the maw. It smells like rotten eggs, copper, and centuries of decay. 

The toxic cloud engulfs the landing. 

Toby coughs violently, pressing his sleeve over his mouth. He shrinks against the wall, his knees pulled to his chest.

TOBY
(choking)
I don't... I don't think I can file a grievance for this.

Valerie doesn't cough. She stares into the acidic abyss. 

The snapping jaws clamp empty air inches from her ruined designer shoes. The ultimate consumer, demanding its next meal.

Valerie presses her hands flat against the wall behind her. There is nowhere left to climb.

scenes.narrative_action: A deep, guttural vibration shakes the landing, rattling Valerie's teeth. The house physically tilts the landing, trying to slide Toby into the pit. Toby desperately clings to Valerie's crimson gown, his feet dangling directly over the churning acid. Valerie looks down at his terrified, tear-filled eyes. She stops trying to pull him up and just stares, seeing her own monstrous, sociopathic reflection in his gaze. Her grip tightens on his hand, her expression shifting from panic to a hardened, self-loathing resolve.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The profound moral awakening. The house offers a literal sacrifice, but Valerie recognizes her own monstrosity and refuses the deal.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FLOATING STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

A low, guttural VIBRATION reverberates through the suspended glass steps. It hits the resonant frequency of human bone. Valerie’s jaw chatters.

The architectural masterpiece isn't settling. It’s chewing.

Below them, the grand foyer has dissolved into a churning, bioluminescent pit of gastric acid. It hisses, spitting droplets of corrosive mist into the humid air.

With a sickening SCREECH of twisting steel, the entire landing TILTS. 

Gravity violently shifts forty-five degrees. 

Toby scrambles. His sensible loafers find zero traction on the polished Italian marble. He slides backward, accelerating toward the drop.

TOBY
(hyperventilating)
Valerie, the coefficient of friction here is-- I’m sliding, I’m so sorry to grab the silk, I know the dry cleaning on this is astronomical--

He blindly catches the hem of Valerie’s crimson gown. 

The bespoke fabric TEARS. 

Valerie drops to her knees, slamming her elbows against the glass railing to anchor herself. She catches Toby’s wrist just as his legs swing out over the abyss.

His full body weight jerks her shoulder socket. Valerie winces, her high heels skidding an inch toward the edge.

VALERIE
Toby, leverage your core! We need to pivot your center of gravity!

TOBY
(dangling)
I’m trying! But the structural integrity of this cantilever is completely compromised, I don't think I can--

VALERIE
Do not conceptualize a loss right now! I am maximizing our current asset retention. Just kick up!

The walls PULSE. A wet, rhythmic contraction echoes through the drywall. 

A wave of hot, rotting air washes over them. The house is salivating. It knows a meal is on the hook.

Toby looks down at the bubbling, blue-white acid. A marble bust of a Roman emperor dissolves into the froth, melting like sugar. 

Toby swallows hard. He looks up at Valerie. His glasses have slipped down the bridge of his nose. 

He clears his throat. A nervous, distinctly professional tic.

TOBY
Valerie, realistically, the overhead is too high. You can't support the sheer weight. 

VALERIE
Shut up and give me your other hand.

TOBY
You're going to liquidate both our positions. It's fine. I'll just... I'll just resign. You should let go. 

Valerie freezes. 

The frantic, kinetic struggle drains out of her. She stops pulling. 

She just looks at him. 

Toby is terrified. Tears cut clean tracks through the plaster dust on his cheeks. He isn't fighting anymore. He is offering himself up as a tax write-off.

Valerie stares into his thick, smudged lenses. 

In the reflection of the glass, she sees herself. 

Her immaculate hair is wild. Her teeth are bared. Her eyes are wide, feral, and calculating. She is holding him by the wrist, weighing his life against her own survival. 

She looks exactly like Richard. She looks exactly like the house.

The silence stretches, deafening against the hiss of the acid.

TOBY
(voice breaking)
I just wanted to build affordable units. That's all.

Valerie’s breath hitches. The sociopathy of her entire existence crashes down in a single, crushing wave.

VALERIE
(whispering)
The ROI on that is terrible.

TOBY
I know.

The house SHUDDERS, impatient. It violently bucks the staircase, demanding its payment. 

Valerie’s fingers slip. 

Toby drops an inch. He closes his eyes, accepting the plummet.

Valerie lunges. 

Both of her hands clamp onto his forearm. A vice grip. Her perfect, manicured nails dig deep enough to draw blood. 

Toby’s eyes snap open. 

Valerie hauls him upward, her muscles screaming. The panic in her face is entirely gone. The corporate mask is dead. 

What remains is a cold, hardened, terrifying resolve.

VALERIE
We are not closing this deal.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, wearing a stunning, blood-red crimson gown, stands at the massive mahogany double doors, greeting a steady stream of the city's worst elites. Richard Sterling arrives, handing his cashmere coat to a terrified server. Valerie flashes a tight, practiced smile and physically places a hand on his back, subtly steering him and a group of predatory hedge-fund managers away from the safe zones and directly toward the 'hungriest' east wing of the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the geography of the trap and Valerie's active complicity in orchestrating the mass feeding.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION GRAND FOYER - NIGHT

Crystal chandelier light fractures across the imported marble like broken ice. 

VALERIE VANCE (30s) anchors the center of the room. She wears a blood-red crimson gown. A predator in haute couture. 

Massive mahogany double doors stand wide open behind her, swallowing the night. A fleet of black town cars idles in the driveway, discharging a steady, toxic stream of the city’s elite. 

Pharmaceutical lobbyists. Tech-bros. Oil heirs. 

Valerie greets them with surgical precision. A nod here. A brief, transactional laugh there.

She catches a frail, ELDERLY SOCIALITE wandering toward the right side of the foyer. Valerie steps in smoothly, blocking her path.

VALERIE
(smiling)
Mrs. Gable. The lighting in the West Wing is much more forgiving to the complexion. Let’s pivot you toward the champagne.

Valerie gently nudges the old woman to the left. The Safe Zone. Brightly lit. Full of string quartets and harmless canapés.

A sharp gust of wind howls through the mahogany doors. 

RICHARD STERLING (50s) glides across the threshold. Impeccably tailored in charcoal wool. He doesn’t walk so much as claim territory. 

Flanking him are two HEDGE FUND MANAGERS, TODD and GREG (40s). They have the flushed, aggressively loud faces of men who short-sell pensions for sport.

A TERRIFIED SERVER (20s) hurries forward, holding a silver tray of hors d'oeuvres with trembling hands. 

Richard doesn’t look at the kid. He simply shrugs off his heavy cashmere overcoat and drops it directly onto the silver tray, crushing the delicate pastries. 

The Server freezes, panicked. Richard steps past him without breaking stride.

RICHARD
Valerie. I must admit, I didn't think you had the stomach for this kind of volume. 

VALERIE
I’ve optimized my risk tolerance, Richard. The market demanded a value add.

RICHARD
It’s a fascinating ecosystem you’ve curated tonight. Half the GDP of the eastern seaboard is standing on this marble. 

Richard’s dead-eyed gaze sweeps the foyer. He looks up at the vaulted ceiling. 

RICHARD (CONT'D)
There is a natural order to architecture like this. It filters out the weak. You can feel the capital baked into the foundation. It’s the apex predator of the zip code.

VALERIE
It absolutely dominates the local comps. 

TODD
(loudly)
Sterling says you’re holding out on the premium square footage. We’re not here to look at the starter-wing, Vance. 

GREG
We want the aggressive portfolio. Show us where the real leverage is.

Valerie’s eyes flick to the men, then back to Richard. She flashes a tight, practiced smile. The veneer is flawless. 

VALERIE
Of course. You gentlemen are looking for maximum absorption. 

Valerie steps directly into Richard’s personal space. 

She places a flat, firm hand against the small of his back. 

With a subtle, irresistible pressure, she physically pivots the head of her brokerage away from the brightly lit West Wing. 

She steers him toward the EAST WING. 

The corridor here is yawning. Dimly lit by flickering sconces. The shadows stretch long and jagged against the vintage wallpaper. 

RICHARD
(chuckling)
Careful, Valerie. You’re becoming territorial. 

VALERIE
Just protecting my top-tier assets. The East Wing is entirely exclusive. Unregulated floor plans. 

Valerie’s hand remains on his back, herding him forward. Herding all three of them.

VALERIE (CONT'D)
The layout in there is incredibly aggressive. Once you get deep inside, you completely lose your sense of the outside world. It’s a total market capture.

A deep, structural groan echoes from the darkness of the East Wing. The massive oak floorboards shift, just a fraction of an inch. 

A cold draft pulls from the hallway. It smells faintly of ozone and old copper. 

The house is salivating.

RICHARD
(smiling)
I love a property with a killer instinct. 

VALERIE
It’s starving for investors like you, Richard. Just keep walking past the library. You’ll find exactly what you deserve.

Richard nods, satisfied by the deference. He strides into the dim corridor, Todd and Greg flanking him, laughing about offshore tax shelters. 

Valerie stops at the edge of the shadows. She lets her hand fall back to her side. 

She watches the three men walk deeper into the belly of the East Wing. 

The tight smile vanishes from her face. Her features go entirely slack. Cold. Dead.

Behind her, the mahogany double doors slam shut with the deafening crack of a vault locking. 

Valerie doesn't flinch. She just turns her back to the dark, smoothing out the fabric of her blood-red gown.

scenes.narrative_action: Toby nervously pushes up his glasses, awkwardly holding open his 'Affordable Housing Trust' binder in front of a dismissive, champagne-swilling tech bro. Across the room, Valerie watches from the wet bar. She grips her crystal flute tightly, her jaw clenching as she witnesses Toby's earnest humiliation. She forces herself to turn her back on him, aggressively checking her diamond-encrusted watch to track the feeding schedule.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Highlights Toby's innocence and Valerie's suppressed guilt, setting up the moral stakes of the impending massacre.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

The living room is a cavernous maw of mid-century modern angles and floor-to-ceiling glass. Jazz plays softly, undercutting the low, predatory hum of elite networking. 

TOBY ARIS (20s, perpetually rumpled) stands near a monolithic limestone fireplace. He clutches a thick, meticulously tabbed binder reading: AFFORDABLE HOUSING TRUST. 

He stands across from BAXTER (30s), a Patagonia-vested tech bro swirling a glass of vintage Krug. Baxter’s eyes dart around the room, actively scanning for more important people.

TOBY
...so if we leverage the municipal tax incentives, sorry, the state-level municipal rebates, we can actually offset the margins on the low-income units. Which I know isn't a traditional ROI, but the community impact is--

BAXTER
Let me stop you right there, Tim.

TOBY
(adjusts glasses)
Toby.

BAXTER
Right. Toby. You're pitching subsidized housing. In my portfolio, that's not an asset class. That's a liability. 

TOBY
Well, actually, the long-term stabilization of the neighborhood increases the adjacent property values by a minimum of--

BAXTER
I build algorithms that replace human middle-management, Toby. I don't house them. Know your audience. 

Baxter laughs, clapping Toby on the shoulder hard enough to make him stumble. 

BAXTER (CONT'D)
Keep grinding, though. Cute binder.

Baxter saunters away, disappearing into a sea of designer suits. 

Toby is left alone. He shrinks into himself, frantically trying to smooth down a bent plastic tab on his binder. 

ACROSS THE ROOM

VALERIE VANCE (30s, armor-plated in Tom Ford) watches from the obsidian wet bar. 

Her grip on her crystal champagne flute tightens. Her knuckles go stark white. A muscle feathers in her clenched jaw. 

She watches Toby meticulously wipe a smudge off his binder cover. The earnest humiliation radiates across the room. Valerie’s pristine, sociopathic veneer cracks for a fraction of a second. She takes a sharp breath. 

She forces herself to turn around. She faces the mirrored backsplash of the bar, putting her back to Toby. 

She snaps her wrist up, aggressively checking her diamond-encrusted Audemars Piguet watch. 

11:42 PM. 

She taps the crystal face of the watch. Tracking the schedule. 

Toby approaches the bar. He stands beside Valerie, staring down at his shoes. He begins physically reorganizing the pens in his breast pocket. 

TOBY
I think I over-indexed on the tax incentives. Sorry. I should have led with the zoning loopholes, he just... he didn't even look at the blueprints.

VALERIE
He's a series-C vulture who monetizes data mining, Toby. It’s a complete misalignment of capital. Pivot. Focus on the value add.

TOBY
Right. Pivot. I just... I thought if I could get one of these guys to see the human element, you know?

Toby takes off his glasses. He cleans them on his tie. A nervous, defeated tic. 

VALERIE
The human element doesn't move the needle. Market comps move the needle. You're trying to sell a teardown to a guy looking for turnkey.

TOBY
I can try Councilwoman Rostova next. If I just reorganize these tabs, maybe I can--

Valerie checks her watch again. 

11:44 PM. 

A low, subsonic vibration rattles the crystal glasses on the glass shelves behind the bar. The floorboards emit a subtle, anticipatory creak. The house is waking up. 

Valerie’s eyes dart to the floor, then back to Toby. 

VALERIE
You're done for the night. 

TOBY
But the open house goes until two. I'm fine, really, I'm sorry if I'm projecting a negative headspace, I can just grab a water and--

VALERIE
You are depreciating my asset right now. Your energy is a liability on the floor. 

Toby freezes. The corporate jargon hits him like a physical slap. 

VALERIE (CONT'D)
Take the binder, go to your car, and audit the escrow files.

TOBY
Audit the files? In the car?

VALERIE
Yes. Now. Do not come back inside. 

TOBY
I... okay. Sorry. I just wanted to maximize the networking window. I'll... I'll go to the car. 

Toby clutches his binder to his chest. He turns and weaves through the dense crowd of doomed billionaires, heading toward the heavy mahogany front doors. 

Valerie watches him go. She doesn't apologize. She doesn't call him back. 

She lifts her crystal flute and downs the rest of her champagne, bracing herself as the floorboards beneath her feet give another hungry groan.

scenes.narrative_action: Julian Vance violently yanks open the drawers of Valerie's staging desk. He discovers a false bottom and rips it out, unearthing a heavy velvet bag. He dumps the contents onto the silk bedsheets: a pile of pristine, golf-ball-sized diamonds mixed with the bloody driver's licenses of the missing clients. Julian's eyes go wide. He pulls out his phone and snaps a rapid series of flash photos of the evidence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The ticking clock of exposure. Julian uncovers the physical proof of Valerie's horrific crimes.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MASTER SUITE - NIGHT

A cavern of minimalist shadows. Floor-to-ceiling glass overlooks a pitch-black canyon. The silence in the room is absolute, heavy, and unnatural. 

The heavy oak door CREAKS open. 

JULIAN VANCE (30s) slips inside. He wears a bespoke suit that screams 'new money' and a pair of designer loafers that squeak faintly on the polished concrete floor. 

He holds his iPhone high, the screen illuminating his perfectly sculpted, tense face. He's recording.

JULIAN
(whispering to the lens)
Vibe check, familiars. We are deep behind enemy lines. Valerie's 'exclusive' pocket listing. 

He pans the camera across the sprawling, sterile bedroom. 

JULIAN
Let's see what the wicked witch of the Westside is hiding. Because nobody pivots from subprime foreclosures to nine-figure listings on sheer hustle. The math ain't mathing.

Julian lowers the phone, keeping the camera rolling. He approaches Valerie’s STAGING DESK. It’s a brutalist slab of imported black marble and dark wood, stationed near the window. 

He pockets the phone. Rubs his hands together. 

He grabs the top drawer. Yanks it open. 

Empty file folders. A lint roller. 

He slams it shut. 

Grabs the middle drawer. Yanks. 

Nothing but high-end staging pens and a single, pristine business card. 

Julian’s tight, arrogant smile begins to falter. He aggressively checks his Apple Watch. Time is running out. 

JULIAN
Come on, Val. Give me the tea. 

He grabs the bottom drawer handle. Pulls hard. 

It sticks. 

Julian grunts, planting a loafer against the marble slab for leverage. He pulls with his entire body weight. 

The drawer rips completely off its metal runners. 

Julian stumbles back. The heavy wooden box clatters against his shins. 

JULIAN
Shit. 

He winces, hopping on one foot. He looks down at the overturned drawer. 

It’s completely empty. 

But it’s too thick. The depth of the drawer doesn't match the interior dimensions. 

Julian drops to his knees. He knocks his knuckles against the inside bottom panel. 

THUD. THUD. 

Hollow. 

Julian’s eyes light up. The unearned bravado returns in a flash. 

He wedges his manicured fingernails into the tight seam of the wood. He pries upward. 

SNAP. 

A thumbnail breaks below the quick. 

JULIAN
Fuck!

He sucks his bleeding thumb, looking frantically around the desk. He grabs a heavy, solid-brass staging pen from the floor. 

He jams the sharp end of the pen into the seam. Uses it as a lever. 

CRACK. 

The wood splinters. The false bottom gives way. 

Julian violently rips the thin wooden panel out and tosses it aside. 

Tucked deep inside the dark cavity is a heavy, black VELVET BAG. It bulges awkwardly, like a swollen stomach. 

Julian reaches in. His breath hitches. 

He lifts the bag. It requires two hands. The weight of it pulls his shoulders down. It clinks with a dense, muffled sound. 

He stands, carrying the bag to the center of the room. 

The California King bed is perfectly made. Crisp, white silk sheets glowing in the ambient moonlight. 

Julian grabs the bottom corners of the velvet bag. 

He upends it over the silk. 

A cascade of impossible wealth and visceral horror spills out. 

DOZENS OF DIAMONDS. 

They are flawless. The size of golf balls. They hit the mattress with heavy, muted thuds, catching the moonlight and refracting cold, sharp prisms across the dark walls. 

But they aren't alone. 

Intermingled with the pristine gems are PLASTIC CARDS. 

Julian leans in, his shadow falling over the bed. 

The cards are California Driver's Licenses. Black American Express cards. Platinum health insurance cards. 

They are thickly smeared with dried, dark brown blood. Strands of torn human tissue cling to the plastic edges. 

The blood stains the immaculate white silk sheets, seeping outward in jagged red halos. 

Julian stops breathing. 

His eyes go impossibly wide. The smarmy, internet-slang persona evaporates entirely. He is just a terrified boy in an expensive suit. 

His trembling hand reaches out. 

He pinches the corner of a blood-caked driver's license, lifting it from a cluster of diamonds. 

The face on the ID is obscured by a bloody thumbprint, but the name is clear: 'BRADLEY, CHAD'. 

Julian drops the card. It clinks against a massive diamond. 

He wipes his bloody fingertips frantically on his bespoke suit pants, leaving dark streaks across the wool. 

JULIAN
(barely a croak)
No... no, no, no. 

He backs away from the bed. His chest heaves. 

He stares at the pile. The sheer, astronomical financial value of the diamonds wars violently with the gruesome reality of the blood. 

He pats down his pockets. Panicking. 

He rips his iPhone out. His hands are shaking so badly he drops it on the carpet. 

He scrambles to his knees, snatches the phone, and stands back up. 

He swipes out of the video app. Opens the camera. 

Julian frames the bed. The glittering diamonds. The bloody IDs. 

FLASH. 

The blinding white strobe illuminates the horrific still-life in hyper-detail. 

FLASH. 

The light paints Julian's terrified, sweat-slicked face in stark bursts. 

FLASH. FLASH. FLASH. 

The rapid-fire bursts of light strobe through the dark master suite, capturing the damning evidence over and over again, as the shadows in the corners of the room seem to stretch just a little bit closer.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches down the dimly lit corridor, only to be violently shoved against the custom wainscoting by Julian. He traps her against the wall, his breath reeking of gin, and shoves the velvet bag of diamonds directly into her chest. He aggressively taps his phone screen, showing her the photos, and holds up five fingers, physically demanding a fifty-percent cut of her operation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The external threat peaks. Julian corners the protagonist, forcing a confrontation that threatens to destroy her hard-won empire.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - NIGHT

The ambient, bass-heavy thrum of a high-end gathering bleeds through the floorboards. Dim, recessed lighting casts sharp, geometric shadows across the corridor.

VALERIE (30s) marches down the hall. Her posture is weaponized. Heels clicking in a rapid, relentless rhythm.

She checks her phone. Swipes away a frantic text message.

A shadow detaches from a darkened alcove.

JULIAN (30s) steps directly into her path.

Before Valerie can pivot, Julian lunges.

He violently shoves her backward. Valerie’s shoulders slam into the custom millwork. The impact knocks the breath from her lungs in a sharp hiss.

Julian crowds her immediately. He traps her against the wall, planting a hand on either side of her head.

The heavy, sweet stench of gin rolls off his breath.

VALERIE
(gasping)
The wainscoting is hand-fluted mahogany, Julian. Please mind the finish.

JULIAN
The vibes are off, Val. Very dark.

He leans in closer. His designer suit is rumpled, the collar unbuttoned. 

JULIAN
I thought you were just crushing it. A sudden, miraculous pivot to the ultra-luxury sector. But then I did a little walkthrough of my own.

Valerie’s eyes dart down the empty, cavernous hallway. She adjusts the lapel of her blazer, attempting to reconstruct her armor.

VALERIE
If you want to discuss market comps, we can schedule a sit-down. My assistant can--

Julian cuts her off, driving a small, heavy velvet bag directly into her sternum. 

It clinks. A dense, icy sound.

Valerie freezes.

JULIAN
Found this in the primary suite's dumbwaiter.

Valerie stares at the velvet bag pressed against her chest. Her pulse jumps visibly in her throat.

VALERIE
You're trespassing on an exclusive listing. It’s a massive liability.

JULIAN
(laughs loudly)
Liability? Look at your shoes, Val. Last season’s Louboutins. You’re faking the whole lifestyle.

Julian pulls out his phone with his free hand. He aggressively taps the screen, pulling up a photo gallery. 

He shoves the glowing screen inches from her face.

Swipe. A photo of the basement incinerator.

Swipe. A photo of a bloody Rolex sitting on the concrete.

Swipe. A macro shot of raw, uncut diamonds glittering in the ash trap.

JULIAN
My followers would eat this up. 'Rogue realtor’s secret side hustle.' It’s very true-crime. Very viral.

Valerie’s polished facade splinters. Her breathing turns shallow, feral. She looks at the phone, then up at Julian's smug, punchable face.

She is cornered. The desperation bleeds through her eyes.

VALERIE
You have absolutely zero understanding of the asset class you're looking at. The overhead on this property is... complex.

Julian’s smile tightens. He hates the corporate speak. He aggressively checks a notification on his phone, avoiding her gaze for a split second, before snapping his attention back to her.

JULIAN
I don't care about your overhead.

He shoves the phone in his pocket. He raises his hand between them. 

He spreads his fingers wide. Five digits.

JULIAN
Fifty.

Valerie stares at his hand. 

VALERIE
Fifty percent of the gross yield? You couldn't handle the tax implications, let alone the operational logistics of the cleanup.

Julian presses his forearm against her collarbone, pinning her harder against the mahogany.

JULIAN
I'm the co-listing agent now. Period. We split the rocks, we split the commissions. Or my followers get a very exclusive house tour.

Valerie’s mind races. The house is hungry. It’s always hungry. 

She looks at Julian. Really looks at him. The gin. The unearned bravado. The absolute, staggering uselessness of his existence.

Her feral panic slowly smooths out into something much colder. A shark recognizing chum in the water.

VALERIE
(softly)
You really want a piece of this property, Julian?

JULIAN
I want half.

Valerie straightens her blazer, ignoring the forearm pressing into her chest. Her eyes are dead and black.

VALERIE
Fine. Let me show you the cellar. That’s where the real value add is.

Julian lowers his arm, smirking. He adjusts his jacket. He thinks he won.

VALERIE
Right this way.

She steps past him, leading him deeper into the dark belly of the hallway.

scenes.narrative_action: Before Valerie can answer, the ambient recessed lighting flickers and shifts to a sickly, pulsating crimson. The pristine white drywall directly behind Julian ripples like a bruised stomach. A wet, fleshy fissure tears open in the wall, violently yanking Julian backward by his blazer. He screams, dropping his phone, as the drywall seamlessly seals over his face. A synchronized, ear-splitting slam echoes through the house as thick, bone-like deadbolts shoot across every door and window.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The sequence turn. The house drops its disguise, eliminates the blackmailer, and traps everyone inside.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Julian shoves his glowing iPhone screen inches from Valerie's face. 

JULIAN
So what's the play, Val? Because the math isn't mathing. You go from zero active listings to gatekeeping the most viral piece of architecture in the hills? 

Valerie doesn't flinch at the screen. Her posture remains rigid, a perfectly tailored statue of corporate composure.

VALERIE
Julian, let's talk ROI. You want exposure, I want discretion for my ultra-high-net-worth clients. We can pivot this into a mutual value-add. A co-listing, perhaps.

JULIAN
(scoffs)
Corporate word salad. Love that for you. But I'm looking at the basement footage right now. And honestly? The vibes are atrocious.

Julian taps the screen, looping a grainy, dark video. He smirks, adjusting the lapels of his overly expensive blazer.

JULIAN
You're not crushing it. You're a fraud. And my followers are dying for a house tour. I think I'm gonna give them the exclusive. Right now. Live.

VALERIE
I wouldn't do that. 

JULIAN
Why? Can't afford the bad press?

Valerie opens her mouth to answer--

The ambient recessed lighting overhead FLICKERS. 

A low, sub-audible hum vibrates through the hardwood floorboards. The crisp, warm white lights shift, bleeding into a sickly, pulsating crimson. 

JULIAN
Smart home glitch? Or did you forget to pay the electric bill?

Valerie's polished veneer cracks. Her eyes dart past Julian, locking onto the pristine white drywall directly behind his head. 

VALERIE
Julian. Step away from the wall.

JULIAN
Nice try. Deflection is so last year.

Behind him, the drywall RIPPLES. 

The solid surface softens, undulating inward and outward like a bruised stomach digesting a heavy meal. The paint stretches, revealing dark, pulsing veins beneath the plaster.

VALERIE
I am offering you a twenty percent referral fee to walk out the front door. Right now.

JULIAN
(laughs loud)
Twenty percent? Try a hundred. Plus an apology on your grid. 

A wet, fleshy FISSURE tears open in the center of the rippling wall. 

Viscous, clear fluid drips from the gap, hissing as it hits the imported baseboards. The smell of raw copper floods the hallway.

Julian finally registers Valerie's feral, panicked stare. His bravado falters. He aggressively checks his phone, avoiding her eyes.

JULIAN
Whatever. I'm going live. You're done--

The fissure LUNGES.

A mass of wet, muscular tissue clamps onto the back of Julian's blazer. 

With a violent, bone-jarring YANK, the wall pulls him backward.

Julian SCREAMS. A raw, uncurated shriek of absolute terror. 

His phone slips from his manicured fingers, clattering onto the floorboards. 

He claws desperately at the smooth hardwood, his fingernails splintering. The house is infinitely stronger. It drags him backward, sinking his body into the architecture.

VALERIE
(stepping back)
No, no, no--

The drywall seamlessly seals over Julian's face. 

His scream is instantly muffled, replaced by a wet, sickening CRUNCH from deep within the framing. The wall flattens out, returning to a pristine, flawless white. 

Only the crimson emergency lights remain, pulsing like a slow heartbeat.

Valerie stands paralyzed, her breath ragged. She stares at the empty space where Julian stood. The ultimate problem solver, completely out of options.

She looks down at Julian's phone. Still glowing. Still recording.

She lunges for it.

SLAM.

A synchronized, ear-splitting echo detonates through the house. 

Valerie spins toward the front entrance. 

Thick, bone-like deadbolts SHOOT across the massive mahogany front door. They calcify instantly, fusing the door to the frame.

SLAM. SLAM. SLAM.

Down the length of the hallway, the same skeletal deadbolts erupt from the window frames, locking every pane of glass in a cage of marrow and steel.

The house is sealed. The meal is over. The trap is set.

scenes.narrative_action: Absolute chaos. A group of embezzling tech billionaires scramble over each other as the massive white sectional sofa liquefies into a fleshy, tooth-filled pit, swallowing three of them whole. Valerie casually sidesteps a snapping floorboard that tries to bite a fleeing guest's ankle. She strides through the blood-soaked carnage with bulletproof arrogance, adjusting her crimson gown and expecting absolute immunity from the slaughter.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates the house's terrifying new form while showcasing Valerie's sociopathic delusion that she is safe from the monster she feeds.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Pandemonium. The minimalist, open-concept living room is a slaughterhouse in motion. 

Half a dozen TECH BILLIONAIRES in bespoke tuxedos and silk dresses scramble like rats in a maze. The architecture itself is hunting them.

DANE (40s, tech-vest over a Brioni shirt) furiously taps his smartwatch. 

BAXTER and CHLOE frantically throw decorative marble vases at the reinforced smart-glass windows. The glass doesn't even scratch.

DANE
There's no signal! The house is jamming the Wi-Fi!

BAXTER
It's an architectural dead zone! Chloe, help me with the credenza!

CHLOE
(weeping)
I have a board meeting at eight!

They rush the massive, pristine white sectional sofa, intending to climb it to reach the skylight.

Dane, Baxter, and Chloe scramble onto the cushions. 

The Italian leather ripples beneath their bare feet. It loses its rigid, geometric shape, liquefying into a grotesque, fleshy membrane.

Dane sinks up to his knees.

DANE
What is this? Memory foam?!

The cushions split open. Rows of jagged, translucent teeth erupt from the seams. 

It's not a sofa anymore. It's a digestive tract.

Baxter screams, clawing at the armrest. The armrest folds over, wrapping around his torso like a muscular tongue.

BAXTER
Help! My equity--!

With a wet, violent CRUNCH, the sofa folds inward. The three billionaires are pulled into the depths of the upholstery.

A geyser of arterial blood paints the stark white walls. 

A sickening GULP echoes through the room. 

Then, silence. 

The sofa burps, reshaping itself back into a perfect, mid-century modern sectional. Not a single drop of blood remains on the white leather.

VALERIE VANCE strides into the room. 

She wears a breathtaking crimson gown. Bulletproof posture. She holds a crystal flute of sparkling water, entirely unfazed by the fresh blood dripping from the abstract art.

She taps her earpiece.

VALERIE
Toby? We have a slight bottleneck in the living room.

TOBY (V.O.)
(filtered, panicked)
Valerie, the biometric locks engaged! The entire C-suite of OmniCorp is trapped in the solarium!

VALERIE
Don't use the word 'trapped,' Toby. They're a captive audience. Pivot the remaining guests toward the atrium. And for god's sake, refresh the caviar station.

A panicked GUEST (50s, torn tuxedo) crawls toward Valerie, leaving a smear of blood on the Brazilian hardwood.

GUEST
Call... call a medevac... my leg...

Valerie steps over him, checking her manicured nails.

VALERIE
I really can't authorize that. The optics of a helicopter extraction on a premier listing? It completely tanks the neighborhood comps.

GUEST
Are you insane?! It ate Dane!

VALERIE
Dane was over-leveraged. The market is just correcting itself.

A wide plank of hardwood suddenly snaps upward like a bear trap, lunging for the crawling Guest's ankle.

Valerie casually sidesteps the snapping wood with the grace of a matador. She doesn't even spill her sparkling water.

The floorboard clamps down on the Guest's leg.

GUEST
Oh god! OH GOD!

He is dragged violently beneath the floorboards. His fingernails leave deep scratches in the wax finish before he disappears entirely.

The wood snaps back into place. Seamless.

Valerie sighs, adjusting the neckline of her crimson gown. She looks at the scratches on the floor.

VALERIE
Toby, make a note. We need a buffer for the hardwood.

She walks toward the fireplace. The marble hearth is breathing. Slow, rhythmic expansions, like the ribcage of a sleeping leviathan.

VALERIE
(to the house)
We discussed this. High net-worth individuals only. You don't just gorge on the seed capital, you let it appreciate.

The house groans. A low, guttural vibration that rattles the Baccarat chandelier. It sounds hungry. It sounds angry.

Valerie doesn't flinch. She stands tall, radiating absolute authority. She genuinely believes she is the one holding the leash.

A loud CLINK. 

Valerie turns. 

Where the sofa just digested the tech billionaires, three small, crystalline objects drop from the ceiling vent. 

They bounce off the glass coffee table and roll to a stop.

Valerie approaches. 

Three flawless, ten-carat diamonds. Pristine. Catching the ambient light and refracting it into tiny rainbows across the blood-spattered walls.

She picks one up, rolling it between her thumb and forefinger. She smiles, a cold, feral stretch of the lips.

VALERIE
Now that is a value add.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's confident stride halts abruptly. Above a cowering Richard Sterling, fleshy, muscle-like tendrils whip down from the HVAC vents. Instead of grabbing the corrupt boss, the tendrils snatch up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. Silver trays crash to the marble floor as innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling. Valerie's arrogant smile vanishes, replaced by sheer, paralyzed horror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Shatters Valerie's illusion of control (All Is Lost). The monster mutates from a tool of vigilante justice into an indiscriminate, gluttonous beast.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie’s Jimmy Choos strike the marble with the rhythm of a ticking metronome. Her posture is flawless. A shark swimming through her own custom-built aquarium.

At the far end of the mahogany dining table, RICHARD STERLING backs away. His bespoke suit is immaculate, but his chest heaves. Trapped.

VALERIE
(smooth)
You always said real estate is about location, Richard. I’d argue it’s about timing the market. And right now? Your equity is severely overvalued.

RICHARD
(straightening his tie)
A hostile takeover. Cute. But you lack the killer instinct, Valerie. You’re a facilitator. You don't have the stomach for the apex.

A low, seismic RUMBLE vibrates through the floorboards. The crystal chandelier clinks like wind chimes in a hurricane. 

Richard freezes. His dead-eyed stare darts to the ceiling.

VALERIE
I think you'll find my new partners are highly motivated. We're doing a complete restructuring. Starting at the top.

The rumble escalates into a guttural, wet GROAN. The walls seem to breathe. 

Above Richard, the massive brass grates of the HVAC vents buckle. Screws ping off the mahogany table like bullets.

Richard’s knees buckle. The fatherly facade shatters. He drops to the floor, curling into a pathetic ball, arms shielding his head. 

Valerie stops her pacing. Her lips curl into a triumphant, arrogant smile. She crosses her arms. Waiting for the merger to complete.

From the dark void of the vents, they descend. 

Thick, fleshy tendrils. Striated with raw muscle. They slither downward, dripping a clear, viscous fluid that sizzles against the polished wood. 

They hover inches above Richard’s cowering back. 

Valerie watches, eyes gleaming with vindication. 

VALERIE
(softly)
Liquidate him.

The tendrils twitch. They smell the air. 

Then--they whip right past Richard.

Valerie’s smile falters. 

Across the room, a terrified YOUNG WAITER presses himself against the floral wallpaper, trembling violently. 

The wet THWACK of impact echoes through the room.

A tendril lashes around the waiter’s throat. Another wraps his waist. 

YOUNG WAITER
Wait, no--

He is yanked brutally off his feet. 

His silver tray plummets. CRASH. Champagne flutes explode across the marble floor. 

Valerie gasps, taking a step back. 

VALERIE
No. No, pivot. The target is the broker. The broker!

More vents burst open. A dozen muscular appendages explode from the ceiling. 

They ignore the corrupt billionaire on the floor. They dive for the corners. For the shadows.

A PREP COOK is snatched by the ankle. He claws at the Persian rug, fingernails tearing as he’s dragged backward.

PREP COOK
Help! God, please!
