TOBY ARIS bursts through the swinging kitchen doors, a stack of clipboards in his arms. He freezes. 

A tendril shoots past Toby's ear, grabbing a TEENAGE BUSBOY by the apron. 

TOBY
(voice cracking)
Valerie! What is happening?!

VALERIE
(panicking)
This isn't the agreement! They don't have any net worth! They're zero-value assets!

The house doesn't care. It is starving. 

The Young Waiter is hoisted to the ceiling. The duct is too small for his shoulders. The tendrils pull harder. 

CRACK. 

Collarbones snap. The waiter’s screams are muffled as he is violently stuffed into the dark vents. 

Blood rains down, splattering across Valerie’s pristine white blazer. 

Toby drops his clipboards. He grabs the Prep Cook’s hand, straining with all his weight. 

TOBY
Pull! Just pull!

The tendril flexes. It effortlessly lifts the Prep Cook--and Toby--into the air. 

Toby’s grip slips. He crashes hard onto the marble. The Prep Cook vanishes into the ceiling with a wet, sickening crunch. 

Silence slams back into the room. 

The vents are empty. The staff is gone. Only the dripping of blood and spilled champagne remains.

Toby lies on the floor, hyperventilating, staring at Valerie with absolute, unadulterated horror. 

Valerie cannot speak. Her arrogant smile is completely dead. She stares at her blood-soaked sleeves. The illusion of control, utterly obliterated.

Slowly, Richard uncurls from the floor. He brushes a shard of glass off his lapel. He looks at the ceiling, then at Valerie's terrified face.

RICHARD
(calm, measured)
Fascinating. It seems your new partners don't care about the quality of the capital. Just the volume. 

Richard steps over a pool of blood, entirely unbothered. 

RICHARD
You really should have read the fine print, Valerie.

Valerie stands paralyzed, the weight of the slaughter crushing the breath from her lungs.

scenes.narrative_action: Toby drops his housing binder, backing away in terror as the hallway walls rapidly calcify into jagged, bone-like spikes. The walls begin to slide together on a motorized track of flesh, crushing inward. Toby covers his head, screaming for help as the spiked drywall tears at his suit jacket. From across the foyer, Valerie spots him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The ultimate consequence of Valerie's greed targets the one person she actually cares about, forcing her to act.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS

Toby freezes. His knuckles are white around his thick, leather-bound HOUSING BINDER.

The sleek, modernist drywall to his left shudders. A wet, sickening SQUELCH echoes from behind the pristine paint.

Toby adjusts his glasses. His breathing turns shallow.

TOBY
(muttering)
Just a... settling foundation. Older builds settle. This is new construction, but... temperature fluctuations.

The wall BULGES.

Plaster splits open like a blister. A jagged, bone-white spike erupts through the gap. Then another. And another.

Toby backs away. The binder slips from his sweaty grip. 

It hits the hardwood. Hundreds of affordable housing proposals scatter across the floor.

TOBY
Oh, God. I'm sorry. I'll clean that up.

He kneels, blindly gathering the papers.

The floorboards groan. A low, mechanical hum vibrates through the corridor, but it isn't machinery. It is the sound of grinding cartilage.

Toby looks up.

The baseboards peel upward, revealing a glistening, motorized track of raw, pulsing flesh. The walls begin to slide.

They are closing in.

Toby scrambles backward. His shoulder brushes the left wall. A bone-spike hooks the fabric of his cheap suit jacket.

It TEARS through the polyester, biting into his shirt.

TOBY
Help! Somebody, please!

He thrashes, but the spike holds him fast. The opposite wall inches closer. It is a wall of jagged calcium and drywall, hungry and accelerating.

TOBY
(screaming)
I'm sorry! I'm not supposed to be in this wing!

EXTREME ANGLE - FROM THE FOYER

VALERIE steps into the archway. She holds a crystal champagne flute and a digital tablet. Her posture is rigid, perfect.

She spots Toby in the narrowing hallway.

VALERIE
Toby, we talked about wandering. The West corridor is entirely off-market right now. It's an active liability.

Toby covers his head with his free arm. The walls are three feet apart. Two feet.

TOBY
Valerie! It's eating my jacket! I can't-- I'm stuck, I dropped the proposals and it just started--

Valerie’s eyes dart from Toby to the walls. She sees the pulsing flesh tracks. She sees the bone-spikes glistening with saliva.

Her perfect veneer shatters. The champagne flute slips from her fingers, shattering on the marble.

Valerie sprints to the threshold.

VALERIE
Take the jacket off! Toby, strip the asset and pivot!

TOBY
It's caught on my suspenders! I can't reach the clasp!

The walls are eighteen inches apart. The spikes scrape against each other, showering the floor with white dust.

Valerie lunges into the corridor. She doesn't hesitate. She grabs Toby by the collar of his shirt.

VALERIE
Look at me. Stop looking at the structural defects and look at me!

Toby is hyperventilating. His glasses sit crooked on his sweaty nose.

TOBY
I just wanted to fix the binder...

VALERIE
I don't care about the binder! You are my assistant! You do not terminate your contract without my approval!

She plants her high heel against the edge of the fleshy track. She pulls with a feral, unpolished grunt.

The spike rips through Toby's suspenders.

Valerie yanks him backward. They tumble out of the corridor and crash onto the foyer's cold marble floor.

Behind them, the corridor walls SLAM together.

The sound is deafening. A crunch of bone and drywall interlocking like teeth. The affordable housing proposals are instantly pulverized into red-stained confetti.

Toby lies on his back, gasping for air. His jacket is gone. His shirt is in ribbons.

He pushes his crooked glasses up the bridge of his nose.

TOBY
I think... I think I need to file a worker's comp claim. If that's okay.

Valerie sits up. Her designer dress is smeared with white dust and a streak of Toby's blood.

She stares at the sealed, spiked wall. Her chest heaves. The transactional mask is completely gone. Only raw, terrified realization remains.

VALERIE
Claim denied, Toby. We're breaking the lease.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie violently kicks off her designer stilettos. She sprints barefoot across the blood-slicked marble, diving headfirst into the closing, spiked corridor. She grabs Toby by the collar of his shirt, using her momentum to violently yank him backward out into the foyer just as the jagged walls slam together with a deafening, powdery crunch.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Valerie takes her first physical action to save someone other than herself, shedding her corporate armor (the shoes).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The foyer breathes. Literally.

Veins of crimson pulse beneath the Calacatta marble floor. A low, guttural grinding echoes from the east wing hallway.

Inside the hall: TOBY (20s). He is on his knees. Not praying. He is frantically gathering scattered client dossiers. His hands shake, smearing wet red fingerprints across glossy cardstock.

The walls on either side of him are no longer drywall. They are jagged, calcified teeth. 

And they are closing in. Fast.

Toby pushes his glasses up his nose.

TOBY
(muttering)
I have the Rostova disclosures. I have them, they just... they slipped.

VALERIE (30s) stands at the heavy mahogany front door. Freedom. Safety. She clutches her Birkin bag like a life preserver.

She looks at the door. Her hand hovers over the brass deadbolt. 

She looks back to Toby.

The grinding intensifies. The hallway shrinks to four feet wide. Plaster dust rains down from the crown molding.

TOBY
(voice cracking)
Valerie? The floorplan is... it's shifting again.

Valerie’s breath hitches. She looks at the exit. Then at the kid. 

Her eyes dart to her own feet. Her Christian Louboutin stilettos. Six inches of weaponized calfskin. Her armor.

She kicks them off. They clatter against the marble.

Valerie drops the Birkin.

She sprints.

Bare feet slap against the wet, blood-slicked stone. She slides, catches her balance, pushes off a Corinthian column.

The hallway is three feet wide. The jagged spikes interlock.

Toby drops a folder. Reaches for it.

TOBY
Just a second, the addendums--

Valerie DIVES.

Headfirst into the collapsing maw.

The air is thick with the smell of copper and plaster dust.

She shoots her hand out. Grabs Toby by the collar of his poly-blend button-down.

VALERIE
Leave the comps!

She uses her forward momentum, planting a bare heel against the closing wall, and YANKS him backward.

They fly out of the corridor.

CRUNCH.

A deafening, powdery explosion of stone and bone.

A cloud of white plaster and red mist blows over them.

Silence. Save for the heavy, rhythmic thud of the house digesting.

Valerie and Toby lie in a tangled heap on the foyer floor. Covered in dust. Panting.

Valerie stares at the sealed wall where the hallway used to be. A feral, wide-eyed panic breaks through her polished mask. Her chest heaves. She just risked her life for a non-revenue-generating asset.

Toby sits up slowly. He pats his pockets. Adjusts his crooked glasses.

TOBY
I'm sorry. I, uh... I think I left the iPad in there. With the master keycodes.

Valerie snaps her head toward him. The feral panic instantly hardens into a transactional glare. She sits up, aggressively brushing plaster from her Chanel skirt.

VALERIE
The iPad is a depreciating asset, Toby. We pivot.

TOBY
Right. Pivot. It's just... the Rostova files were on the local drive. The cloud sync has been spotty since the, um... since the dining room ate Mr. Sterling's assistant.

Valerie gets to her feet. She winces, looking at her bare, blood-stained feet. She hates this. She hates being exposed.

VALERIE
We don't need the Rostova files. The square footage just went down. That hallway is gone. We have to adjust the listing price. Less inventory, higher demand.

Toby stares at her. At her bare feet. At the crushed wall. 

He clears his throat, a nervous, high-pitched sound. He starts picking lint off his sleeve.

TOBY
You dropped your shoes.

Valerie looks back at the Louboutins sitting near the front door.

VALERIE
They were last season. The heel-to-arch ratio was a liability.

She doesn't look him in the eye.

TOBY
Right. Of course. I just... I should probably sweep up the...

He gestures vaguely to the powdery red dust drifting onto the marble.

TOBY
The structural debris. Before the three o'clock showing. If we're still doing that.

Valerie finally looks at him. Really looks at him. He is trembling. She just risked her life for a kid making forty grand a year. It terrifies her.

VALERIE
We are maintaining the schedule. We have an exclusive right-to-sell agreement.

She turns away, marching barefoot across the blood-slicked floor toward her discarded bag.

VALERIE
(over her shoulder)
Get the broom, Toby. Value doesn't sleep.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags Toby up the glass floating stairs, desperately trying to escape the shifting ground floor. Midway up, the architectural glass violently buckles and shatters under their weight. They plummet, slamming hard onto a narrow, trembling landing. Beneath them, the hardwood floor splits wide open, revealing a gaping, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth. Sulfurous smoke billows up, choking them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Physically corners the protagonist and her B-story anchor, removing all avenues of escape for the climax of the sequence.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FLOATING STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

The ground floor is a churning ocean of imported Brazilian walnut. Floorboards snap and curl upward like hungry fingers. 

Valerie Vance claws her way up the architectural floating staircase. Her tailored blazer is torn, but her grip on Toby Aris’s collar is absolute iron. 

She hauls him up another glass step.

VALERIE
Pivot, Toby! We are pivoting to the second level! Up!

TOBY
(stumbling)
I’m sorry, my loafers don't have the tread for this, I should have worn the orthotics--

VALERIE
Leverage! We need higher elevation to assess the asset! Move!

Toby’s foot slips. His left loafer tumbles backward, swallowed instantly by the writhing hardwood below. A sickening CRUNCH echoes up.

Toby gasps. He compulsively wipes a smudge of dirt from his lapel with trembling fingers.

TOBY
Valerie, the foyer just ate my Cole Haan. We can’t-- the structural integrity is entirely compromised!

VALERIE
It’s a cosmetic defect. Easily staged around. Keep climbing!

They reach the midpoint. The stairs here are pure, cantilevered glass. A marvel of modern engineering suspended in thin air.

Valerie steps onto the center pane.

A high-pitched SQUEAL of stressed tempered glass cuts through the roar of the house. 

Spiderweb cracks erupt outward from the stiletto point of Valerie's Prada heel.

She freezes. Toby freezes two steps below her. 

The house goes dead silent. The calm before a foreclosure.

VALERIE
(breath shallow)
It’s settling. High-end new builds always settle. The contractor assured me the load-bearing capacity was--

TOBY
(adjusting glasses)
I’m sorry, but I really think we exceed the maximum tensile strength of--

The glass step violently BUCKLES. 

With a deafening SHATTER, the staircase explodes into razor-sharp confetti.

Gravity rips them down. 

Valerie and Toby plummet through the empty space. They slam brutally onto the mid-level landing--a narrow, suspended slab of Carrara marble. 

The impact knocks the wind from them. 

Above, the remaining glass steps detach and rain down, shattering around them. 

Toby bleeds from a cut on his cheek. He scrambles to his knees, his hands shaking as he instinctively tries to sweep the jagged shards of glass into a neat, organized pile.

TOBY
I’ll clean this up. I’m sorry. I’ll get a broom, I just need to find the utility closet--

VALERIE
Leave it!

Valerie forces herself up. Her polished veneer is violently cracking. Feral panic bleeds into her eyes. 

She looks up. The stairs to the second floor are completely gone. A sheer, unscalable drop. 

She looks down over the edge of the trembling landing.

Beneath them, the foyer’s hardwood floor doesn't just shift. It SPLITS wide open.

A deafening, guttural tear of timber. 

A gaping, cavernous MAW reveals itself in the subflooring. 

Acidic, bioluminescent green light bleeds upward. 

Rows of translucent, jagged teeth--like shattered chandeliers--unfold from the joists, dripping with a viscous, clear sap. 

Sulfurous, yellow smoke billows up from the throat of the house. 

It hits the landing. Toby violently coughs, eyes watering. He clutches his briefcase to his chest like a shield.

TOBY
(coughing)
I’m sorry, Valerie. I didn't update my will. I just have a cat and some student loans, I shouldn't have enabled this--

Valerie chokes on the sulfur. She backs up until her spine hits the cold marble wall. There is nowhere left to climb. 

She stares down into the glowing, acidic throat. Her chest heaves. Her hands grip the edge of the landing, knuckles white.

VALERIE
(voice trembling)
It’s a buyer’s market. Everything is negotiable. We just... we reassess the comps. We offer a concession.

TOBY
(weeping)
It doesn't want a concession, Valerie! It wants us!

VALERIE
I can generate more yield! I am a value add!

The landing violently LURCHES downward. 

The jaws below snap at the empty air, waiting for the drop.

scenes.narrative_action: A deep, guttural vibration shakes the landing, rattling Valerie's teeth. The house physically tilts the landing, trying to slide Toby into the pit. Toby desperately clings to Valerie's crimson gown, his feet dangling directly over the churning acid. Valerie looks down at his terrified, tear-filled eyes. She stops trying to pull him up and just stares, seeing her own monstrous, sociopathic reflection in his gaze. Her grip tightens on his hand, her expression shifting from panic to a hardened, self-loathing resolve.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The profound moral awakening (Dark Night of the Soul). The house offers a literal sacrifice, but Valerie recognizes her own monstrosity and refuses the deal.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FLOATING STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

A deep, guttural VIBRATION. Not an earthquake. Digestion. 

It rattles the tempered glass balustrades, vibrating up through the soles of Valerie's Louboutins. Her teeth chatter against her will. She clings to the chrome handrail, her crimson couture gown soaked in sweat and something viscous.

Below them, the grand foyer floor has irised open. 

A churning vat of luminescent, translucent stomach acid boils where the Italian marble used to be. Uncut diamonds bob to the surface, clinking against the rim like ice in a highball glass. 

The house GROANS. The architectural geometry warps. 

The floating staircase suddenly TILTS. Forty-five degrees.

Toby screams. His cheap loafers lose traction on the polished oak treads. He slides backward toward the drop.

Valerie lunges. Her manicured hands claw into the cheap polyester of his blazer. 

Toby's dead weight yanks her down. She slams her knees into the edge of the landing, sliding inches before her heels catch a brass floor runner. 

Toby dangles directly over the abyss. His hands frantically search for purchase, finding the silk of Valerie's crimson gown. He twists the fabric into a lifeline.

TOBY
(hyperventilating)
Valerie. Valerie, the structural integrity is-- the joists are failing. We need to assess the-- I dropped the iPad.

He looks down. A splash of acid hisses against a fallen crystal chandelier, melting the brass instantly. 

TOBY
I'm sorry. I really can't afford to replace the iPad.

Valerie strains, the tendons in her neck popping. Her knuckles turn white.

VALERIE
Stop talking about the hardware! You're a value-add, Toby. Just-- pivot your weight. Pivot!

The house shudders again. A wet, tearing sound echoes from the drywall. The incline steepens. Fifty degrees.

Valerie slips an inch. The silk of her gown begins to tear under Toby's desperate grip.

TOBY
(adjusting his glasses with a trembling finger)
It's basic math. The counterweight is off. If you don't release the liability, you're going to breach the contract. Both of us go in.

Valerie stares at him. He's giving her permission to drop him. To balance the ledger.

VALERIE
I don't liquidate assets. Grab my wrist.

TOBY
I can't. My palms are sweating. I'm going to ruin the silk, Valerie. It's couture.

The vibration intensifies. A low, rhythmic thrumming. A heartbeat. The house is impatient. It wants its meal. 

Valerie looks down into the pit. The diamonds sparkle in the corrosive sludge. Pristine. Flawless. Millions of dollars, just waiting for a deposit.

Then, she looks back at Toby.

His eyes are wide, bloodshot, and utterly terrified. But there is no judgment in them. Just the sad, beaten acceptance of a boy who has always known he was disposable.

Valerie freezes. The frantic pulling stops. 

She sees it. Reflected in his wet, dilated pupils. 

Not the house. Herself. The sharp, predatory angles of her face. The cold, calculating monster she built to survive. The exact same monster as Richard Sterling. 

Time dilates. The roaring of the house fades into a muffled, distant hum. 

TOBY
(whispering)
It's okay. I'm a bad investment.

A beat. 

The transactional armor shatters. 

Valerie's jaw sets. The feral panic drains from her face, replaced by a terrifying, absolute stillness. A hardened, self-loathing resolve.

VALERIE
The market is closed.

She doesn't let go. She shifts her grip. Skin to skin. 

Her fingernails dig into his wrist, drawing blood. She anchors her bleeding knees against the splintering oak.

VALERIE
You are not a deductible.

She pulls. Not with panic, but with violent, focused rage. Against gravity. Against the house. Against herself.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie violently yanks Toby backward by his cheap lapels, hauling him away from the snapping, translucent teeth of the acidic pit. She stands tall on the trembling landing and screams into the cavernous maw, officially breaking her lease. The house shrieks, a deafening wail of twisting metal, and the remaining staircase collapses into a slide of jagged glass.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Act III turn. Valerie actively chooses human life over wealth, severing her pact with the monster.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FLOATING STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

The floorboards beneath TOBY ARIS (30s) are no longer imported Brazilian teak. They are a pulsating, translucent membrane. 

Below the membrane: a churning vat of bioluminescent acid.

Toby slips. His sensible, rubber-soled loafers find zero traction. He slides toward a widening fissure in the steps. 

Snapping, translucent teeth--like jagged quartz--erupt from the edges of the gap. They gnash at the empty air, dripping a clear, smoking viscous fluid.

Toby scrambles. His fingernails crack and bleed against the glass balustrade.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) stands on the upper landing. Safe.

She watches him slide. The feral, rapid-fire calculus behind her eyes is visible. 

Let him fall. The house gets fed. The escrow closes. 

Toby looks up at her. He adjusts his crooked glasses with a trembling, bloodied hand.

TOBY
I think... I think the foundation is settling, Valerie. I'm sorry, I didn't file the updated seismic--

Valerie's jaw tightens. The veneer cracks.

She lunges. 

Valerie grabs Toby by the lapels of his cheap, off-the-rack polyester suit. Her designer heels dig violently into the vibrating landing. 

She doesn't ask if he's okay. She just pulls. 

With a guttural, un-corporate grunt, Valerie yanks him backward. 

They collapse onto the marble landing just as the quartz teeth snap shut on the space Toby's legs occupied. The jaws click with the force of a bear trap.

Toby hyperventilates. He scrambles backward, furiously brushing non-existent lint off his torn sleeves.

TOBY
I ruined your blazer. The silk blend. I'm so sorry, I can get that dry-cleaned, I know a guy in the Valley who does miracles with blood--

VALERIE
Shut up, Toby.

Valerie stares at her hands. Her perfect, French-tipped nails are chipped to the nailbed. Ruined.

The house rumbles. A deep, seismic stomach growl beneath the floorboards. It's hungry. And it's pissed.

The hallway sconces bleed a sickly, dimming gold. The ambient temperature plummets. 

VALERIE
(whispering)
Negative ROI.

TOBY
What? Valerie, the structural integrity is severely compromised, the lateral load is--

Valerie stands up. 

She violently smooths down her ruined blazer. She rolls her shoulders back. The polished, untouchable broker returns. 

She steps right to the edge of the trembling precipice. 

VALERIE
Hey!

The house goes dead silent. The acid stops churning. The walls seem to hold their breath.

Valerie glares down into the cavernous, shifting maw.

VALERIE
We need to discuss the terms of our arrangement.

The massive crystal chandelier above them sways. Teardrop prisms clink together like warning bells.

VALERIE
The market has shifted. You're over-leveraged. You want way too much capital upfront and frankly... the maintenance is a nightmare.

A low, mechanical whine builds in the walls. The drywall begins to blister. The house does not like being audited.

TOBY
Valerie, please step back from the load-bearing--

VALERIE
I'm pivoting!

Her voice cracks like a whip, echoing off the vaulted ceilings.

VALERIE
This asset is toxic. I'm liquidating.

She reaches into her pocket. Pulls out the heavy, brass master key. The one bearing the property's arrogant gold crest. 

She holds it out over the abyss.

VALERIE
Consider the lease broken.

She drops the key. 

It tumbles into the dark. Swallowed by the acid pit with a tiny, pathetic hiss.

A beat of absolute silence.

Then--

The house SHRIEKS.

A deafening, unnatural wail of twisting steel and tearing insulation. The sound of a dying, furious god.

The walls buckle inward. The gold light violently shifts to a bruised, pulsing purple.

Valerie grabs Toby's collar.

VALERIE
Move.

The floating staircase groans. The high-tensile steel support cables snap like over-tightened piano strings. They whip through the air, slicing deep into the plaster.

The remaining steps collapse entirely. 

It's an avalanche of splintering oak and shattering balustrades. The elegant architecture transforms into a sheer, deadly slide of jagged glass.

Valerie and Toby are thrown backward against the heavy landing doors as the floor drops out into the roaring, hungry void below.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie grabs Toby's hand and sprints through the collapsing foyer. She dodges snapping floorboards and raining crystal chandeliers. Relying on her obsessive knowledge of the floor plan, she physically yanks Toby out of the path of a collapsing column and steers him toward the narrow, unglamorous servant's corridors.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions the protagonist from passive survivor to active leader, utilizing her previously selfish real estate knowledge for survival.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The mahogany double doors of the drawing room splinter outward. VALERIE bursts through, dragging TOBY by the wrist. 

The grand foyer is a digestive tract of shifting mid-century modern geometry. Italian marble floor tiles buckle. Grout hisses like stomach acid. Hardwood planks beneath the Persian rugs snap upward like bear traps.

TOBY
I'm sorry, I'm so sorry, I left the master keys in the conservatory, if I had just grabbed the lanyard--

VALERIE
The locks are melting, Toby. The hardware is literally digesting. Keep moving.

Above them, the grand crystal chandelier groans. The thick brass chain twists, torqued by unseen muscle in the ceiling.

Toby looks up, freezing in his tracks. He pushes his glasses up his nose, his breathing shallow and rapid.

TOBY
That's a Baccarat. That's a three-hundred-pound Baccarat.

The brass chain SNAPS. A rain of razor-sharp crystal plummets.

Valerie yanks his arm hard. The chandelier detonates against the marble right where Toby just stood. Shards spray outward like shrapnel, slicing the fabric of Valerie's pristine blazer.

VALERIE
Depreciated asset. Pivot, Toby. We need a new exit strategy.

Toby points a shaking finger toward the massive front doors. The vaulted entryway. Sunlight peeks through the frosted glass.

TOBY
The portico! If we just vault the credenza, I think the latch is still, maybe, if I push--

VALERIE
No. Look at the crown molding.

Toby squints through the choking plaster dust. The walls are physically inching closer together. The custom crown molding compresses, groaning under immense, deliberate pressure. 

VALERIE
It's a funnel. It wants us at the front door. It's herding the traffic flow.

TOBY
Traffic flow? Valerie, it's trying to eat us!

VALERIE
And I am telling you the curb appeal is a trap.

A deafening CRACK echoes over the chaos. The central load-bearing column--solid Corinthian marble--fractures at the base. Spiderwebs of fissure lines shoot up the stone.

It tips. The sheer mass of it displaces the air as it falls directly toward them.

Toby scrambles backward, slipping on the pulverized crystal. He goes down hard, his knee slamming into the buckling floorboards.

TOBY
I can't-- my ankle, I think I rolled it, just go, Valerie, just--

Valerie doesn't hesitate. She grabs him by the collar of his cheap suit, hauling him up with a feral, uncharacteristic strength. Her polished veneer is entirely gone.

VALERIE
I am not taking a loss on you today. Up!

She drags him sideways. The marble column SLAMS into the floor, obliterating the space they occupied a microsecond ago. The impact vibrates violently through their teeth. 

Dust chokes the air. Valerie coughs, wiping a smear of blood from her cheek. Her eyes dart around the shifting architecture. Calculating.

VALERIE
The original blueprints. 1924 build. Before the Sterling renovation.

TOBY
(wheezing)
What?

VALERIE
Sterling gutted the interior walls. Added the open-concept footprint to maximize natural light. 

TOBY
So we're dead! We're in an open floor plan!

VALERIE
But he didn't touch the original bones. There's zero ROI in upgrading the servant's quarters. They never touched the service routes.

She zeroes in on a blank, unadorned stretch of oak paneling beneath the grand staircase. No handles. No trim. 

Valerie sprints for the paneling. Toby limps after her, glancing back at the front doors. The vaulted entryway is now a crushing, jagged maw of splintered wood and drywall, sealing shut.

TOBY
Valerie, there's no door there!

Valerie reaches the flat oak panel. She runs her hands frantically over the grain, her manicured nails chipping and bleeding against the rough wood.

VALERIE
It's a flush mount. Pre-war staff entrance. They hid the help to maintain the aesthetic.

The floor beneath them tilts. A steep, twenty-degree drop. Toby slides, desperately clawing at the wall to stay upright.

Her fingers find a microscopic seam in the woodwork. She digs her bloody nails in and PULLS.

The panel pops open. A narrow, pitch-black corridor is revealed. It smells of old dust and stagnant air. No luxury. Just raw, exposed framing.

The mansion lets out a guttural, structural roar. The foyer ceiling begins to cave in entirely.

VALERIE
Get in the walls!

She shoves Toby into the dark passage, diving in right behind him. 

SLAM. 

Valerie pulls the panel shut just as the foyer collapses into a mountain of rubble.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Toby slide into the kitchen, finding terrified, blood-spattered catering staff huddled behind a shattered marble island. The walls secrete a corrosive acid, melting the stainless steel double ovens. Valerie barks orders, her sociopathic arrogance replaced by protective fury, physically herding the weeping staff toward the pantry.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates Valerie's completed character arc; she is now risking her life to save the help she previously ignored.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

VALERIE and TOBY slide across the slick herringbone floor, crashing through the swinging double doors.

The culinary masterpiece is digesting itself.

Viscous, yellow acid weeps from the grout of the pristine subway tiles. The twin Sub-Zero refrigerators blister, their stainless steel skins bubbling like burnt sugar.

A massive double oven groans. The metal hinges melt into liquid slag. The heavy glass doors shatter outward.

Toby flinches, shielding his face. He frantically adjusts his crooked glasses.

TOBY
(panting)
I’m sorry, I’m so sorry, I told the valet to hold the cars, but the driveway just-- it swallowed the gravel, Valerie. The whole driveway.

Valerie ignores him, her eyes scanning the smoke-filled room.

Behind the shattered remnants of a Calacatta marble island, three CATERERS huddle in a knot. Their crisp white uniforms are heavily spattered with arterial red.

A YOUNG WAITER rocks back and forth, clutching a bent silver serving tray. Next to him, a LINE COOK weeps silently into her hands.

Acid hisses against the back of the marble island. The stone is dissolving.

Valerie stares at the exit doors behind her. The hallway is clear. She could run. 

She looks back at the weeping staff. Her jaw tightens. The sociopathic sheen vanishes, replaced by a feral, protective fury.

VALERIE
Toby. Stop apologizing.

TOBY
I can't, it's just a massive liability nightmare, the insurance won't cover acts of-- of digestion, we need to--

VALERIE
I need a value add! Right now. The pantry. Is it reinforced?

Toby blinks, his brain struggling to catch up. He violently cleans a smudge off his glasses with his thumb.

TOBY
The walk-in? Yes. Industrial steel. Airtight seal. But it's on the other side of the prep station.

Between them and the pantry: a gauntlet of melting copper pots and dripping ceiling beams.

Valerie strips off her bespoke Tom Ford blazer. She wraps it around her forearm like a shield.

VALERIE
Hey!

The caterers don't move. The Young Waiter just whimpers.

Valerie stalks across the kitchen, dodging a glob of acid that burns a hole straight through the floorboards. 

She slides behind the island, dropping to a crouch beside the terrified staff.

VALERIE
Look at me.

The Line Cook peers up, eyes wide with terror.

VALERIE
The market conditions have shifted. We are liquidating this position. You are going to stand up, and we are going to pivot to the pantry.

YOUNG WAITER
(sobbing)
Mr. Sterling... he pushed Chef into the garbage disposal... the sink just opened up and--

Valerie grabs the Waiter by the collar of his blood-soaked shirt. Hard.

VALERIE
Sterling is bankrupt. I am your broker now. And I am not taking a loss on you today. Do you understand?

The Waiter stares at her. Nods weakly.

Above them, a wooden ceiling beam splinters. A torrent of yellow acid prepares to breach.

VALERIE
Move!

Valerie hauls the Waiter to his feet. She shoves the Line Cook forward.

They sprint.

A jet of acid sprays from the melting espresso machine. Valerie throws herself in front of the Line Cook, taking the splash on her wrapped forearm.

The designer fabric hisses and dissolves. Valerie doesn't even flinch.

TOBY
(waving frantically)
Over here! Come on, come on!

Toby wrestles with the heavy steel handle of the walk-in pantry. It unseals with a heavy hiss.

Valerie physically throws the Waiter and the Line Cook inside. 

The third caterer trips on a melted floor mat.

The ceiling beam snaps.

Valerie grabs the fallen caterer by the belt, violently yanking him across the slick floor just as a massive chunk of acid-soaked plaster crashes down where his head was.

She hurls him into the darkness of the pantry.

Valerie backs up to the steel door. She looks at the kitchen one last time.

The marble island collapses entirely into a bubbling pool of stomach acid.

Valerie steps into the pantry.

TOBY
Got it!

Toby slams the heavy steel door shut. The deadbolt clicks into place.

The muffled, wet roars of the house echo uselessly against the thick metal.

scenes.narrative_action: The entity roars, sending fleshy, muscular tendrils whipping down from the ceiling vents. Valerie kicks down a reinforced wooden door leading to the subterranean mechanical room. She shoves the last caterer through the frame just as a tendril shoots forward. Valerie slams the heavy steel door shut, severing the tip of the tendril in the jamb.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates the monster's pursuit and traps the survivors in the final confrontation arena.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION PANTRY - CONTINUOUS

The walls BREATHE. A low, sub-bass rumble vibrates through the floorboards, shaking a towering rack of imported Beluga caviar. Glass jars vibrate off the edge. Shatter.

VALERIE VANCE backs into the narrow, claustrophobic aisle. Her thousand-dollar blazer is torn, smeared with plaster and something that smells distinctly like stomach acid. 

Right behind her is RICHARD STERLING. Not a hair out of place, though his dead-eyed stare is stretched wide. He grips a terrified, teenage CATERER by the collar. Using the boy as a physical buffer.

RICHARD
(adjusting his cuffs)
A complete failure of due diligence, Valerie. The liability exposure here is astronomical.

VALERIE
We are liquidating our position in this room, Richard. Pivot. Now.

Above them, the HVAC vents bulge inward. The aluminum grating groans under immense, unnatural weight. Screws pop from the drywall like bullets.

Then--a deafening ROAR. 

It’s not just a sound, it’s a violent shift in atmospheric pressure. The vent covers blow out completely.

Fleshy, muscular TENDRILS whip down from the ceiling. They are thick as pythons, pulsing with dark veins, glistening with digestive bile. Predatory umbilical cords. 

One tendril lashes sideways, wrapping around a hanging rack of cured ibérico ham. It squeezes. The meat and steel rack are instantly pulverized into a fine red paste.

The Caterer whimpers, paralyzed.

RICHARD
Apex predators, Valerie. They don't negotiate. They simply correct the market. 

Valerie’s eyes dart wildly. Calculating. Scanning the dead end of the pantry. 

Behind a fallen wine rack: a reinforced wooden door. A faded brass plaque reads: SUBTERRANEAN MECHANICAL. 

Valerie vaults over the shattered wine bottles. She hits the door shoulder-first. Locked. 

Above, three more tendrils slither from the vent, tasting the air. They snap toward the sound of her impact.

VALERIE
(kicking the deadbolt)
Depreciate. This. Asset!

She delivers a brutal, feral kick to the lock. The heavy wood splinters. The door kicks inward, revealing a pitch-black stairwell and an inner fire-door. 

The tendrils rear back, muscles coiling like springs. 

Richard shoves the frozen Caterer toward the center of the room. A calculated distraction. 

RICHARD
Natural order, kid.

Valerie catches the boy's arm before he stumbles into the strike zone. Her transactional mask shatters. Pure, raw humanity bleeds through the cracks. 

VALERIE
Your life has value! Move!

She hurls the Caterer through the splintered frame, sending him tumbling down into the dark mechanical room. 

A massive tendril SHOOTS forward. A blur of wet, muscular violence aimed straight for the doorway. 

Valerie grabs the heavy steel edge of the inner fire-door. She uses her entire body weight to swing it on its massive industrial hinges. 

SLAM. 

The heavy steel door crushes shut. 

It catches the tip of the lunging tendril perfectly in the jamb. 

A sickening CRUNCH of cartilage. Purple, acidic ichor bursts across the doorframe. The severed tip of the appendage drops to the floor, twitching wildly in a puddle of truffle oil.

Valerie throws the heavy deadbolt. Leans her forehead against the cold steel. 

Silence, except for the wet, heavy thuds of the entity battering the other side.

scenes.narrative_action: In the dim, vibrating basement, Valerie directs the staff toward a rusted ventilation shaft. Toby boosts the servers up into the narrow chute. As Valerie turns to follow, a heavy iron pipe swings out of the shadows, smashing into her ribs. She collapses to the concrete. Richard Sterling steps into the flickering light, covered in blood but grinning with manic greed.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the final physical obstacle. The antagonist returns, embodying the unkillable nature of corporate greed.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The belly of the beast. Stifling hot. The concrete floor VIBRATES with a deep, gastric hum. Copper pipes overhead groan, dripping condensation like sweat.

VALERIE VANCE shoves a terrified CATERING WAITER toward a rusted ventilation shaft high on the back wall. 

TOBY ARIS is on his hands and knees, covered in plaster dust. He frantically stacks two empty caviar crates, forming a makeshift step-stool.

VALERIE
Pivot! Up the chute, let's go. We are liquidating this asset, move!

TOBY
Sorry, just step on the crate, it's fine, watch your head on the flange--

The Waiter scrambles into the dark shaft, kicking Toby in the shoulder on the way up. Toby winces, adjusting his crooked glasses.

TOBY
That's the last of the independent contractors. I think. I mean, assuming the coat check girl didn't... you know. I should probably cross-reference the guest list--

Toby starts patting his dust-covered pockets, looking for a pen.

VALERIE
The ledger is irrelevant, Toby. The overhead is gone. Get in the vent.

Toby hesitates. He looks at the pulsing, fleshy mortar between the cinderblocks. He wipes a smudge of dirt off his lapel, avoiding her gaze.

TOBY
I just... I feel like we're leaving a massive liability downstairs. The councilwoman was technically our invitee.

Valerie grabs him by the collar. Her polished veneer cracks, revealing pure, feral desperation. 

VALERIE
I am streamlining the exit strategy. Up.

Toby scrambles onto the crates. He pulls himself into the lip of the rusted shaft. He reaches down, extending a trembling hand.

TOBY
Okay. Come on. Give me your hand.

Valerie reaches up--

SWOOSH.

A heavy IRON PIPE swings violently out of the blind spot. 

CRACK. The pipe catches Valerie square in the ribs.

She drops to the concrete. A breathless, agonizing gasp escapes her lungs. She clutches her side, scrambling backward on her elbows like a cornered animal.

Footsteps. Slow. Deliberate.

RICHARD STERLING steps into the flickering fluorescent light. 

His bespoke Brioni suit is soaked in fresh, dark blood. He drags the iron pipe behind him. He calmly adjusts a ruined French cuff with his free hand. 

He is grinning. A serene, manic smile.

RICHARD
You know, Valerie... I always admired your aggressive restructuring. But a mass exodus? That's just terrible retention.

Valerie spits a wad of blood onto the concrete. Her eyes dart around the room. Calculating.

VALERIE
Richard. The structural integrity is compromised. We need to vacate the premises before the property value zeroes out.

RICHARD
Zeroes out?

Richard chuckles. A warm, fatherly sound that doesn't reach his dead eyes. He steps closer. The pipe SCRAPES along the floor.

RICHARD
Oh, Valerie. You're stuck in the micro. You have to look at the macro. This property is the ultimate apex predator. It consumes the weak, it concentrates the capital. It is the purest free market I've ever seen.

Toby's pale face peers out from the vent, terrified.

TOBY
Mr. Sterling? Oh god, you're bleeding. We should... I have gauze in the HR kit, if we just file an incident report--

RICHARD
(without looking up)
Quiet, Toby. The adults are discussing a merger.

Valerie tries to push herself up. Blinding pain shoots through her ribs. She clutches a rusted water valve, hauling herself to her knees.

VALERIE
It's a bubble, Richard. It's eating the clientele. There's no ROI if the buyers are digested!

RICHARD
They aren't dead, Valerie. They've been liquidated. Converted into hard, flawless assets. 

Richard stops three feet from her. He looks up at the ceiling. The house RUMBLES beneath them, a massive, hungry roar. 

RICHARD
And frankly... you've been hoarding the dividends.

Richard raises the iron pipe with both hands. His smile stretches, impossibly wide.

RICHARD
The board demands a sacrifice. And I am nothing if not a fiduciary.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard grabs Valerie by her torn crimson gown, gleefully attempting to drag her toward the buckling basement door to offer her to the house. A brutal fistfight erupts. Valerie dodges a wild swing from the iron pipe, drives her bare knee into Richard's stomach, and slams his head against a vibrating water heater.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The physical manifestation of Valerie battling her own former ideology and the toxic mentor who shaped her.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The belly of the beast. 

Thick copper pipes throb along the low ceiling with a sickening, biological pulse. A massive industrial water heater shrieks, vibrating hard enough to rattle the concrete foundation.

At the far end of the narrow corridor, the heavy steel BASEMENT DOOR buckles inward. 

BOOM. 

Something massive throws its weight against the other side. The metal groans. The house is starving.

VALERIE scrambles backward, her bare feet slipping on slick, oily condensation. Her once-immaculate crimson gown hangs in ragged strips. She looks less like a polished executive and more like cornered prey.

RICHARD steps into the flickering fluorescent light. 

His bespoke Tom Ford suit is flawless. Not a single hair out of place. He steps over a ruptured steam valve with the casual, strolling grace of a man touring a summer home.

RICHARD
(adjusting his cuffs)
It’s a simple market correction, Valerie. The natural order asserting itself.

Valerie scrambles for a handhold, her fingers slick with grease. 

VALERIE
I hold the exclusive listing! You are trespassing on my territory!

Richard lunges. Faster than a man his age should be. 

His manicured hand twists violently into the torn fabric of her crimson gown. He yanks her across the concrete.

VALERIE
(gasping)
Let go of my inventory!

She claws at his wrist. Her acrylic nails dig deep into his skin, carving red tracks. 

Richard doesn't flinch. His dead-eyed stare remains fixed on the buckling basement door. He drags her effortlessly toward the splintering steel.

RICHARD
You always lacked the killer instinct. My father saw it in me, and I see it in you. Empathy. It’s a terrible, terminal inefficiency.

Valerie digs her bare heels into a rusted floor drain, fighting for an inch of traction. The feral panic sets in. Her polished corporate veneer shatters completely.

VALERIE
I generated a four-hundred percent ROI this quarter! I am the principal stakeholder!

RICHARD
And now, you are capital. 

He drags her another two feet. The heat in the room is oppressive. Steam billows around their ankles.

RICHARD
(cont'd)
The property requires a deposit. I’m simply closing the escrow.

BOOM. 

The basement door bows violently. A massive dent blooms in the center of the steel. The hinges scream, popping rivets onto the wet floor. 

Valerie kicks out, her heel catching Richard in the shin. He barely registers the impact. 

VALERIE
We can pivot! Richard, listen to me, we can source a new demographic! We can scale!

RICHARD
(smiling, paternal)
Why outsource when we have everything we need in-house?

Richard reaches into the shadows of a utility rack. His hand closes around a heavy, rusted IRON PIPE. 

He tests its weight. A gentle, approving nod.

RICHARD
(cont'd)
Try to remain still. Volatility only damages the asset.

He raises the pipe. 

Valerie stops pulling. She stares at the rusted iron. The desperate panic in her eyes hardens into something cold. Something final.

Richard swings the iron pipe in a vicious, horizontal arc aimed squarely at her skull.

Valerie drops. 

The pipe whistles mere millimeters over her head. It smashes into a concrete pillar with a deafening CLANG. 

Sparks shower the wet floor. The kinetic shockwave travels straight up Richard's arm. 

For the first time, his iron grip loosens. 

Valerie doesn't hesitate. She drives her bare knee upward with every ounce of leverage she has left.

A sickening crunch. 

The kneecap sinks deep into Richard’s stomach. All the air leaves his lungs in a sharp, undignified hiss. 

His impeccable posture folds. The iron pipe clatters to the concrete.

Valerie grabs him by the lapels of his ruined suit. She isn't the victim anymore. She is the apex predator.

VALERIE
Consider this a hostile takeover.

She pivots her hips, planting her feet, and violently throws her entire body weight forward. 

She slams Richard’s head directly into the vibrating water heater. 

The metal rings out like a massive gong. 

Richard’s eyes roll back into his skull. He slumps backward onto the wet concrete, a marionette with its strings brutally cut. 

Valerie stands over him. Her chest heaves. She is covered in grease, sweat, and blood. She stares down at the toxic mentor who built her.

The basement door shudders again. 

A low, guttural rumble echoes from the pipes, vibrating through the soles of Valerie's bare feet. 

Valerie turns toward the buckling steel. She wipes a smear of blood from her mouth. 

She isn't running anymore.

scenes.narrative_action: The house senses the bloodshed. The concrete walls groan and literally begin to slide inward on a fleshy track, shrinking the room to crush them. Richard tackles Valerie to the floor, wrapping his hands tightly around her throat. Valerie's vision blurs, her hand flailing blindly across the concrete until her fingers close around a heavy cast-iron wrench. She brings it crashing down onto Richard's skull.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The climax of the physical fight, utilizing the house's collapsing geometry to force a desperate, do-or-die moment.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Blood drips from Valerie's split lip. It hits the porous concrete floor. 

A beat. The blood doesn't pool. It absorbs. Sucked into the foundation like rain into dry earth.

Deep within the walls, a low, seismic GROAN vibrates through the soles of their shoes. 

Valerie backs away, chest heaving. She glances at the heavy steel door. Jammed shut. 

RICHARD stands ten feet away. His bespoke Tom Ford suit is torn, splattered in crimson, yet he casually adjusts his French cuffs. He watches the blood vanish into the floor with mild, academic interest.

A wet, fibrous TEARING echoes through the cramped space. 

The concrete walls shudder. The sharp, ninety-degree corners of the room begin to split. 

Beneath the cracking plaster, the house's true architecture reveals itself: a glistening, fleshy track of sinew and muscle. It pulses with a dark, steady heartbeat. 

The walls slide INWARD. 

Concrete grinds against concrete. The ceiling pipes buckle, hissing steam. The room is actively shrinking, becoming a digestive tract.

VALERIE
(hoarse)
It's liquidating the asset, Richard. We're both collateral. 

Richard doesn't look at the closing walls. His dead, unblinking eyes lock onto Valerie. 

RICHARD
A common misconception, Valerie. The market doesn't destroy indiscriminately. It merely consolidates power.

He steps forward. The room is now a third of its original size. The fleshy tracks squelch under the pressure of the moving walls. 

RICHARD
Only the apex predator survives a correction.

Richard LUNGES. 

He hits Valerie with the force of a freight train. They crash hard onto the vibrating concrete slab. 

Valerie scrambles, kicking wildly, but Richard is heavier. Relentless. He mounts her, pinning her hips. 

His large, manicured hands snap out. They lock around her throat. 

Valerie thrashes. A feral, panicked animal. Her nails dig into the back of his hands, gouging deep crescent moons into his skin. 

Richard doesn't flinch. He leans his weight into the choke. 

RICHARD
(calm, measured)
You always lacked the killer instinct. Too much emotional overhead. 

The walls grind closer. A steel shelf buckles, raining rusted bolts and tools onto the floor around them. 

The room is now a tomb. The fleshy tracks hiss, secreting a thick, acidic bile that eats into the drywall. 

Valerie's mouth opens in a silent scream. Her legs kick, boots slamming against the encroaching left wall. There's nowhere left to move. 

RICHARD
Shhh. Let the market forces do their work. 

Valerie's vision fractures. The edges of the room bleed into a fuzzy, static gray. 

Her lungs burn. The corporate buzzwords, the desperate pitches, the armor of her wealth--it all burns away, leaving only raw, naked survival. 

She stops clawing at his hands. 

Her right arm drops to the floor. She flails blindly across the cold, trembling concrete. 

Fingers scraping through dust. Through slick patches of house-bile. 

Richard's face looms above her, a blurry, patronizing mask of calm. 

RICHARD
(whispering)
Hostile takeover, Valerie. 

Valerie's fingertips brush against cold, heavy metal. 

A CAST-IRON WRENCH. Dropped from the buckled shelf. 

Her fingers curl around the thick, rusted handle. The weight of it grounds her. 

Valerie's eyes snap wide open. The feral desperation crystallizes into pure, violent focus. 

She swings the wrench upward in a vicious, blinding arc. 

CRACK. 

The heavy iron connects dead-center with Richard's temple. 

The sickening sound of bone giving way. 

Richard's eyes roll back. His patronizing smile goes slack. The grip on her throat instantly releases. 

He collapses sideways, his impeccable suit hitting the fleshy, pulsing floor like a sack of dead weight. 

Valerie rolls over, gasping violently for air. She clutches her bruised throat, coughing up dust and spit as the walls continue to grind inward.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie scrambles away from the dazed Richard. She spots the massive, industrial gas main protruding from the shrinking wall. Swinging the wrench with both hands, she smashes the main valve. A deafening hiss erupts as volatile fumes flood the cramped space. The fleshy walls burst through the concrete, jagged teeth lunging directly toward them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The sacrificial play. Valerie weaponizes the house's own infrastructure to destroy it, accepting the likelihood of her own death.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

The room is a concrete throat. The walls pulse, literally encroaching inward. Dust falls from the ceiling like dirty snow. Copper pipes groan under immense, unnatural pressure.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) scrambles backward over a tangle of PVC. Her immaculate designer suit is ruined, streaked with drywall and something that looks dangerously like gastric acid. 

She clutches a heavy steel PIPE WRENCH in both hands. Her chest heaves. Feral, panicked desperation cracks through her usually flawless veneer.

A few feet away, RICHARD STERLING (60s) pushes himself up from the floor. His bespoke Tom Ford jacket is torn at the shoulder. 

He brushes the dust off his lapel with agonizing composure. A nasty gash on his forehead bleeds freely into his eye, but he doesn't blink.

RICHARD
A minor market correction, Valerie. Nothing we can't leverage.

VALERIE
The asset is liquidating us, Richard. We are the capital now.

The wall behind Richard bulges. The cinderblocks crack. A low, guttural stomach rumble vibrates through the floorboards, shaking the dust.

RICHARD
Apex predators do not panic at the sight of a little blood. This property is merely establishing its boundaries. It is testing our margins.

Valerie’s eyes dart around the shrinking room. 

She spots it. Protruding from the far wall, flanked by yellow warning labels: a MASSIVE INDUSTRIAL GAS MAIN. The iron valve wheel is thick, rusted into place.

VALERIE
There is no value add here. It’s a sunk cost.

RICHARD
We negotiate. We feed it the Councilwoman. We feed it Toby. The natural order requires a bottom tier to sustain the top.

Valerie scrambles toward the gas main, her heels snapping off as she kicks them away to gain traction. She raises the heavy wrench.

VALERIE
Toby is off the market. 

RICHARD
(calmly)
There is no 'off the market.' Put the tool down. You lack the killer instinct for this. You always have.

Valerie hesitates. The wrench hovers in the air.

The house groans. A thick, viscous drool drips from a newly formed fissure in the ceiling. It hits the concrete between them and sizzles, eating through the floor.

RICHARD
You are a facilitator, Valerie. Not an owner. Step away from the infrastructure.

Valerie looks at the wrench, then at Richard. Her grip tightens. Her breathing slows.

VALERIE
I’m restructuring the portfolio.

She swings the wrench with everything she has. 

CLANG. 

The cast-iron valve splinters, but doesn't break. The vibration shoots up Valerie's arms, nearly dropping her to her knees.

RICHARD
(sighs)
An emotional response to a logistical problem. Disappointing.

Valerie swings again. Harder. A primal grunt tears from her throat, shedding the last ounce of her corporate polish.

CRACK. 

The valve shears off entirely.

A DEAFENING HISS. 

Invisible, volatile fumes flood the cramped space instantly. The pressure in the pipe is immense. The hissing gas whips Valerie's hair around her face. She coughs, tasting the bitter chemical additives. 

The house HATES this. 

The concrete walls shatter inward. Thick, muscular folds of dark red flesh burst through the cinderblocks, spraying mortar like shrapnel.

Rows of jagged, translucent teeth--like a deep-sea anglerfish--erupt from the plaster. The entire room is now a maw, lunging directly toward them.

Richard stands perfectly still. The wind from the hissing gas flutters his tie. He stares at the encroaching teeth, unblinking.

RICHARD
Fascinating. A total market collapse.

Valerie backs against the doorframe. The gas is so thick it ripples the air. 

She pulls a silver ZIPPO LIGHTER from her pocket. Her thumb rests on the flint. 

VALERIE
Consider the market disrupted.

The fleshy walls roar, a sound like twisting steel and tearing meat. The teeth snap inches from Richard's face. 

Valerie stares him dead in the eye, her thumb tightening on the wheel.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pulls a silver lighter from her gown. She sparks the flint and tosses the flame directly into the hissing gas geyser. Without looking back, she dives headfirst into the mansion's titanium-lined panic room. She grabs the heavy vault door and heaves it shut just as the entity's massive maw snaps down on a screaming Richard.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The explosive resolution of the climax, eliminating the antagonist and the monster in one decisive action.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

A deafening, pressurized HISS. 

Raw natural gas violently vents from a sheared iron main. The air shimmers, thick and heavy with the stench of rotten eggs.

VALERIE backs through the cavernous gloom. Her haute-couture gala gown is shredded, stained with soot and god-knows-what. She keeps her eyes locked on the gleaming, titanium-lined PANIC ROOM embedded in the far wall.

Footsteps crunch on broken concrete. 

RICHARD steps out of the shadows. His bespoke Tom Ford suit is dusted with plaster, but his posture remains impeccably straight. He casually brushes a flake of drywall from his lapel.

RICHARD
This is a gross overreaction, Valerie. A temporary market fluctuation.

The mechanical room GROANS. The boiler pipes overhead flex and bulge like gorged arteries.

RICHARD
You burn down the asset, nobody profits. You're letting emotion dictate your portfolio. It's exactly what my father warned me about people of your... pedigree.

Valerie reaches into the folds of her ruined gown. Her hand trembles, knuckles white. She pulls out a heavy silver lighter.

RICHARD
Put that away. We restructure. We feed it the catering staff. We adapt to the natural order. 

Valerie stares at the hissing gas geyser. She looks back at Richard. The feral panic in her eyes hardens into cold, absolute certainty. 

VALERIE
The market's crashed, Richard. 

She flips the lighter open. 

VALERIE
Consider this a hard pivot.

Her thumb rips across the flint. A tiny, perfect flame erupts.

Richard stops. For the first time, the genial fatherly facade cracks. His dead eyes widen, finally registering the shimmering waves of gas pooling between them.

Valerie tosses the lighter. A silver arc through the toxic air. 

Without waiting for it to land, she lunges. She dives headfirst across the threshold of the panic room.

Behind her, the ceiling TEARS OPEN. 

The architecture ceases to be structural. Cinderblocks, copper wiring, and HVAC ducts violently splinter and peel back. The ceiling forms a colossal, jagged MAW. The house's true throat.

Richard looks up. The apex predator, suddenly realizing he is at the bottom of the food chain.

His measured composure shatters into a raw, guttural SCREAM. 

Valerie hits the titanium floor. She scrambles to her knees, grabbing the massive steel wheel of the vault door. 

The lighter hits the gas stream.

The architectural maw SNAPS down. Rebar teeth crush through bone and bespoke wool with a sickening crunch.

Valerie heaves the vault door shut just as the jaws close over him. 

The deadbolts SLAM into place. 

Through the tiny, reinforced quartz window, a blinding, white-hot wall of FIRE instantly vaporizes the room.

scenes.narrative_action: A catastrophic, earth-shattering explosion rips through the subterranean levels. The blast wave obliterates the fleshy labyrinth and blows the roof of the modernist mansion clean off the foundation. A spectacular firestorm incinerates the property, sending burning debris, shattered glass, and pulverized drywall raining down across the pristine hills.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Visual spectacle of the monster's destruction, permanently altering the landscape and ending the threat.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. HOLLYWOOD HILLS - CONTINUOUS

The pristine, quiet night of the canyons. The glowing infinity pool of the modernist mansion sits completely still.

Then-- the earth HICCUPS.

The driveway buckles, asphalt snapping like brittle bone. The water in the infinity pool instantly boils, shooting upward as a cloud of hissing steam.

A blinding flash of blue-white light erupts from deep within the subterranean foundation.

THE DETONATION.

It is earth-shattering. A tectonic shockwave rips upward through the bedrock.

The fleshy, pulsating labyrinth beneath the house is violently excavated. Thick, muscular roots and organic rot are blasted into the open air, instantly incinerated into a fine black ash.

The blast wave hits the surface. The mansion's floor-to-ceiling structural glass walls bulge outward. For a fraction of a second, the house resembles an over-inflated lung.

THEN IT SHATTERS.

Millions of razor-sharp shards explode outward into the canyon, glittering violently in the firelight.

The cantilevered roof--tons of steel and imported mahogany--is violently severed from its foundation. It launches into the night sky like a massive, spinning blade.

It hangs suspended against the moonlight for a breathless moment. Then, the sheer force of the updraft snaps the roof entirely in half.

A towering FIRESTORM breaches the surface. A geyser of pure, cleansing orange flame roars a hundred feet into the air. The beast's final, deafening death rattle.

The surrounding imported palm trees instantly catch fire, becoming towering torches against the dark. The manicured hillside is scorched black, the earth permanently scarred.

Gravity reclaims the wreckage.

A spectacular rain of fire falls across the Hollywood Hills.

Chunks of burning drywall. Twisted, glowing steel rebar. The charred, unrecognizable remnants of extreme luxury plummet back to earth, smashing into the winding canyon roads below.

Amidst the fiery downpour, a single, flawless diamond drops from the sky.

It hits the asphalt with a sharp clink.

It rolls through the falling ash, coming to a dead stop against a burning chunk of debris.

The flames roar on. The property is gone. The threat is permanently erased.

scenes.narrative_action: Hours later. Smoke drifts over a smoldering, blackened crater. The heavy vault door of the panic room groans open. Valerie emerges, covered in soot, her crimson gown reduced to rags. She climbs out of the wreckage, taking a deep, ragged breath of the cool morning air. The suffocating weight of the house and her ambition is gone.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The aftermath and rebirth. Valerie emerges from the underworld cleansed of her sins.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MANSION CRATER - DAY

A deafening silence. 

Where the pinnacle of luxury real estate stood hours ago, there is only a massive, smoldering void. A blackened crater carved into the Hollywood Hills.

Morning light cuts through the thick, drifting haze. Embers hiss in the damp soil.

Twisted steel beams point toward the sky like the ribs of a rotting carcass. A charred 'FOR SALE' sign lies melted into a puddle of toxic plastic.

Deep in the epicenter of the blast zone, a pile of concrete rubble SHIFTS.

Dust cascades down the incline. 

A heavy, metallic GROAN echoes from the bowels of the earth.

Beneath a slab of shattered marble, the titanium vault door of the panic room pushes upward. It strains against tons of debris. 

The door slips. Slams back down. 

Silence.

Then, a furious, guttural SCREAM from beneath the earth. 

The door HEAVES upward again. This time, the marble slab rolls away, crashing heavily down the debris pile.

The heavy vault door groans open.

VALERIE VANCE pulls herself out of the blackness.

She is unrecognizable. The razor-sharp shark is gone. Her bespoke crimson gown is a scorched, asymmetrical rag. Her skin is coated in a thick, gray paste of ash and dried blood.

She collapses onto the warm rubble. Chest heaving.

She doesn't check her phone. She doesn't smooth her hair. 

She just stares at the empty sky.

Slowly, her fingers dig into the dirt. She forces herself to her feet. 

The ascent begins.

The crater walls are steep, lined with the pulverized remnants of extreme wealth. Valerie climbs. 

Her bare feet find purchase on the splintered keys of a grand piano. 

She grips a twisted piece of an imported chandelier to pull herself up. The jagged brass slices her palm. She doesn't flinch. 

Halfway up, her foot slips. She slides down a few feet, dislodging a cascade of debris.

Among the falling rubble, something tumbles out. It lands perfectly in the dirt, inches from her bleeding knees.

A diamond. 

Massive. Flawless. Pristine and completely free of blood.

It catches the morning sun, throwing fractured rainbows across Valerie's soot-stained face.

Valerie stops. Her breathing slows.

She reaches out with a trembling, ash-covered hand. 

She picks it up. The stone is heavy. Cold. It represents millions. Total financial security. The absolute top of the food chain.

She stares into the facets. Her shattered reflection stares back.

TOBY (O.S.)
Valerie?

Valerie looks up.

Standing at the jagged rim of the crater, silhouetted against the dawn, is TOBY ARIS. 

He looks exhausted, his suit rumpled. He clutches a thermal coffee cup in one hand and a leather-bound planner in the other, gripping them like lifelines.

Valerie looks back at the diamond in her palm.

Without a word, she turns her hand over. 

The diamond drops. 

It tumbles down the incline, disappearing forever into a deep, smoldering fissure in the earth.

Valerie resumes her climb. Faster now. Lighter.

She crests the rim. Toby immediately drops his coffee and extends a hand, pulling her over the edge.

They stand together on the scorched grass. 

Toby looks her up and down. The feral, soot-covered survivor. He nervously adjusts his glasses, unable to maintain eye contact with the wreckage.

TOBY
The police... they're setting up a perimeter down the hill. Fire trucks are behind them. They're going to have a lot of questions.

Valerie doesn't pivot. Doesn't deflect. 

VALERIE
I know.

Toby clears his throat, frantically brushing a speck of ash off his lapel.

TOBY
I brought your planner. In case you needed to... to look at the market comps. Or reschedule the morning showings.

Valerie looks at the leather-bound planner in his trembling hands. The old bible of her ambition. 

VALERIE
Throw it in.

Toby blinks.

TOBY
It has the escrow codes. And the offshore routing numbers. 

VALERIE
Toby. Throw it in.

Toby looks at her. Really looks at her. The manic, desperate energy that defined her for years is entirely absent. 

He looks down at the smoking abyss. 

He tosses the planner. 

They watch it flutter down into the belly of the beast, swallowed by the smoke.

Valerie turns her face toward the horizon. The cool morning breeze washes over her, lifting the matted hair from her eyes.

Sirens wail in the distance, echoing up the canyon.

Valerie takes a deep, ragged breath of the clean air. 

The suffocating weight is gone.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie spots a glint in the gray ashes. She reaches down and picks up a flawless, massive diamond. She rolls the cold stone between her fingers, staring at the promise of instant wealth. With a quiet exhale, she winds up and pitches the diamond deep into the smoldering ruins. She turns her back on the crater and walks up the hillside toward Toby, who waits for her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The final thematic test. Valerie proves her transformation is permanent by voluntarily rejecting the wealth that started the nightmare.
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. MANSION CRATER - DAY

Smoke rises from a jagged, black scar in the earth. The modernist mansion is gone. In its place lies a smoldering pit of twisted rebar, shattered glass, and pulverized concrete. 

Silence. Just the low, rhythmic hiss of ruptured gas lines burning out.

VALERIE VANCE stands at the lip of the abyss. Her bespoke designer suit is scorched, torn, and soaked in ash. Her rigid, corporate posture is completely gone. She looks like a soldier surviving a siege.

Down in the gray soot, a sharp, unnatural refraction of sunlight catches her eye. 

Muscle memory twitches. Valerie slides down the loose, ashen embankment. Her ruined heels sink deep into the scorched dirt.

She drops to her knees. Reaches into the still-warm ash. 

Her trembling fingers pull it free. 

A flawless, massive diamond. The size of a quail egg. Pristine. Blood-free. 

It catches the sun, throwing fractured rainbows across Valerie's soot-stained face. 

Up on the ridge, TOBY ARIS appears. His clothes are rumpled, his glasses cracked down the left lens. He clutches a singed clipboard against his chest like a shield. 

He looks down into the crater.

TOBY
The, uh, the fire department is staging at the bottom of the hill. They're asking about survivors. I didn't... I didn't know what to tell them about Mr. Sterling.

Valerie doesn't look up. She rolls the cold stone between her thumb and forefinger. The ultimate commission. 

VALERIE
Tell them the market corrected itself.

TOBY
Right. Yeah. Market correction. I can, uh, I can phrase it like that. Or maybe just a structural collapse. Unforeseen subsidence.

Toby shifts his weight, kicking a loose pebble into the pit. He sees what she's holding. The sharp glint of the diamond hits his eye. He stops shuffling.

TOBY
Valerie.

She stares into the depth of the stone. It is millions of dollars. It is absolute armor against the world. It is the end of eviction notices and secret debt. 

Her thumb traces the razor-sharp facets. Her breathing shallows. 

Toby takes a half-step down the embankment. He adjusts his cracked glasses.

TOBY
We have to go. Before they come up here.

Valerie closes her fist around the diamond. The sharp edges bite into her palm. She squeezes until her knuckles turn white, grounding herself in the physical pain.

VALERIE
You still want to build those affordable units?

Toby blinks, totally thrown by the pivot. He nervously brushes a layer of ash off his clipboard.

TOBY
I... I don't think I have the capital for that right now. Or a job, technically. Sorry.

VALERIE
We'll pivot.

She opens her hand. Looks at the diamond one last time. 

A quiet exhale. 

Valerie winds up. She pitches the stone hard, deep into the smoldering center of the ruins. 

It vanishes into the black ash. Swallowed by the earth. 

Valerie doesn't watch it land. She turns her back on the pit and climbs up the embankment. Her ruined heels slip, but she drives her hands into the dirt and pulls herself up.

She reaches the top, standing eye-level with Toby. 

She looks at his singed clipboard. Plucks it out of his hands. Tosses it over her shoulder into the crater.

VALERIE
Let's go.

TOBY
Where are we going? We don't have a car. Or a brokerage.

VALERIE
We're walking.

Valerie strides past him, heading down the hillside. Her pace is steady. Unburdened.

Toby watches her for a second. He adjusts his glasses again, a faint, relieved smile breaking through his anxiety. 

TOBY
Walking. Yeah. Okay. I can do walking.

He hurries to catch up. They descend the hill together, leaving the smoke and the ashes behind them.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie hoists the last weeping caterer up toward a rusted ventilation shaft. Toby, already inside the duct, grabs the caterer's arms and pulls them to safety. Valerie turns to climb up next, but a heavy iron pipe swings out from the shadows, brutally smashing into her ribs. She collapses to the damp concrete, gasping. Richard Sterling steps into the flickering fluorescent light, his expensive tuxedo torn and bloodied, his eyes wide with manic greed.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the final physical obstacle. The antagonist actively blocks the protagonist's escape to preserve his own wealth.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - NIGHT

Thick, humid heat. The walls sweat a viscous, amber sap. Deep within the concrete, a rhythmic, wet THUD pulses. The house is digesting.

VALERIE VANCE (30s) strains against gravity. Her designer pantsuit is ruined, soaked in sweat and grime. She hoists a weeping teenage CATERER by the waist, shoving him upward toward a rusted ventilation shaft.

Inside the narrow duct, TOBY ARIS (20s) reaches down, his glasses crooked, hands trembling.

TOBY
(straining)
I got him, I got him! Just-- sorry, watch your head!

VALERIE
Stop apologizing and leverage your body weight, Toby! Pull!

With a frantic heave, Toby drags the caterer up into the dark aluminum throat of the duct. 

Valerie slumps against the damp concrete, chest heaving. She wipes a smear of grease from her cheek, eyeing the open shaft.

TOBY
Okay! Give me your hand! We have to move before the foundation shifts again!

Valerie reaches up. Her fingertips brush Toby's.

From the shadows-- a brutal WHOOSH of displaced air.

A heavy IRON PIPE swings out of the darkness. It connects with Valerie's ribs with a sickening CRACK.

Valerie is thrown sideways. She hits the wet floor hard, all the oxygen violently expelled from her lungs. She curls into a fetal position, gasping for air that won't come.

Toby screams her name, his face pressed against the grate.

Footsteps splash through the shallow puddles. Slow. Deliberate.

RICHARD STERLING (50s) steps into the flickering fluorescent light. His bespoke Tom Ford tuxedo is shredded. He is soaked in blood-- none of it his own. 

Yet, he carries the iron pipe casually, resting it over his shoulder like a pitching wedge. His eyes are blown wide, pupils dilated in a state of manic, transcendent greed.

RICHARD
You know, Valerie, I've always said panic is the hallmark of middle management.

Valerie spits a glob of crimson onto the floor. She clutches her side, trying to push herself up. Her arm gives out.

VALERIE
(wheezing)
Richard... the asset is toxic. We need to... pivot. Now.

Richard steps closer. He places the tip of the iron pipe gently against Valerie's collarbone, pinning her to the floor.

RICHARD
Pivot? From the greatest value-add in the history of real estate? You lack vision.

TOBY
(voice cracking)
Mr. Sterling! You're... you're covered in blood! Let her go!

Richard doesn't even look up at the vent. He smoothly adjusts his torn, bloody cufflink with his free hand.

RICHARD
Toby. Still focused on the minor logistics, I see. The mess upstairs is simply... a market correction. We are trimming the fat.

VALERIE
It ate the Mayor, Richard. It's going to eat us.

RICHARD
Apex predators do not get eaten, Valerie. We adapt. We monopolize. Do you have any idea what the yield is on a Councilwoman? 

A euphoric shudder rolls through Richard's shoulders. He looks up at the ceiling. The muffled, horrific sounds of the gala being consumed echo down the pipes.

RICHARD
The diamonds this property will produce by morning... It's pure, untaxed capital. No supply chains. No labor unions. Just raw, biological input converted to flawless equity.

Valerie's feral survival instinct kicks in. She forces a tight, blood-stained smile, looking up at him.

VALERIE
You can't manage it alone. You need a broker to move the stones. I have the offshore contacts. Let me up, and we restructure the equity split.

Richard smiles down at her. A cold, dead-eyed fatherly grin.

RICHARD
A beautiful pitch. Truly. But you see, empathy is a market inefficiency. And right now, you aren't a partner, Valerie.

He lifts the iron pipe, adjusting his grip.

RICHARD
You're just overhead.

Toby frantically kicks at the metal ductwork, the noise echoing sharply.

TOBY
I'm calling the police! I'm calling everyone!

Richard finally tilts his head toward the vent.

RICHARD
And tell them what, Toby? That the natural order has finally asserted itself? Run along. The adults are liquidating assets.

Valerie's eyes dart around the room. She is completely cornered. 

The house groans again, a deep, tectonic rumble that shakes the dust from the ceiling. 

Richard raises the pipe higher, stepping squarely between Valerie and the only way out.

scenes.narrative_action: Richard calmly monologues about the property's unparalleled ROI as he grabs Valerie by her torn crimson gown. He hauls her across the concrete, dragging her toward the buckling basement door where the house's fleshy tendrils are actively beating against the steel frame. He gleefully attempts to shove her toward the door to offer her as a peace offering to the hungry architecture.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Richard's absolute moral bankruptcy and sets the immediate, life-or-death stakes of the physical struggle.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. MANSION MECHANICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Pipes hiss. The air is thick with boiler heat and the metallic tang of copper. 

A heavy STEEL DOOR buckles outward. *THUMP.*

Something wet and massive slams against the other side. The metal groans. Paint chips flake to the concrete floor.

RICHARD STERLING (60s) stands amidst the grime. His bespoke tuxedo is immaculate. Not a single thread out of place. 

At his feet: VALERIE VANCE (30s). Her once-stunning crimson gown is shredded, stained dark with soot and blood. She scrambles backward on her hands and knees, breath rattling in her chest.

Richard steps forward. Smooth. Leisurely. He grips the torn collar of her silk gown.

RICHARD
You have to admire the efficiency, Valerie.

He hauls her up. Valerie thrashes, her nails digging into his manicured hands. He doesn't flinch. He begins to DRAG her across the rough concrete toward the buckling door.

VALERIE
(gasping)
Richard, stop-- wait. The comps. We haven't saturated the overseas market. There are buyers in Dubai who will pay triple--

RICHARD
Stop pitching, my dear. The market has already spoken.

*THUMP.* The steel door bows deeper. The hinges shriek.

A thick, translucent TENDRIL -- resembling a raw, pulsing vein -- slithers through a crack in the doorframe. It whips the air, tasting the boiler heat.

VALERIE
I brought you this listing! I am your top producer!

RICHARD
You were a highly leveraged asset. But every asset has a lifecycle.

Valerie's hand flails over the concrete as she is dragged. Her fingers close around a heavy, rusted valve wheel. She swings it backward, aiming for his kneecap.

Richard casually sidesteps. He brings his Italian leather heel down on her wrist. 

Valerie cries out. The valve wheel clatters away into the dark. Richard doesn't even break his stride. He drags her another five feet. The concrete shreds the remaining tulle of her dress.

RICHARD
Look at this property. Truly look at it. No property taxes. No maintenance fees. It sustains itself. A perfect, closed-loop economy. The ROI is unprecedented.

VALERIE
It's going to eat you too, you psychotic fuck!

RICHARD
Apex predators do not consume each other. They recognize their own. We merely provide the... feed.

They are ten feet from the door. 

The tendril slaps the concrete, leaving a trail of acidic slime. It senses the vibration of their approach. It points directly at Valerie.

VALERIE
(feral panic)
I can pivot! Richard, listen to me! I can source more clients. Tech billionaires. Hedge fund managers. I can feed it an entire board of directors by Tuesday!

RICHARD
(smiling gently)
I appreciate the initiative. But the architecture is hungry tonight.

He yanks her upward, spinning her around. He locks his forearm across her throat, pinning her back against his chest. 

They are inches from the doorframe. 

Three more tendrils squeeze through the widening gap. They smell the fresh blood on her gown. They reach out, quivering with blind excitement.

RICHARD
